pairs.e: Capitalism was born from the industrial revolution.

pairs.m: मुदा डेरीडा एकरा उघार करैत छथि, संरचनावाद अपन विश्लेषण लेल एकटा ठोस आधार ताकैत अछि, व्यवस्थाक बाहर एकटा केन्द्र जइसँ ओ एकर वैज्ञानिक विश्लेषण कऽ सकय, मुदा से मात्र दार्शनिकक आभास मात्र अछि।
pairs.e: Now, in the post-industrial society, the importance of information and communication has increased over production.

pairs.m: माने कोनो लोकगाथाक कोनो स्थायी बा निर्धारित संरचना केना भऽ सकैए।
pairs.e: History may not have ended, but traditional history has ended. State, class, nation, party, society, family, morality, marriage—all are being redefined. Many institutions, due to the consequences of constant change, have become fragmented and context-less.

pairs.m: से लोककथा बा लोक गाथाक संरचनाक अध्ययन करबा लेल अहाँकेँ ओ विचार बा ओ केन्द्र, जकर आधारपर अहाँ एकर विश्लेषण करऽ चाहैत छी, निर्धारित करऽ पड़त।
pairs.e: Michel Foucault says—knowledge and truth are created. Deleuze and Guattari say we desire through the other because we are desire machines.

pairs.m: डेरीडा कहै छथि जे कोनो संरचना लेल ओकर संकल्पना-विचार आवश्यक अछि, फेर टुकड़ी-टुकड़ी जोड़ि कऽ अहाँ ओकरा बनायब।
pairs.e: Mikhail Bakhtin takes language as a form of social action.

pairs.m: मुदा बिनु अन्त सोचने संरचना सम्भवे नै अछि।
pairs.e: Russian formalist thinkers consider literature merely a specific use of language.

pairs.m: आ से साहित्यमे सेहो अछि।
pairs.e: Jean-François Lyotard says—the authenticity of truth and history is only virtual.

pairs.m: यावत अहाँ कोनो रचना लेल एकटा स्वयंसिद्ध अर्थ नै ताकि लैत छी ओकर संरचना अहाँ नै ताकि सकै छी, कारण अर्थ माने अन्त ओकर संरचनाकें निर्धारित करैत अछि।
pairs.e: Baudrillard says—advertising and television have erased the distinction between truth and the virtual.

pairs.m: से संरचनावादीकेँ अर्थ पहिनहियेसँ पता रहै छै आ तखने ओ ओकर विभिन्न अंग आ तकर आपसी सम्बन्धकेँ विश्लेषित कऽ सकैए।
pairs.e: Both are key postmodernist thinkers.

pairs.m: आ यएह विश्लेषणसँ पहिले ज्ञात स्वयंसिद्ध अर्थ अछि डेरीडाक केन्द्र।
pairs.e: Lacan's specialty is that he applied linguistics to Freud's method.

pairs.m: आ डेरीडा कहै छथि जे यएह निर्धारित करैत अछि जे पाठक संरचना केना बनत, कोन अंगकेँ लेल जायत आ कोन अंगकेँ छोड़ल जायत।
pairs.e: He says the structure of the unconscious is like a language.

pairs.m: से जखन हम साहित्यक संरचनाक विश्लेषण करैत छी तँ ओकर अर्थ आ ओकर प्रभावक गप करैत छी तँ हम ओइ संरचनासँ ओइ तत्त्व सभकेँ चिन्हबाक, फराक करबाक प्रयास करैत छी जे ओइ प्रभाव लेल उत्तरदायी अछि, से ओइ सम्भावित पैटर्नकेँ हम छोड़ि दैत छी जे ओ प्रभाव नै आनत।
pairs.e: When a child learns language, a signifier is taught for a signified. Desire, error, and the Other are three facts Lacan presents well.

pairs.m: से ई केन्द्र आरम्भिक स्थल अछि संरचनाकेँ बुझबाक हेतु।
pairs.e: Desire is both need and demand, but it cannot be fragmented into these two forms.

pairs.m: मुदा ई विश्लेषणकेँ सीमित सेहो करैत अछि।
pairs.e: Error and emptiness appear in the description of the Other.

pairs.m: कारण केन्द्र अपनाकेँ स्थिर रखबाक लेल स्तरीकरणक निर्माण करैत अछि, आ ओकरा पर नियंत्रण करैत अछि।
pairs.e: The subject is a fleeting effect of meaning, and it occurs with the Other when it becomes virtual and becomes the cause of error, from which desire arises.

pairs.m: आ ई अर्थक पूर्व ज्ञान पाठकक पूर्व इतिहास आ समकालीन आलोचना सिद्धान्त आ विचारधारापर निर्भर सेहो करैत अछि, ईहो सभ तँ संस्कृतिक अंग अछि तखन केना एकरा सभकेँ संरचनासँ दूर राखल जाइए जे एकरा सभकेँ सीमित करैए मुदा अपने एकरा सभसँ सीमित नै होइए?
pairs.e: There are three post-colonial thinkers: Homi K. Bhabha (close to Foucault and Lacan), Gayatri Chakravorty Spivak (close to Foucault and Derrida), and Edward Said (close to Foucault), who explain the work done and theorizing regarding the cunning, weakness, etc., of the former colonizers.

pairs.m: से अपन विश्लेषणक आधार कोनो स्थायी केन्द्रकेँ बनायब एकटा नुस्खा देब सन अछि आ ई प्रतिक्रियावादी बा यथास्थितिवादी स्थिति अछि।
pairs.e: Raymond Williams's study of culture connects literature with economic conditions.

pairs.m: आ ऐ सँ मानक आ गएर-तुलनात्मक स्वतंत्र अर्थ निकालबाक मात्र इच्छा सिद्ध होइए।
pairs.e: New Historicism compares the linguistics of history and the historicity of linguistics.

pairs.m: से ई भ्रम जे हम संरचना ताकि रहल छी भ्रमे अछि, वास्तवमे अहाँ पाठ्य सामग्रीसँ संरचनाक निर्माण कऽ रहल छी।
pairs.e: Elaine Showalter identifies the mental, biological, and linguistic characteristics of women's writing.

pairs.m: से डेरीडाक उत्तर संरचनावादमे सेहो केन्द्रक बिना प्रारम्भ नै भऽ सकैत अछि मुदा ओ ऐ सिद्ध नै भेल स्वयंसिद्धक विखण्डनात्मक विश्लेषणक आधारपर ओइ केन्द्रकेँ दोसर केन्द्र द्वारा स्थानापन्न करैत अछि मुदा ई नव केन्द्र सेहो स्थायी नै रहत।
pairs.e: Simone de Beauvoir considers class and caste (which later feminist theory opposed) as obstacles in a woman's commitment to women.

pairs.m: तहिना सौसियोर (Saussure) अपन प्रतीक सिद्धान्तमे प्रतीकक विश्लेषण करैत छथि जे कोनो शब्द जेना बिलाड़ि- बिलाड़ि अछि कारण ओ किछु आन नै अछि।
pairs.e: Virginia Woolf considers economic independence and privacy necessary for a woman writer.

pairs.m: से शब्द अछि प्रतीक चिन्ह आ जकरा ओ दर्शाबैत अछि से अछि बौस्तु/ प्रतीक।
pairs.e: Her ideas were not considered revolutionary.

pairs.m: मुदा डेरीडा कहै छथि जे अहू लेल पहिने एकटा संकल्पना आनऽ पड़ैत अछि।
pairs.e: Mary Wollstonecraft emphasized incorporating revolution and education based on justice in women's education.

pairs.m: आ जँ अहाँ प्रतीक चिन्हकेँ निरपेक्ष बना देब जे ओकर कोनो प्रतीकसँ सम्बन्ध नै छै, जे स्वयंमे एकटा स्वतंत्र संकल्पना अछि आ कोनो प्रतीक/ बौस्तुसँ ओकर कोनो प्रत्यक्ष सम्बन्ध नै छै, मुदा तखन ई संकल्पना सभ प्रतीक चिन्हकेँ पार कऽ जायत आ किछुओ सूचित नै करत।
pairs.e: Neo-criticism: Eliot opposed the primacy of emotion in poetry and insisted on making it impersonal.

pairs.m: आ जँ हम प्रतीक आ प्रतीक चिन्हकेँ एक्के मानी तँ प्रतीक चिन्हक जड़ियेपर चोट पहुँचत।
pairs.e: Wimsatt and Wordsworth said criticism should not be based on the poet's intention or historical study.

pairs.m: से सौसियोरक सिद्धान्त तर्काधारितक विपक्षमे अछि जखन ओ कहैत अछि जे प्रतीक चिन्हमे अहाँ प्रतीकक कोनो लक्षण नै देखू।
pairs.e: Nor should it be based on the emotional effect on the reader, as that is relative.

pairs.m: मुदा जखन ओ कहैत छथि जे प्रतीक चिन्ह प्रतीककेँ लक्षित करबा लेल मात्र प्रयुक्त होइत अछि आ तेँ ओकर अधीन अछि ओ तर्क आधारित सिद्धान्तक पक्षमे बुझाइत छथि।
pairs.e: It should be based on actual linguistics.

pairs.m: से डेरीडा कहैत छथि जे प्रतीक आ प्रतीक चिन्हक ई स्पष्ट भेद मान्य नै अछि, आ प्रतीककेँ प्रतीक चिन्हक ऊपर देल वरीयता सेहो उनटेबाक खगता अछि।
pairs.e: English criticism can be traced back to Philip Sidney—he saw poetry in terms of beauty, meaning, and human interest.

pairs.m: प्रतीक चिन्हमे अर्थ पहिनहियेसँ विद्यमान नै रहै छै।
pairs.e: John Dryden—considered moral discourse in ancient literature and its benefits and harms.

pairs.m: आ पूर्ण अर्थ कोनो एकटा प्रतीक चिन्हमे नै भेटत।
pairs.e: Samuel Johnson wrote on the tragic and comic elements in Shakespeare's plays.

pairs.m: से पाठकेँ अहाँकेँ घोर-मट्ठा करऽ पड़त, आ ई अनन्त खोज दिस अहाँकेँ धकेलत।
pairs.e: Rousseau's romanticism does not doubt that man is good (classical critics doubt this, but neo-classical critics say human nature is corrupt, but institutions can make it good), but he also says that institutions are corrupt and only help corrupt people.

pairs.m: से ई प्रतीक चिन्ह दोसर प्रतीक चिन्हसँ अपन अन्तरक आधारपर प्रतीक निर्धारण करैत अछि, जतेक बेशी अन्तर ततेक लग अहाँ प्रतीकसँ होइ छी।
pairs.e: Modern situationalism—no question mark on the situation of literature—but structuralism attacked it, and after that, the author lost the right to analyze the text he himself wrote.

pairs.m: मुदा ई कहियो नै हएत जे अहाँ सभटा अन्तर ताकि सकब।
pairs.e: Post-structuralism said literature is a thing beyond what structuralism understands.

pairs.m: मुदा प्रतीक चिन्हमे बारम्बारता हेबाक चाही, तखनो जखन एक प्रतीक चिन्ह भिन्न प्रतीकक निर्धारण करैत अछि।
pairs.e: One type of post-structuralism is postmodernity.

pairs.m: आ ऐ लेल ओइ प्रतीक चिन्हक लिखित इतिहास जानब आवश्यक।
pairs.e: Post-structuralism said that in literature, structure operates between culture and theory, where some feelings and thoughts are excluded, which oppose continuity.

pairs.m: से प्रतीक चिन्ह विभिन्न अर्थ आ कखनो काल उल्टा अर्थ सेहो देत।
pairs.e: Deconstruction and postmodernity came after post-structuralism.

pairs.m: प्लेटो- प्लेटो कहै छथि जे कोनो कला नीक नै भऽ सकैए किए तँ ई सभटा असत्य आ अवास्तविक अछि।
pairs.e: Post-colonialism does not accept new forms of colonialism and considers theories of disorder as reasons for not achieving appropriate results for failed objectives.

pairs.m: प्लेटोक ई विचार स्पार्टासँ एथेंसक सैन्य संगठनक न्यूनताकेँ देखैत देल विचारक रूपमे सेहो देखल जएबाक चाही।
pairs.e: Structuralism attacked the oppressive Western system and society, and this strengthened Marxism (Althusser).

pairs.m: डेरीडा लिखै छथि जे प्लेटोसँ सौसियोर (Saussure) आ लेवी स्ट्रॉस धरि सभ लिखलाहासँ ऊपर बजलाहाकेँ राखै छथि, कारण लिखब एकटा माध्यम अछि, असल चीज तँ वाणी अछि।
pairs.e: After modernism-situationalism, neo-criticism, structuralism, and post-structuralism came deconstruction and postmodernism, which was called late capitalism (Fredric Jameson).

pairs.m: सौसियोर लिखित रूपमे उच्चारण त्रुटिपर ध्यान दियाबैत छथि।
pairs.e: After the 1970s, the term 'modern' took the form of a theory, and then the term 'post-modern' became a term whose perspective was not worldly important.

pairs.m: मुदा डेरीडा कहै छथि जे ओ सभ विशेषता जे वाणीमे छै से लेखनमे सेहो छै।
pairs.e: All life and history up to the modern period became unimportant and ended. This theory became post-history, post-development, and post-causality.

pairs.m: आगाँ ओ कहै छथि, प्रतीकक विलुप्ति वाणीमे विचारक प्रत्यक्ष रहबाक भ्रम उत्पन्न करैए।
pairs.e: The importance of truth and interconnectedness ended. In magical realism, magical objects are inserted into a realistic situation.

pairs.m: मुदा जँ बाजल वाणीकेँ हम रेकॉर्ड कऽ कय सुनी तँ ओहो लिखल अक्षर सन प्रतीकक शृंखले अछि, जइमे विभिन्न प्रतीककेँ ओकर एक-दोसराक अन्तर सँ चिन्हल जा सकैए।
pairs.e: The writer uses such experiments to better understand reality.

pairs.m: आ लेखन सेहो सामान्य लेखन आ चित्रसँ बुझा कऽ कएल लेखन, ऐ दू तरहेँ भऽ सकैए।
pairs.e: Post-modernity uses Western bourgeois audio-visual media to call concepts of inequality, injustice, and deprivation merely words that are related to equality, attainment, and justice.

pairs.m: 'अपन अवस्थितिक तत्त्वमीमांसा' एकर सभक पाछाँ अछि।
pairs.e: Poverty, which is not a problem in the West, is a major problem in India and Nepal today.

pairs.m: पाश्चात्य दर्शनक 'अपन अवस्थितिक तत्त्वमीमांसा'मे जे मुख्य अछि से अछि सद्यः अनुभव- मुदा विखण्डनवाद कहैत अछि जे एहेन कोनो अनुभव परा-भाषा स्तरपर नै होइत अछि, कारण ई अनुभव भाषाक माध्यमेसँ चिन्हल जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: Post-modernity is opposed to feminism and Marxism, and feminism and Marxism have opposed it.

pairs.m: फेर ई जे दैवीय चेतनासँ हम परम सत्यकेँ बुझैत छी मुदा विखण्डनवाद कहैत अछि जे ई मात्र सर्जकक सृजन अछि।
pairs.e: For example, Marxism is in favor of historical analysis and has strengthened its theory in the context of structuralism-post-structuralism and postmodernism.

pairs.m: फेर ईहो जे कोनो बौस्तुक पाछाँ सत्य नुकायल अछि, मुदा विखण्डनवाद कहैत अछि जे तेहन कोनो स्वतंत्र अस्तित्व नै होइ छै, सभटा निर्माण आ पुनर्निर्माण व्यवस्था द्वारा होइ छै।
pairs.e: Marxism emphasizes worldly aspects, but it is not close to utilitarianism and Charvaka philosophy, because utilitarianism and Charvaka philosophy only consider physical needs.

pairs.m: से सौसियोरक स्वतः उपस्थिति किछु नै अछि कारण सभटा व्यवस्थाक अन्तर्गत निर्मित अछि।
pairs.e: Feminist perspectives have also opposed the status quo of postmodernism because until it ends, the situation of women will not improve.

pairs.m: डेरीडा कहैत छथि जे तर्क ऐ सभटा दार्शनिक चिन्तनक आधार अछि, से कोनो अन्तिम सत्य आ विश्वात्माक परिकल्पना देल जाइत अछि जे सर्वज्ञानी अछि।
pairs.e: Derrida's deconstruction method brings down a highly placed work/author and elevates the low.

pairs.m: मुदा डेरीडा कहै छथि जे ओ सिद्ध नै भेल केन्द्रकेँ कखनो गॉड, कखनो विचार आ कखनो विश्वात्मा कहल गेल आ ओ अपनासँ नीचाँ विभिन्न स्तरक निर्माण केलक।
pairs.e: Roland Barthes writes that when a work becomes separate from its author and its analysis begins independently, the work becomes important, which he calls the death of the author.

pairs.m: से धर्म गॉडकेँ परम सत्य मानलक आ मनुक्ख आ आन रचनाकेँ ओ अपूर्ण आ विरोधी; आ ई सभटा केन्द्र बनल जे अपना हिसाबे विचार-व्यवस्था बनेबाक दावा केलक।
pairs.e: Post-structuralism destroyed the concept of structuralism being complete and well-structured. Saussure's theory of language—spoken/written, contemporary literature or the linguistics of historical perspective, any important work or person is important, importance is a feeling, language in contemporary times or its historical perspective; but even this was turned upside down by Derrida's deconstruction theory.

pairs.m: मुदा एकरा सभकेँ व्यवस्थासँ ऊपर हेबाक चाही।
pairs.e: The feminist perspective says that all theories are made by men, so they cannot provide a complete explanation.

pairs.m: से गॉडक स्वतंत्र रूपसँ धर्मक बाहर उपस्थिति हेबाक चाही।
pairs.e: Simple humanism arose against all these theories, but it too became a theory.

pairs.m: उत्तर संरचनावादी विखण्डनवाद एहेन कोनो परासत्यक उपस्थितिकेँ आभासी मानैत अछि, आ उत्तर संरचनावादी एकरा भाषाक सिद्धांतक परिणाम मानैत अछि ।
pairs.e: Significant literary creation occurred under it.

pairs.m: से ऐ प्रतीक चिन्ह सभक आपसी खेलमे किछु अर्थ कोनो विचारधाराक हस्तक्षेपसँ उच्च स्थान प्राप्त करैत अछि आ ओकर दोसर अर्थ तकर पाछाँ जेबा लेल धकेल देल जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: Psychoanalysis and the dialectical method, along with Foucault's analysis and Derrida's analysis, remained as enduring paradigms for criticism after being synthesized. Thus, literature can be idealistic, naturalistic, realistic, humanistic, socialistic, or empiricistic-sensual-realistic.

pairs.m: स्वतंत्रता, गणतंत्र, न्याय आदि सन विचारधारा हमरा सभक जिनगीक भाग छी मुदा लागैए जे ओइ सभसँ हमरा सभक जिनगीक बहुत रास अर्थ निकलल मुदा अन्वेषणक उपरान्त ओ सभ दोसर विचारसँ बहार भेल बुझायत।
pairs.e: Or it can be pragmatic, which includes utilitarianism, pragmatism, behaviorism, causation, economism, and consequentialism.

pairs.m: कोनो संकल्पना एहेन नै अछि जइमे दोसर विचारक अवशेष नै भेटय।
pairs.e: This is the modern perspective.

pairs.m: "गैसलाइटिंग"क प्रतिकारमे अबैत अछि स्टोरी साइंस, कोन कथा सुनेलासँ लोक आ समाजपर की असर पड़त, ई ओइ आधारपर पूर्व-विश्लेषण करैत अछि।
pairs.e: According to Julia Kristeva, a son distances himself from his mother, but in a daughter, those rhythms and qualities persist, so even when she distances herself from her mother, she remains close to culture.

pairs.m: आ स्टोरी साइंस सेहो सएह कहैए, जे कथा सुनायत से करत राज, आ जे जत्ते कथा सुनाओत से तत्ते आगाँ बढ़त।
pairs.e: The reason for Julia Kristeva's male-centric and stubborn approach is this.

pairs.m: "जे सुनबैए खिस्सा से राज करैए ऐ संसारपर"- होपी अमेरिकी कबीलाक लोकोक्ति।
pairs.e: In her presentation "Word, Dialogue and Novel", Julia Kristeva proposed the concept of inter-textuality, meaning no text can exist in isolation.

pairs.m: काव्यक भारतीय विचार: मोक्षक लेल कलाक अवधारणा, जेना नटराजक मुद्रा देखू।
pairs.e: She calls the text a compilation.

pairs.m: सृजन आ नाश दुनूक लय देखा पड़त।
pairs.e: The text is a subject of practice and production.

pairs.m: स्थायी भावक गाढ़ भऽ सीझि कऽ रस बनब- आ ऐ सन कतेक रसक सीता आ राम अनुभव केलन्हि (देखू वाल्मीकि रामायण)।
pairs.e: It produces two types of voices: the voice of socio-cultural texts and the voice of literary texts.

pairs.m: कृष्ण भारतीय कर्मवादक शिक्षक छथि तँ संगमे रसिक सेहो।
pairs.e: The text incorporates ideological conflict and tension.

pairs.m: कलाक स्वाद लेल रस सिद्धांतक आवश्यकता भेल आ भरत नाट्यशास्त्र लिखलन्हि।
pairs.e: In Julia Kristeva's longitudinal axis, there is a direct dialogue between the author and the reader.

pairs.m: अभिनवगुप्त आनन्दवर्धनक ध्वन्यालोकपर भाष्य लिखलन्हि।
pairs.e: That is, there is a direct dialogue between the content of the envelope and the address written on the envelope.

pairs.m: भामह ६अम शताब्दी, दण्डी सातम शताब्दी आ रुद्रट ९अम शताब्दी, एकरा आगाँ बढ़ेलन्हि।
pairs.e: But in Julia Kristeva's vertical axis, the "text" dialogues with mainstream literature and also with events that occurred in a short period.

pairs.m: रस सिद्धान्त: भरत:- नाटकक प्रभावसँ रसक उत्पत्ति होइत अछि।
pairs.e: That is, there is a dialogue between the text and its context; one author's text dialogues with another author's text.

pairs.m: नाटक कथी लेल?
pairs.e: When these two axes intersect, a word or text is produced in which at least one other text can be read.

pairs.m: नाटक रसक अभिनय लेल आ संगे रसक उत्पत्ति लेल सेहो।
pairs.e: Julia Kristeva describes the author's authority and duty as preserving speech and as a text separate from the human being.

pairs.m: रस कोना बहराइए?
pairs.e: Julia Kristeva's presentation "Word, Dialogue and Novel" furthers Mikhail Bakhtin's concept.

pairs.m: रस बहराइए कारण (विभाव), परिणाम (अनुभाव) आ संग लागल आन वस्तु (व्यभिचारी)सँ।
pairs.e: Additionally, Julia Kristeva analyzes language as a sign and symbol.

pairs.m: स्थायीभाव गाढ़ भऽ सीझि कऽ रस बनैए, जकर स्वाद हम लऽ सकै छी।
pairs.e: When a child communicates through rhythms as a sign, there is no structure or meaning.

pairs.m: भट्ट लोलट:- स्थायीभाव कारण-परिणाम द्वारा गाढ़ भऽ रस बनैत अछि।
pairs.e: But as he grows, he understands the difference between self and other, starts speaking, and moves out of the courtyard.

pairs.m: अभिनेता-अभिनेत्री अनुसन्धान द्वारा आ कल्पना द्वारा रसक अनुभव करैत छथि।
pairs.e: And then he distances himself from his mother.

pairs.m: लोलट कविकेँ आ संगमे श्रोता-दर्शककेँ महत्व नै दै छथि।
pairs.e: However, he does not immediately move away from the symbolic; rather, he oscillates between the semiotic and the symbolic.

pairs.m: शौनक:- शौनक रसानुभूति लेल दर्शकक प्रदर्शनमे पैसि कऽ रस लेब आवश्यक बुझै छथि, घोड़ाक चित्रकेँ घोड़ा सन बूझि रस लेबा सन।
pairs.e: The semiotic is feminine, musical, poetic, and rhythmic; the symbolic is masculine, rule-based, and structured. The devaluation of women's bodies and their characters in cinema and other media by men stems from the fear of the mother's body threatening their existence.

pairs.m: भट्टनायक कहै छथि जे रसक प्रभाव दर्शकपर होइत अछि।
pairs.e: But a baby child remains close to its mother, so it is more imbued with feminine semiotic qualities—musical, poetic, rhythmic.

pairs.m: कविक भाषाकेँ ओ भिन्न मानैत छथि।
pairs.e: Thus, while it rejects the mother, it also defines itself in relation to her.

pairs.m: रससँ श्रोता-दर्शकक आत्मा, परमात्मासँ मेल करैए।
pairs.e: Cornel supports Jacques Derrida's intervention in feminist ideology through his phallocentric deconstruction theory, and Gayatri Chakravorty Spivak welcomes it.

pairs.m: रसक आनन्द अछि स्वरूपानन्द।
pairs.e: Derrida's focus on the limitations of the phallocentric perspective is a meaningful intervention, and it has made it possible to redefine the components of symbols under a process.

pairs.m: आ ऐसँ होइत अछि आत्म-साक्षात्कार।
pairs.e: Derrida sees the impossibility of simplifying the factors of dialogue; it cannot be simplified. So, women should not be seen as objects of inference and knowledge.

pairs.m: रस सिद्धान्त श्रोता-दर्शक-पाठक पर आधारित अछि।
pairs.e: Feminism should not be seen in relation to masculinity; rather, there is a need to create a separate space for feminism.

pairs.m: ई श्रोता-दर्शक-पाठकपर जोर दैत अछि।
pairs.e: Derrida says a text is roughly a political act that can be defined as a dialogue concerning power relations.

pairs.m: ध्वनि सिद्धान्त: आनन्दवर्धन ध्वन्यालोक- साहित्यक उद्देश्य अर्थकेँ परोक्ष रूपेँ बुझाएब वा अर्थ उत्पन्न करब अछि।
pairs.e: However, Derrida is accused of trying to appropriate the voice of women under the guise of male discourse, to snatch the authority that women have painstakingly achieved, the authority with which they speak for themselves.

pairs.m: ई सिद्धान्त दैत अछि परोक्ष अर्थक संरचना आ कार्य, रस माने सौन्दर्यक अनुभव आ अलंकारक सिद्धान्त।
pairs.e: Derrida is not concerned with women's daily problems and powerlessness.

pairs.m: आनन्दवर्धन काव्यक आत्मा ध्वनिकेँ मानैत छथि।
pairs.e: Derrida takes deconstruction positively and places feminism as a form of dualism in existential metaphysics/ logic, like man-woman.

pairs.m: ध्वनि द्वारा अर्थ तँ परोक्ष रूपेँ अबैत अछि मुदा ओ अबैत अछि सुसंगठित रूपमे।
pairs.e: Derrida exposes the obsession with a center-based structure in Western philosophy.

pairs.m: आ ऐसँ अर्थ आ प्रतीक, ई दूटा सिद्धान्त बहार होइत अछि।
pairs.e: Structuralism, inspired by Saussure's linguistics, attempts a scientific analysis of cultural existence.

pairs.m: ऐसँ रसक प्रभाव उत्पन्न होइत अछि, ऐसँ रस उत्पन्न होइत अछि।
pairs.e: Levi-Strauss's structural anthropology does the same for folklore.

pairs.m: न्याय आ मीमांसा ऐ सिद्धान्तक विरोध केलक, ई दुनू दर्शन कहैत अछि जे ध्वनिक अस्तित्व कतौ नै अछि, ई परिणाम अछि अनुमानक आ से पहिनहियेसँ लक्षणक अन्तर्गत अछि।
pairs.e: Similarly, attempts at structural analysis are constantly made in literature for prose and poetry.

pairs.m: आ से सभ शब्द द्वारा वर्णित हएब सम्भव नै अछि।
pairs.e: But Derrida exposes this.

pairs.m: स्फोट सिद्धांत: भर्तृहरीक वाकपदीय कहैत अछि जे शब्द आकि वाक्यक अर्थ स्फोट द्वारा संवाहित अछि।
pairs.e: Structuralism seeks a solid basis for its analysis, a center outside the system from which it can conduct its scientific analysis, but that is just an illusion of a philosopher.

pairs.m: वर्ण स्फोटसँ वर्ण, पद स्फोटसँ शब्द आ वाक्य स्फोटसँ वाक्यक निर्माण होइत अछि।
pairs.e: How can a folktale have a permanent or fixed structure?

pairs.m: कोनो ज्ञान बिनु शब्दक सम्बन्धक सम्भव नै अछि।
pairs.e: So, to study the structure of a folktale, you have to determine that idea or center based on which you will analyze it.

pairs.m: ई भारतीय दर्शनक ज्ञान सिद्धान्तक एकटा भाग बनि गेल।
pairs.e: Derrida says that for any structure, its conceptualization/idea is necessary; then by joining the fragments, you build it.

pairs.m: अर्थक संप्रेषण अक्षर, शब्द आ वाक्यक उत्पत्ति बिन सम्भव अछि।
pairs.e: But a structure is impossible without an end.

pairs.m: स्फोट अछि शब्दब्रह्म आ से अछि सृजनक मूल कारण।
pairs.e: And this is true for literature as well.

pairs.m: अक्षर, शब्द आ वाक्य संग-संग नै रहैए।
pairs.e: As long as you do not assume a self-evident meaning for a work, you cannot find its structure, because meaning, i.e., the end, determines its structure.

pairs.m: बाजल शब्दक फराक अक्षर अपनामे शब्दक अर्थ नै अछि, शब्द पूर्ण हएबा धरि एकर उत्पत्ति आ विनाश होइत रहै छै।
pairs.e: So, the structuralist already knows the meaning and can then analyze its different parts and their interrelationships.

pairs.m: स्फोटमे अर्थक संप्रेषण होइत अछि मुदा तखनो स्फोटमे प्राप्ति समए वा संचारक कालमे अक्षर, शब्द वा वाक्यक अस्तित्व नै भेल रहै छै।
pairs.e: And this pre-known, self-evident meaning before analysis is Derrida's center.

pairs.m: शब्दक पूर्णता धरि एक अक्षर आर नीक जकाँ क्रमसँ अर्थपूर्ण होइए आ वाक्य पूर्ण हेबा धरि शब्द क्रमसँ अर्थपूर्ण होइए।
pairs.e: Derrida says this determines how the structure of the text is built, which parts are included and which are excluded. So, when we analyze the structure of literature and discuss its meaning and effect, we attempt to identify and differentiate those elements responsible for that effect; we discard the potential patterns that do not produce that effect.

pairs.m: सांख्य, न्याय, वैशेषिक, मीमांसा आ वेदान्त ई सभ दर्शन स्फोटकेँ नै मानैत अछि।
pairs.e: Thus, this center is the starting point for understanding the structure.

pairs.m: ऐ सभ दर्शनक मानब अछि जे अक्षर आ ओकर ध्वनि अर्थकेँ नीक जकाँ पूर्ण करैत अछि।
pairs.e: But it also limits the analysis.

pairs.m: फ्रांसक जाक डेरीडाक विखण्डन आ पसरबाक सिद्धान्त स्फोट सिद्धान्तक लग अछि।
pairs.e: Because the center creates hierarchies to stabilize itself and controls them.

pairs.m: अलंकार सिद्धान्त: भामह अलंकारकेँ समासोक्ति कहै छथि जे आनन्दक कारण बनैए।
pairs.e: And this pre-knowledge of meaning also depends on the text's prior history and contemporary critical theory and ideology.

pairs.m: दण्डी आ उद्भट सेहो अलंकारक सिद्धान्तकेँ आगाँ बढ़बै छथि।
pairs.e: If these are all part of culture, then how can we keep them separate from the structure, which they limit, but why should we ourselves not be limited by them?

pairs.m: अलंकारक मूल रूपसँ दू प्रकार अछि, शब्द आ अर्थ आधारित आ आगाँ सादृश्य-विरोध, तर्कन्याय, लोकन्याय, काव्यन्याय आ गूढ़ार्थ प्रतीति आधारपर।
pairs.e: Making the basis of your analysis a fixed center is like providing a recipe, and this is a reactionary or status quo position.

pairs.m: मम्मट ६१ प्रकारक अलंकारकेँ ७ भागमे बाँटै छथि, उपमा माने उदाहरण, रूपक माने कहबी, अप्रस्तुत माने अप्रत्यक्ष प्रशंसा, दीपक माने विभाजित अलंकरण, व्यतिरेक माने असमानता प्रदर्शन, विरोध आ समुच्चय माने संगबे।
pairs.e: And from this, the desire to extract standard and incomparable independent meaning is proven false.

pairs.m: औचित्य सिद्धान्त: क्षेमेन्द्र औचित्य विचार चर्चामे औचित्यकेँ साहित्यक मुख्य तत्व मानलन्हि।
pairs.e: So, the illusion that we are seeing the structure is an illusion; in reality, you are constructing the structure from the textual material.

pairs.m: आ औचित्य कतऽ हेबाक चाही?
pairs.e: Thus, even in Derrida's post-structuralism, it is impossible to start without a center, but through the deconstructive analysis of that unproven self-evident fact, he replaces that center with another center, but this new center is also not permanent. Similarly, Saussure, in his theory of the sign, analyzes the sign: a word like 'cat' is 'cat' because it is not something else.

pairs.m: ई हेबाक चाही पद, वाक्य, प्रबन्धक अर्थ, गुण, अलंकार, रस, कारक, क्रिया, लिंग, वचन, विशेषण, उपसर्ग, निपात माने फाजिल, काल, देश कुल, व्रत, तत्व, सत्व माने आन्तरिक गुण, अभिप्राय, स्वभाव, सार-संग्रह, प्रतिभा, अवस्था, विचार, नाम आ आशीर्वादमे।
pairs.e: So, the word is the signifier, and what it denotes is the signified/ object.

pairs.m: टॉम स्टोपार्डक रेडियो नाटक “इन द नेटिव स्टेट” आ ओइपर आधारित हुनकर नाटक “इण्डियन इंक” रस सिद्धान्तक विस्तृत चर्चा करैत अछि।
pairs.e: But Derrida says you first need to bring a concept.

pairs.m: जॉन केजक “सोनाटाज एण्ड इण्टरल्यूड्स” मे सेहो रस सिद्धान्त आधारित धुन सभ अछि।
pairs.e: And if you make the signifier absolute, claiming it has no relation to any signified, that it is an independent concept in itself, with no direct relation to any signified/object, then this concept transcends all signifiers and signifies nothing. And if we consider the signifier and signified as identical, it challenges the very basis of the signifier.

pairs.m: संगे रिचा बिस्वाल “Analysis of John Donne's Poetry in the Light of Bharata's Rasa-Theory” लिखलन्हि जे कवि कुलगुरु कालिदास संस्कृत विश्वविद्यालय, रामटेकसँ प्रकाशत अछि।
pairs.e: So, Saussure's theory is opposed to logic-based reasoning when he says you do not see any characteristic of the signified in the signifier.

pairs.m: सुशील चारियेटा पोथी लिखलनि, दूटा उपन्यास, एकटा नाटक आ एकटा लघुकथा संग्रह।
pairs.e: But when he says the signifier is merely used to denote the signified and is therefore subordinate to it, he appears to support a logic-based theory.

pairs.m: रामलोचन ठाकुर अस्मिताक आमुखमे लिखै छथि- “ई बहुत रास कथा लिखने छथि, किन्तु सभ पुस्तकाकार प्रकाशित कराएब सम्भव नहि।”
pairs.e: So Derrida says this clear distinction between signified and signifier is not valid, and the priority given to signified over signifier should also be abandoned. Meaning does not pre-exist in the signifier. And complete meaning is not found in a single signifier.

pairs.m: दुनू उपन्यासमे सेहो हमरा लागल जे उपन्यासकार सुशीलपर कम पन्नामे लिखबाक दवाब छलन्हि, मुदा ओत्तौ ओ थाकल नै लगला।
pairs.e: So, you have to delve deep into the text, and this drives you towards an endless search.

pairs.m: अस्मिता, भोकाटा आ असल बात जड़िअओठा तीनू कमजोर कथा लागल, कथाकार थाकल बुझेला।
pairs.e: Thus, the signifier determines the signified based on its difference from other signifiers; the more difference, the closer you are to the signified. But you can never capture all the differences.

pairs.m: मुदा कथाकार थाकि गेला आकि थका देल गेल।
pairs.e: However, there must be repetition in the signifier, even when one signifier determines a different signified. And for this, knowing the written history of that signifier is necessary. So, the signifier gives different meanings and sometimes even opposite meanings.

pairs.m: मैथिलीक छद्म समीक्षा हुनकर हत्या कऽ देलक।
pairs.e: Plato: Plato says no art can be good because it is all false and unreal.

pairs.m: हिनकर कथा ‘एकटा कलाकारक आत्महत्या’ मे कलाकार कला छोड़ि देलक तँ भऽ गेल आत्महत्या; मुदा जँ दोसर ओइ लेल दोषी अछि आ तेँ ओ छोड़ि देलक कथा-उपन्यास-नाटक लिखनाइ तँ ई भेल हत्या।
pairs.e: Plato's idea should also be seen as a reaction to the military inferiority of Athens compared to Sparta. Derrida writes that from Plato to Saussure and Levi-Strauss, everyone prioritized speech over writing, because writing is a medium; the real thing is speech.

pairs.m: उपन्यासमे सुशील एकटा समय-स्थानक खाका खीचि दै छथि।
pairs.e: Saussure focuses on pronunciation errors in the written form.

pairs.m: जेना घराड़ी (उपन्यास) (१९७३) गामक दलानपर प्रारम्भ होइत अछि, दलानपर की-की राखल रहै छै आ तइ दलानक सोझाँक दृश्य सेहो।
pairs.e: But Derrida says the characteristics present in speech are also present in writing.

pairs.m: फेर मिलिट्री मैन रतन अबैए जकर घराड़ी जोति लेल गेल छै।
pairs.e: Furthermore, he says the effacement of the signified creates the illusion of the direct presence of thought in speech. But if we record spoken words and listen, it is also a chain of signifiers like written words, in which different signifiers can be identified by their differences from each other.

pairs.m: गाम आ बहरियाक संघर्ष शुरू।
pairs.e: And writing can also be of two types: ordinary writing and writing done through understanding or pictographic writing.

pairs.m: घराड़ीक मोह, लालबाबू बदलेन करैले तैयार छथि बरू दू कट्ठा बेसी देता मुदा रतन बदलेन नै करत, दोसर ठाम उपटि कऽ किए जायत?
pairs.e: Behind all this is the 'metaphysics of presence'.

pairs.m: जकर जन्म नग्रमे भेल छै ओकरा घराड़ीक मोह बुझैमे दिक्कत हेतै।
pairs.e: The 'metaphysics of presence' in Western philosophy, the core of which is immediate experience—but deconstruction says there is no such experience at a meta-linguistic level, because this experience is identified through the medium of language. And then, the idea that we understand ultimate truth through divine consciousness—deconstruction says this is merely a creation of the creator.

pairs.m: घराड़ीक लेल रतनक छोटका भाइ महादेबक हत्या भऽ जाइ छै आ आरोप रतनपर लागि जाइ छै।
pairs.e: And also, the notion that truth is hidden behind an object—deconstruction says no such independent existence exists; everything is created and recreated by the system.

pairs.m: ओ पड़ा जाइए।
pairs.e: So, Saussure's self-presence is nothing, because everything is constructed within a system.

pairs.m: फेर पेंचपर पेंच।
pairs.e: Derrida says that logic is the basis of all these philosophical thoughts; an ultimate truth and universal self is hypothesized, which is omniscient. But Derrida says this unproven center has been called God at times, Idea at times, and Universal Self at times, and it created different levels below itself.

pairs.m: कथा शुरू होइए ब्राह्मण डॉ. बिनायक आ मलाह नर्स करुणाक।
pairs.e: So, religion considered God as the ultimate truth, and humans and other creations as incomplete and opposed; and all these became centers that claimed to create a system of thought in their own way.

pairs.m: करुणा कहैए, ई विवाह असम्भव अछि, ओ नीच जातिक अछि, मलाहक बेटी अछि आ बिनायक पैघ लोक।
pairs.e: But they must be above the system.

pairs.m: मुदा प्रेम तँ ओकरो छैहे।
pairs.e: So, God must exist independently outside religion. Post-structuralist deconstruction considers the existence of such a meta-truth as virtual, and post-structuralists consider it the result of a theory of language.

pairs.m: बिनायक करुणाकेँ कायर बुझैए।
pairs.e: Thus, in the interplay of these signifiers, some meaning achieves a higher status due to the intervention of some ideology, and its other meaning is pushed behind it.

pairs.m: मुदा उपन्यासकार एकटा लाठी लेने बूढ़ाक प्रवेश ओकर स्वप्नमे करबै छथिन्ह- जे बिनायककेँ कहै छथि जे जकरा बिनायक सबला बुझि रहल छथि ओ समाजक गनहायल परिपाटीक चलते अबला अछि।
pairs.e: Ideologies like freedom, democracy, justice are part of our lives, but it seems that many meanings of our lives have been derived from them, but upon investigation, they turn out to be derived from other ideas.

pairs.m: ओम्हर रतन अपन मित्र शंकर भगवानक भक्त अवधेशक ताकिमे अछि, निमतल्ला घाटमे भूतनाथ मन्दिरमे शंकर भगवानक शरणमे जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: There is no concept that does not contain traces of other ideas. In response to "gaslighting," Story Science emerges.

pairs.m: आ अवधेश भेट जाइ छै, भूपेन बोश एवेन्यूमे ओ पत्नी सुनीता संगे रहैए।
pairs.e: It pre-analyzes based on the impact of hearing a story on people and society.

pairs.m: गामसँ नग्र, उपन्यासकारक विवरण आब दोसर दिशामे जाइए।
pairs.e: Story Science also says that the one who tells the story rules, and the more stories one tells, the further ahead one goes.

pairs.m: रतनपर वारंट छै।
pairs.e: "He who listens to the story rules this world"—Hopi American Indian proverb. Indian Concept of Poetry: The concept of art for the purpose of liberation (moksha), as seen in the Nataraja's posture.

pairs.m: फेर नग्रसँ गाम।
pairs.e: The rhythm of creation and destruction is visible.

pairs.m: कोन एहेन गप छै जे मलहा आ लालबाबूए टा केँ बूझल छन्हि।
pairs.e: The permanent emotion (sthayibhava) deepens, matures, and becomes rasa (essence)—and thus, many rasas were experienced by Sita and Ram (see Valmiki Ramayana). Krishna is the teacher of Indian karma (action) and also a connoisseur (rasika).

pairs.m: अवधेश संगे शेफालीसँ भेँट होइ छै।
pairs.e: For the enjoyment of art, the concept of Rasa became necessary, and Bharata wrote the Natya Shastra.

pairs.m: गपक क्रममे शेफाली कहै छथि जे अवधेश कतेक मैथिल सभकेँ नोकरी दियेलथिन मुदा वएह सभ हिनकर निन्दा करैत रहै छन्हि।
pairs.e: Abhinavagupta wrote a commentary on Anandavardhana's Dhvanyaloka.

pairs.m: शेफाली कहै छथिन जे शेफालीक मामा वकील छथिन्ह, ओ कहै छथिन्ह जे रतनक जीत हेतै मुदा असली हत्याराक पता सेहो लगबऽ पड़त।
pairs.e: Bhamaha (6th century), Dandi (7th century), and Rudrata (9th century) advanced it further. Rasa Theory: Bharata: Rasa is produced from the effect of drama.

pairs.m: रतनकेँ विश्वास छै जे असली हत्यारा लालबाबूक जिरतिया परगा छै।
pairs.e: What is drama for?

pairs.m: शेफालीक पिता राय जी मैथिल आ माता बंगाली।
pairs.e: Drama is for the enactment of rasa and also for the production of rasa.

pairs.m: से नग्रमे प्रेम विवाह एक पीढ़ी पहिने भऽ चुकल छै।
pairs.e: How does rasa emerge? Rasa emerges from the determinants (vibhava), consequences (anubhava), and accompanying elements (vyabhichari).

pairs.m: लालबाबू परगासक माध्यमसँ करुणाकेँ निपत्ता करबा देता।
pairs.e: The permanent emotion deepens, matures, and becomes rasa, whose essence we can taste.

pairs.m: परगास चारि गोटेसँ गेल मुदा करुणा दाँत काटि कऽ अपनाकेँ छोड़ा लेलक।
pairs.e: Bhatta Lollata: The permanent emotion, through cause and effect, becomes rasa.

pairs.m: करुणा आ परगासक बियाह करा देता आ मामिले खतम।
pairs.e: Actors and actresses experience rasa through practice and imagination. Lollata does not give importance to the poet or the listener/viewer.

pairs.m: घर घुरै छथि।
pairs.e: Shounaka: Shounaka believes that to experience rasa, the viewer must immerse themselves in the performance, like understanding the essence of a horse by seeing it as a horse from its picture.

pairs.m: एमहर नग्रक वर्णन।
pairs.e: Bhatta Nayaka says the effect of rasa is on the viewer.

pairs.m: मिस्टर राय सभक बदौलत रतनकेँ नोकरीयो भेटलै आ जमानतो।
pairs.e: He considers the poet's language to be special. Rasa unites the soul of the listener/viewer with the Supreme Soul.

pairs.m: परगासकेँ लालबाबू अन्तिम नाटक खेलेबा लेल कहै छथिन्ह।
pairs.e: The bliss of rasa is the bliss of one's own nature.

pairs.m: अन्तिम खण्ड अछि खण्ड सत्रह।
pairs.e: And this leads to self-realization.

pairs.m: दुर्गा भसानक दिन।
pairs.e: Rasa theory is based on the listener/viewer/reader. It emphasizes the listener/viewer/reader.

pairs.m: दुर्गापूजामे तीनटा नाटक मंचस्थ कएल जाइए, दूटा मैथिली आ एकटा हिन्दी।
pairs.e: Dhvani Theory: Anandavardhana's Dhvanyaloka—the purpose of literature is to suggest meaning indirectly or to produce meaning.

pairs.m: भाषा विमर्श तइ दिनमे आबि गेल छल।
pairs.e: This theory provides the structure and function of indirect meaning, the experience of beauty (rasa), and the theory of figures of speech (alankara).

pairs.m: रहस्योद्घाटन आ पश्चाताप।
pairs.e: Anandavardhana considers dhvani (suggestion) as the soul of poetry. Through dhvani, meaning comes indirectly, but it comes in a well-organized manner.

pairs.m: आ बिजयादशमीक नाटक समाप्त।
pairs.e: And from this, two theories emerge: meaning and symbol.

pairs.m: तँ घराड़ी उपन्याससँ मैथिली साहित्य पढ़निहारकेँ की भेटलै आ मैथिली साहित्यकेँ की भेटलै?
pairs.e: From this, the effect of rasa is produced. Nyaya and Mimamsa opposed this theory; both philosophies say that dhvani does not exist anywhere; it is a result of inference and already falls under the category of indication. And not everything can be described by words.

pairs.m: मैथिली साहित्यक पाठक लेलक टाइम-मशीनक अनुभवक सङे स्पेस-मशीनक अनुभव सेहो।
pairs.e: Sphoṭa Theory: Bhartrihari's Vakyapadiya says that the meaning of a word or sentence is conveyed by sphoṭa (the linguistic unit).

pairs.m: सन् १९७३ मे हिन्दी नाटक मिथिलामे आबि गेल रहै।
pairs.e: From varṇa-sphoṭa, the phoneme; from pada-sphoṭa, the word; and from vakya-sphoṭa, the sentence is constructed. No knowledge is possible without the connection of the word.

pairs.m: अन्तर्जातीय विवाहक स्वीकृति बाप नै दै छलै मुदा माय दऽ दै छलै।
pairs.e: This became a part of the epistemology of Indian philosophy. The transmission of meaning is possible without the origin of the letter, word, and sentence.

pairs.m: घराड़ी आ जमीन-जाल लेल हत्या तक होइ छलै।
pairs.e: Sphoṭa is the Word-Brahman and is the root cause of creation.

pairs.m: मिथिलेक लोक कलकत्तामे एक पुस्त पहिने अन्तर्जातीय विवाह कऽ रहल छल।
pairs.e: The letter, word, and sentence do not exist simultaneously.

pairs.m: एक वर्गमे शिक्षा रहै, मुदा दोसर वर्गकेँ हतोत्साहित कएल जाइ, जमीन खेती लेल देल जाइ मुदा बिनु रजिस्ट्रीक।
pairs.e: The separate letters in spoken words do not contain the meaning of the word; their origin and destruction continue until the word is complete. In sphoṭa, meaning is transmitted, but even then, at the time of realization or the moment of communication, the letter, word, or sentence did not exist.

pairs.m: जमीन-जथा बला लोकक दलानपर संदूक होइ छलै, दरबज्जापर बड़द।
pairs.e: Until the word is complete, each successive letter becomes meaningful, and until the sentence is complete, each successive word becomes meaningful.

pairs.m: कलकत्ता (आब कोलकाता) मे बाते अलग रहै।
pairs.e: Samkhya, Nyaya, Vaisheshika, Mimamsa, and Vedanta—all these philosophies do not accept sphoṭa.

pairs.m: मुदा ओत्तौ जइ मैथिलकेँ नोकरी लगाउ सएह खिधांश करत।
pairs.e: These philosophies believe that letters and their sounds adequately complete the meaning. France's Jacques Derrida's theory of deconstruction and dissemination is close to the sphoṭa theory.

pairs.m: गामबाली (उपन्यास) (१९८२) एक डेगा आगाँ बढ़ैत अछि।
pairs.e: Alankara Theory: Bhamaha calls alankara (figures of speech) as 'samasokti', which causes delight.

pairs.m: घराड़ी उपन्यासमे बादमे खुलासा भेलै जे लड़की मलाह नै ब्राह्मणे रहै।
pairs.e: Dandi and Udbhata also advanced the theory of alankara.

pairs.m: मुदा गामबालीमे से खगता उपन्यासकारकेँ नै पड़लन्हि।
pairs.e: There are fundamentally two types of alankara: based on word and based on meaning. Further classifications are based on similarity-opposition, logic, folk logic, poetic logic, and suggestion of deeper meaning.

pairs.m: ओ आब लिव-इन रिलेशन (बिनु बियाहक संग रहनाइ), ओइसँ भेल बच्चा, ओकर गाममे स्वीकृति, पढ़ुआ सभक आगाँक सोच सभ किछु आनि लेने छथि।
pairs.e: Mammata divides 61 types of alankara into 7 parts: Upama (simile), Rupaka (metaphor), Aprastuta (indirect praise), Dipaka (shared illumination), Vyatireka (contrast), Virodha (opposition), and Samucchaya (accumulation).

pairs.m: पहिने श्वेत-श्याम टी.
pairs.e: Aucitya Theory: Kshemendra, in his discussion of propriety (aucitya), considered it the main element of literature.

And where should propriety exist? It should exist in pada (word), vakya (sentence), the meaning of the composition (prabandha), guna (qualities), alankara, rasa, karaka (agent), kriya (action), linga (gender), vachana (number), visheshana (qualifier), upasarga (prefix), nipata (particle), kala (time), desha (place), kula (lineage), vrata (vow), tattva (principle), satva (inner nature), abhipraya (intention), svabhava (nature), sara-sangraha (essence collection), pratibha (genius), avastha (condition), vichara (thought), nama (name), and ashirvada (blessing).

pairs.m: क जमाना रहै, १९८२ मे १९ नवम्बरकेँ भारतमे एशियाडक उद्घाटन भेलै आ ओही दिनसँ रंगीन टी.
pairs.e: Tom Stoppard's radio play "In the Native State" and his subsequent play "Indian Ink" based on it discuss the Rasa theory in detail.
John Cage's "Sonatas and Interludes" also contains melodies based on the Rasa theory.

pairs.m: क प्रसारण दूरदर्शन करऽ लगलै।
pairs.e: Additionally, Richa Biswal wrote "Analysis of John Donne's Poetry in the Light of Bharata's Rasa-Theory," published by Kavikulguru Kalidas Sanskrit University, Ramtek. Sushil wrote four books: two novels, one play, and one short story collection.

pairs.m: घराड़ीक लालबाबूसँ भिन्न स्थिति अछि मालिक माने सोनमनि झा केर।
pairs.e: Ramlochan Thakur writes in the introduction to Asmitā: "He has written many stories, but publishing all of them in book form was not possible."

pairs.m: ओ छथि पाउ-पाउ करैत।
pairs.e: In both novels, I felt that the novelist Sushil was under pressure to write concisely, but he didn't seem tired. 'Asmitā', 'Bhokaṭa', and 'Asal Bāt Jaṛiāoṭhā' seemed like three weak stories; perhaps the story writer was tired. But was the writer tired, or was he made to feel tired? The pseudocriticism in Maithili killed him.

pairs.m: माने जमीन्दारीक अन्त बुझू।
pairs.e: In his story 'Ekta Kalakar Ki Atmahatyā', when the artist gave up art, it was a suicide; but if others are responsible for it, and he gave up writing stories, novels, and plays, then it was a murder.

pairs.m: गामबालीक संस्कार जादव समाज मिलि कऽ करतै।
pairs.e: In his novel, Sushil sketches a picture of a specific time and place.

pairs.m: गोअर टोली, बभन टोली, कैथ टोली, जोलहा टोली।
pairs.e: For example, Gharāṛī (novel) (1973) begins on the veranda of the village house, describing what was kept on the veranda and the view in front of it. Then enters Ratan, a military man, whose share of land has been plowed. The conflict between the village and the outside world begins.

pairs.m: ई सभ टोल छै।
pairs.e: The attachment to the ancestral land; Lalbabu is ready to exchange it, even offering two kathas extra, but Ratan does not exchange; why would he uproot himself and go elsewhere?

pairs.m: गामबालीक वर बूढ़, से बाल विवाह छै।
pairs.e: Someone born in a city might find it hard to understand the attachment to ancestral land.

pairs.m: ओ तेसर बहु छली, से बहुविवाह सेहो छै, हिन्दू मैरेज एक्ट १९५५ मे लागू भेलै मुदा मिथिलाक गाममे १९८२ मे छपल उपन्यासमे ओ लागू नै छै, नग्र सभक वी.
pairs.e: For the sake of the land, Ratan's younger brother Mahadev is murdered, and Ratan is accused. He goes into hiding. Then, twists and turns.

pairs.m: आइ.
pairs.e: The story begins with the Brahmin Dr. Binayak and the Mallah nurse Karuna.
Karuna says this marriage is impossible; she is of low caste, a Mallah's daughter, and Binayak is a big man.

pairs.m: मोहल्लामे लागो छल हेतै।
pairs.e: But she too has love. Binayak thinks Karuna is a coward.

pairs.m: आइ गामबालीक मृत्युक बाद ओकर प्रशंसक कम नै छै, शानसँ आयलि आ शानसँ चलि गेलि।
pairs.e: But the novelist introduces an old man with a stick into his dream, who tells Binayak that the one whom Binayak considers strong is actually weak due to the suffocating norms of society.

pairs.m: जमाना बदलि रहल छै।
pairs.e: Meanwhile, Ratan is under the protection of his friend Avadhesh, a devotee of Lord Shiva.

pairs.m: गीतक प्रेमी रहय ज्ञानचन।
pairs.e: He goes to the Bhootnath temple at Nimtala Ghat to seek refuge with Lord Shiva. He meets Avadhesh, who lives with his wife Sunita on Bhupen Bose Avenue.

pairs.m: विदेशिया नाच आ नौटंकी मे खूब मोन लागै, एकरा सोलकन्हक विधा मानल जाइ छलै मुदा कुलीन धीया पूता आ नवतूर आगुए जा कय बैसए।
pairs.e: From the village to the city, the novelist's description now moves in another direction.

pairs.m: विदेशिया नाचक सभटा गीत याद रहै ओकरा।
pairs.e: There is a warrant against Ratan.

pairs.m: विद्यापति, निर्गुण नचारी ओ ओहुना टेरि दीअय, बाभन सभकेँ खूब पसिन्न पड़ै, बाभनक गाम रहै।
pairs.e: Then back from the city to the village. What was the matter that the Mallahs and Lalbabu understood?

pairs.m: मीरा आ कबीरक भजन ओहिना याद रहै ओकरा।
pairs.e: He meets Shéfali with Avadhesh.

pairs.m: मुदा जालिम सिंहक नाच आ गीत लेल मोन अहुरिया काटै, धुरछीया हेतै, साहस नै होइ।
pairs.e: During the conversation, Shéfali says Avadhesh gave jobs to many Maithils, but they all keep criticizing him.

pairs.m: मुदा आब गाममे, ओइ गाममे जकर चर्चा सुशील केने छथि, बहुत किछु बदलि गेल छै।
pairs.e: Shéfali says her uncle is a lawyer; he says Ratan will win, but the real murderer must also be found. Ratan believes the real murderer is Lalbabu's henchman, Paragas.

pairs.m: सुशीलक आधुनिकता ऐ मे छै जे ओ नग्र आ गाम दुनूक नीक आ अधला बौस्तुकेँ सोझाँ अनै छथि, ओ नहिये आधुनिक, नहिये पुरातन, नहिये नव बौस्तु बा विचारधाराक मोहमे छथि।
pairs.e: In 'Bāt Tākait Ekta Sehantā', the buffalo's application was approved; the condition of schools is the same across the country, so how could that be right? In 'Mātsarya', the story skillfully portrays the narrator's mental state.

pairs.m: सुशीलक आधुनिकता समावेशी अछि, एक दोसराक विचारधारा सँ किछु लेबा, किछु देबामे विश्वास करैत अछि, संगहि कोनो गलतीकेँ झँपबा-तोपबामे विश्वास नै करैत अछि, विचारधाराक नामपर सेहो नै।
pairs.e: 'Vigyāpan Karāmāt' is about the magic of advertising.

pairs.m: आ तेँ मूलधाराक छद्म समीक्षक गंगेश सन हिनको ऑनर-किलिंग करैत छन्हि।
pairs.e: 'Masjid': Devkant's guru has to go far to offer namaz.

pairs.m: आ तेँ जखन ई पोथी हम समाप्त कऽ रहल छी तहियो परिस्थिति बदलल नै छै आ हमरा विदेहक ३९७ म अंकक सम्पादकीय एना लिखऽ पड़ि रहल अछि।
pairs.e: Devkant decides to build a mosque in the village for his guru, with everyone's cooperation. 'Haḍtāl': Bhola passed matriculation.

pairs.m: ११७ म सगर राति दीप जरय, दरभंगा (आयोजक हीरेन्द्र झा आ अशोक कुमार मेहता) साहित्य अकादेमीक मैथिली परामर्शदातृ समितिक अध्यक्ष (आब भूतपूर्व) अशोक अविचलक दृष्टि सगर राति दीप जरय पर पड़ल आ ओकरा गिड़बाक प्रयासमे हीरेन्द्र झा केर ब्लैकमेलिंग केर शिकार ओ सहर्ष भेला आ टैगोरक १५० म जयन्ती वर्षक साहित्य अकादेमीक कार्यक्रम जे दिल्लीक एकटा संस्था करबेने रहय केर गिनती सेहो करैत नम्बरमे हेरफेर करबाक अपराध केलन्हि, जकरा ओ चलाकी बुझैत छथि।
pairs.e: He opened a tea shop. Today there is a strike.

pairs.m: मैथिली परामर्शदातृ समितिक अध्यक्ष बनलाक बाद लोकमे ऐ तरहक प्रवृत्ति देखल गेल छै, जकर शिकार रामदेव झा भेला, जे शोधक लेल ख्यात रहथि; चन्द्रनाथ मिश्र अमर भेला, जिनकर पहिल फेजक आन्दोलनी योगदान झँपा गेलन्हि, आब नचिकेतापर ई ग्रहण लागल छन्हि।
pairs.e: A pipe gun bullet hits him; he dies. 'Sadak': A road is being built in the village.

pairs.m: हीरेन्द्र झा ऐ लगा तेसर बेर ई कुकृत्य केलन्हि अछि, जइमे ऐ बेर कमल मोहन झा आकि मिश्र चुन्नू सेहो हुनकर संग देलखिन्ह।
pairs.e: Sukru could open a tea shop. There is great shame in keeping buffaloes; some say it's a 'Naath Kasu' issue, people grab onto caste.

pairs.m: हँ सह-सहयोजक अशोक कुमार मेहता मुदा ऐ कुकृत्यमे हुनकर संग नै देलखिन्ह।
pairs.e: But Lambodar Babu.

pairs.m: पूर्व नियोजित षडयंत्र जइमे अशोक अविचलक योगदान ऊपर कहल गेल अछि, मे हीरेन्द्र झा केँ सगर राति दीप जरय केर सह-आयोजक बनाओल गेल (स्पष्ट रूपसँ कहि देल गेलन्हि जे नम्बरमे हेरफेर नै करता), फेर कमल मोहन झा आकि मिश्र चुन्नूकेँ तैयार राखल गेल।
pairs.e: the road doesn't get built.

pairs.m: मुदा सभ चोरिमे एकटा निशान छुटिते छै।
pairs.e: Well, half a life has already passed. 'Katahu Nahi': The village seems new, like a different village.

pairs.m: अध्यक्ष महोदय बिना कोनो प्रस्ताव एने धड़फड़ीमे बाजि देलखिन्ह जे अगिला गोष्ठी पटना आ तकर अगिला दिल्ली (जतऽ साहित्य अकादेमीक मुख्यालय अछि) मे हएत, आ फेर गप सम्हारलखिन्ह जे से तकर निर्णय (दिल्लीक) पटनामे कएल जायत।
pairs.e: Chhote Babu had passed away; he had told Pritam two days ago to be present on the 15th of August, but Pritam had gone off somewhere with some sister yesterday. He would understand everything here; such things are not hidden.

pairs.m: कमल मोहन झा आकि मिश्र चुन्नू तैयारे-तैयार रहथि, फटाकसँ चोरिक रजिस्टर आ दीप लेलन्हि हीरेन्द्र झा सँ आ निर्ललज्जापूर्वक हँसबाक प्रयास करैत (मुदा हँसी तँ छोड़ू हास्यक पात्र लगै छथि) फोटो घिचबेलन्हि।
pairs.e: The body shivered. 'Sabta Rehal-Khehal': The ten cars, gossip, then a robbery.

pairs.m: प्रतिकार की हुअय?
pairs.e: Then the R.

pairs.m: ई ऐ लगा हीरेन्द्र झा केर तेसर गलती अछि से हुनका तीन सगर राति लेल बैन कएल जाय, कमल मोहन झा आकि मिश्र चुन्नूक ई पहिल गलती अछि से हुनका एक सगर राति लेल बैन कएल जाय।
pairs.e: P. F.

pairs.m: उपस्थित लोकक बहु-संख्या ११८ म सगर रातिक आयोजक ताकथि।
pairs.e: arrives.

pairs.m: अगिला सगर रातिक नम्बर ११८ रहय आ तकर प्रचार हुअय, जइसँ साहित्य अकादेमीक मैथिली परामर्शदातृ समितिक अध्यक्ष ई कुकर्म करबाक विषयमे सोचितो डराथि।
pairs.e: These passengers come once or twice a year; the ten cars are all ruined. Sushil's modernity lies in the fact that he brings forth both the good and bad aspects of the city and the village.

pairs.m: नव साहित्य अकादेमीक मैथिली परामर्शदातृ समितिक अध्यक्ष आ सदस्य लोकनिक कुकृत्य नव साहित्य अकादेमीक मैथिली परामर्शदातृ समिति अपन प्रचारमे पुरना समितिक लेल ई श्लोक पढ़लन्हि: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।
pairs.e: He is neither modern nor ancient, nor is he enamored by new things or ideologies. Sushil's modernity is inclusive; it believes in taking something from one ideology and giving something else, and it does not believe in covering up or hiding mistakes, even in the name of ideology.

pairs.m: अभुत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥
pairs.e: Therefore, mainstream pseudocritics like Gangesh commit honor killing on him.

pairs.m: ७ (श्रीमदभागवदगीता) जखन-जखन धर्मक नाश आ अधर्मक उत्थान प्रारम्भ हएत हे भरत, तखन-तखन हम आयब (शरीर धारण करब)।
pairs.e: And as we are concluding this book, the situation has not changed, and we have to write an editorial like this in issue 397 of Videha.

pairs.m: आ से ततेक अधर्म भेलै जे नचिकेता आ हुनकर टीम अवतार लेलक।
pairs.e: 117, Sagar Raati Deep Jare, Darbhanga (Organizers: Hirendra Jha and Ashok Kumar Mehta) The gaze of the then-chairperson of the Sahitya Akademi's Maithili Advisory Committee (now former), Ashok Avichal, fell on 'Sagar Raati Deep Jare', and in an attempt to destroy it, Hirendra Jha became a willing victim of blackmailing. He committed the crime of tampering with numbers, even counting the programs of a Delhi-based organization organized during Tagore's 150th birth anniversary year for the Sahitya Akademi, which he considers cleverness.

pairs.m: आ केलक की?
pairs.e: Such tendencies have been observed in people after becoming the chairperson of the Maithili Advisory Committee.

pairs.m: मासो नै बितलै पहिल बैसकीमे मोनोग्राफ, अनुवाद आदिक बँटवारा भेलै आ टीमक सदस्य सभ अपनेमे सभटा असाइनमेण्ट बाँटि लइ गेल, अपना-अपनी आ लगुआ-भगुआमे, नचिकेताजी देखैत रहि गेला, वरन सहयोगे केलखिन्ह।
pairs.e: Victims include Ramdev Jha, known for his research; Chandranath Mishra Amar, whose contributions to the first phase of the movement were covered up; and now an eclipse has fallen on Nachiketa.

pairs.m: समानान्तर धाराकेँ स्कोर पहिनहियो शून्य रहै, अवतारी पुरुष सभ तकरा शून्ये रखलन्हि।
pairs.e: Hirendra Jha has done this misdeed for the third time; this time, Kamal Mohan Jha or Mishra Chunoo also joined him. Yes, the co-organizer Ashok Kumar Mehta did not join him in this misdeed. What happened?

pairs.m: पुरस्कारक राजनीति नारायणजी दाड़िमक बदला अनार लिखलन्हि आ बाल साहित्यकार ओ छथि नै, पुरस्कार लेल बाल साहित्य लिखलन्हि।
pairs.e: In a pre-planned conspiracy, to which Ashok Avichal's contribution is described above, Hirendra Jha was made the co-organizer of 'Sagar Raati Deep Jare' (clearly told not to tamper with the numbers), and Kamal Mohan Jha or Mishra Chunoo were kept ready.

pairs.m: मुन्नी कामतक चुक्का विदेह पेटारमे उपलब्ध अछि, नारायणजी ओइ पोथीकेँ पढ़थु आ निर्णय लेथु जे की ओइ पोथीक सोझाँमे हुनकर पोथी ठठै छन्हि।
pairs.e: But every theft leaves a clue. The chairperson, in a hurry without any proposal, declared that the next conference would be in Patna, and the one after that in Delhi (where the Sahitya Akademi's headquarters are), and then he corrected himself, saying the decision (about the Delhi one) would be made in Patna.

pairs.m: आ जँ अन्तरात्मामा नै छन्हि तँ हीरेन्द्र झा/ कमल मोहन झा आकि मिश्र चुन्नू सन निर्लज्जतासँ पुरस्कार लेथु, हमर अग्रिम शुभकामना।
pairs.e: Kamal Mohan Jha or Mishra Chunoo were ready; they immediately took the registration book and lamp from Hirendra Jha and tried to smile shamelessly (but they seem like objects of humor, not even laughter).

pairs.m: आ जँ अन्तरात्मा नै मानै छन्हि तँ दलदलसँ बाहर आबथु।
pairs.e: They got their pictures taken.

pairs.m: [विदेह अंक ३९७ सम्पादकीय] [मिशेल फोको (Foucault)क "अनुशासन संस्था" बा मनोवैज्ञानिक बार्टन आ ह्वाइटहेडक "गैसलाइटिंग" दुनूक लक्ष्य एक्के छै।
pairs.e: What should be the response? This is Hirendra Jha's third mistake in this series; he should be banned from three 'Sagar Raati' events.

pairs.m: मिशेल फोकोक "अनुशासन संस्था" अछि, सोझाँबलाकेँ अनुशासनमे आनू आ तइ लेल सभकेँ आपसेमे लड़ाउ, किछुकेँ पुरस्कृत करू आ जे अनुशासनमे नै अबैए तकरा आस्ते-आस्ते माहुर दियौ।
pairs.e: This is Kamal Mohan Jha or Mishra Chunoo's first mistake; they should be banned from one 'Sagar Raati' event.

pairs.m: गैसलाइटिंगमे सोझाँबला केँ विश्वास दिआओल जाइत अछि जे अहाँ जे यथास्थितिक विरोध कऽ रहल छी से कोनो विरोध नै, ई तँ सभ कऽ रहल अछि, अहाँ तँ विरोधक नामपर विरोध कऽ रहल छी आ से अपन कमी नुकेबा लेल।
pairs.e: The majority of the people present were waiting for the 118th 'Sagar Raati' organizers.

pairs.m: ऐमे समाजमे वर्तमान आधारभूत कमीक सहायता सेहो लेल जाइत अछि, आ आस्ते-आस्ते टारगेट बताह भऽ जाइत अछि बा पलायन कऽ जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: The next 'Sagar Raati' number should be 118, and it should be publicized so that the chairperson of the Sahitya Akademi's Maithili Advisory Committee would think twice before doing such a deed. Misdeeds of the New Sahitya Akademi Maithili Advisory Committee Chairperson and Members For their own publicity, the new Sahitya Akademi Maithili Advisory Committee recited this shloka for the old committee: Yada yada hi dharmasya glanirbhavati Bharata Abhyutthanam adharmasya tadatmanam srijamyaham ||4.7|| (Bhagavad Gita) Whenever there is a decline in righteousness and a rise in unrighteousness, O Bharata, then I manifest myself. And so much unrighteousness occurred that Nachiketa and his team took an avatar.

pairs.m: मिशेल फोकोक सभटा डिसीप्लिनरी इंस्टीट्यूशन "अनुशासन संस्था" जेना साहित्य अकादेमी, मैथिली अकादमी, मैथिली भोजपुरी अकादमी, आ साहित्य अकादेमी द्वारा मान्यता प्राप्त कथित लिटेरेरी असोसियेशन सभ मूल धारा लग छै।
pairs.e: And what did they do?

pairs.m: सगर राति दीप जरय केँ मिशेल फोकोक परिभाषित डिसीप्लिनरी इंस्टीट्यूशनमे अनबाक प्रयास बहुत दिनसँ कएल जा रहल छै।]
pairs.e: Within a month, in the first meeting itself, the distribution of monographs, translations, etc., was done, and the team members distributed all the assignments among themselves, their cronies, and followers.

pairs.m: सुशीलक नाटक, कथा बा उपन्यासमे कन्नारोहट नै अछि।
pairs.e: Nachiketaji kept watching, even cooperating. The score for the parallel stream was zero anyway; the avataric people kept it at zero.

pairs.m: जइ कथामे नीक अन्त अछि ओतऽ तँ नहिये जतऽ अधला अन्त अछि तत्तौ कन्नारोहट नै अछि, हँ व्यंग्य अछि।
pairs.e: Politics of Awards Narayanji wrote 'Anar' instead of 'Daarim' and he is not a children's writer; he wrote children's literature to get an award.

pairs.m: जेना सड़क कथामे- “चलू आधा जिनगी तँ बीतिये गेल अछि”।
pairs.e: Munni Kamat's 'Chukka' is available in the Videha Petar.

pairs.m: जेना हड़ताल कथामे -पाइप गनक गोली ओकरा लागै छै, ओ मरि जाइए।
pairs.e: Narayanji should read that book and decide if his book stands up to it. And if they have no conscience, then they should shamelessly take awards like Hirendra Jha/Kamal Mohan Jha or Mishra Chunoo—our best wishes.

pairs.m: साँझमे ओकरा शोकमे मौन जुलुस निकलै छै।
pairs.e: And if they don't listen to their conscience, they should come out of the quagmire.

pairs.m: से सुशीलक रचना कोनो रस सिद्धान्तपर आधारित नै अछि, ओइमे तत्कालीन चिन्ता छै, ऐतिहासिक नाटकमे सेहो तत्कालीन सामाजिक विमर्श घोसिआयल छै।
pairs.e: [Videha Issue 397 Editorial] [Both Michel Foucault's "Disciplinary Institution" and the psychological concept of "gaslighting" by Barton and Whitehead have the same target.

pairs.m: आ तेँ सुशीलकेँ मार्क्सवादक सोंगरक खगता नै भेलन्हि, मुदा हड़ताल कथा ओ लिखि जाइत छथि।
pairs.e: Foucault's "Disciplinary Institution" is about bringing the targeted person into discipline, pitting everyone against each other, rewarding some, and gradually poisoning those who do not comply. In gaslighting, the target is made to believe that their opposition to the status quo is not opposition, that everyone is doing it, and that they are just opposing for the sake of opposition to hide their own shortcomings.

pairs.m: सुशीलकेँ देशमे बढ़ि रहल साम्प्रदायिक वातावरणक चिन्ता छन्हि जे मस्जिद कथाक अन्तमे ओ आनै छथि।
pairs.e: It also takes help from the basic shortcomings present in society, and gradually the target becomes disgraced or flees.

pairs.m: ओ संस्कृति आ भाषाक प्रति सचेत छथि।
pairs.e: All of Foucault's disciplinary institutions, like the Sahitya Akademi, Maithili Akademi, Maithili Bhojpuri Akademi, and the so-called literary associations recognized by the Sahitya Akademi, are with the mainstream.

pairs.m: परिवर्तनक प्रति सचेत छथि।
pairs.e: Attempts have been made for a long time to bring 'Sagar Raati Deep Jare' under Foucault's defined disciplinary institution.] There is no melodrama in Sushil's plays, stories, or novels.

pairs.m: चारू दिसुका प्रकृतिक प्रति सचेत छथि, बघोतियामे सेहो आ उपन्यासमे सेहो।
pairs.e: Where there is a happy ending, there is no melodrama; where there is a sad ending, there is no melodrama either; there is satire.

pairs.m: मुदा मनुक्ख हुनकर केन्द्रमे छन्हि।
pairs.e: Like in the story 'Sadak': "Well, half a life has already passed." Like in the story 'Haḍtāl': "A pipe gun bullet hits him; he dies. In the evening, a silent procession is taken out in his mourning."

pairs.m: अध्याय २ सिद्धान्तोत्तर (पोस्ट-थ्योरी) उत्तर-विखण्डनवादमे पोस्ट-थ्योरी (सिद्धान्तोत्तर) आयल।
pairs.e: Thus, Sushil's work is not based on any rasa theory; it contains contemporary concerns; even in historical plays, contemporary social discourse is embedded. So, Sushil did not need the cloak of Marxism, yet he wrote the story 'Haḍtāl'.

pairs.m: बीसम शताब्दीक शुरुहेमे रूसमे रूपवाद शुरू भेल।
pairs.e: Sushil is concerned about the growing communal atmosphere in the country, which he brings out in the end of the story 'Masjid'.

pairs.m: ओ लोकनि ऐ विश्वासकेँ अस्वीकार करैत छथि जे लेखकक विश्वदृष्टिक अभिव्यक्ति साहित्यक रचना छल, वरन् ओ मानैत छथि जे ई अछि एकटा कानून शासित प्रणाली।
pairs.e: He is conscious of culture and language. He is conscious of change.

pairs.m: हुनका सभक लेल शिल्प आ रूप कथावस्तुसँ बेशी महत्व रखैत अछि।
pairs.e: He is conscious of nature on all four sides, as seen in 'Baghotiya' and also in his novels.

pairs.m: ओ सभ मनोवैज्ञानिक समालोचना आ लेखकक जीवनी बा भोगल यथार्थक आधारपर रचना लिखल जाइ छै, ऐ दुनू धारणाकेँ नै मानैत छथि।
pairs.e: But humans are at his center.

pairs.m: ओ साहित्यक प्रति वैज्ञानिक दृष्टिकोणक ओकालत करैत छथि आ मानैत छथि जे साहित्यिक भाषा सामान्य भाषासँ अलग होइत अछि, ओकर अलग स्वतंत्र अस्तित्व होइ छै आ ओकर विश्लेषण स्वतंत्ररूपसँ हेबाक चाही नै कि लेखकक जीवनमे ई भेलै, ओ भेलै; बा मनोविश्लेषणक आधारपर माने ऐ साहित्यमे हैमलेट अपन कक्काक हत्या करैमे हिचकिचाइत अछि मुदा अनकर हत्या करैमे ओ नै हिचकिचाइत अछि कारण ओ स्वयं पिताक हत्या करऽ चाहैत छल आ कक्काक हत्या केनाइ ओकरा अपन हत्या केनाइ सन लगैत छै (फ्रायड); ऐ तरहक मनोविश्लेषणक आधारपर नै वरन् साहित्यिक भाषाक जे पाठ छै, तकर आधारपर एकर विश्लेषण हेबाक चाही।
pairs.e: Chapter 2 Post-Theory After post-deconstruction, post-theory emerged. Formalism began in Russia in the early 20th century. They rejected the belief that literary creation is an expression of the author's worldview; rather, they believed it is a system governed by rules.

pairs.m: फ्रांसक अल्बर्ट कामू जीवनक अयुक्तता, जइमे संकट आ भ्रम चारूकात पसरल छै, मे आस्थाक सोंगर लऽ लऽ चलैबलाकेँ “दार्शनिक आत्महत्या” केनिहार कहै छथि।
pairs.e: For them, craft and form were more important than subject matter.

pairs.m: (१९४२) सुशीलक साहित्यमे अहाँकेँ कत्तौ सुशील द्वारा ऐ तरहक दार्शनिक आत्महत्या नै भेटत।
pairs.e: They did not accept the notions that literature is written based on psychological criticism, the author's biography, or experienced reality.

pairs.m: एकटा कलाकारक आत्महत्यामे सेहो नै।
pairs.e: They criticized a scientific approach to literature and believed that literary language is different from ordinary language; it has a separate, independent existence, and its analysis should be done independently, not based on what happened in the author's life, nor on psychoanalysis like Hamlet hesitates to kill his uncle but not others because he wanted to kill his father himself and killing his uncle seems like killing himself (Freud). Analysis should be based on the actual text of literary language.

pairs.m: फ्रांसक दार्शनिक रोलेण्ड बाथ्स साहित्य रचनाक बादक स्थितिकेँ लेखकक मृत्यु कहै छथि, आ कहै छथि जे पाठ लेखकक की उद्देश्य रहै ओइसँ नै वरन् पाठक ओकर की व्याख्या करैए, तइसँ निर्धारित होइए।
pairs.e: France's Albert Camus (1942) calls those who wander holding onto faith amidst the absurdity of life, where crisis and illusion are everywhere, as committing "philosophical suicide."

pairs.m: (१९६७) फ्रांसिस फुकुयामा कहै छथि जे पश्चिमक उदार-गणतंत्र विरोधी विचारधारा (माने साम्यवाद आ गणतंत्र) क संघर्षसँ निकलल अन्तिम विकासक्रम अछि, आ किएक तँ आब एक्केटा विचारधारा अछि तेँ ई इतिहासक अन्त अछि।
pairs.e: In Sushil's literature, you will never find such philosophical suicide by Sushil, not even in 'Ekta Kalakar Ki Atmahatyā'.

pairs.m: (१९९२) पाठककेँ कोनो रचना नीक लगै छै आ कोनो रचना नीक नै लगै छै, आ तेँ साहित्यक समीक्षा पाठक करत कोनो वाद नै।
pairs.e: French philosopher Roland Barthes (1967) calls the situation after the creation of literature the "death of the author," and says the text is determined not by the author's intention but by how the reader interprets it. Francis Fukuyama (1992) says that Western liberal democracy is the final form of evolution emerging from the struggle of opposing ideologies (like communism and democracy), and since there is now only one ideology, it is the end of history.

pairs.m: नव-नव सिद्धान्त पुरनके सिद्धान्त सभक नूतन रूप अछि, नव ढ़ंगसँ पुरनके सिद्धान्त सभक रिमिक्सिंग आ रिपैकेजिंग कएल जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: Some works please a reader, others do not; thus, literary criticism is done by the reader, not by any school.

pairs.m: रचनाक पाठ मात्र महत्त्वपूर्ण अछि, ओइले कोनो वाद नै चाही।
pairs.e: New theories are just new versions of old ones, remixing and repackaging old theories.

pairs.m: आ ऐ तरहे तार्किक प्रत्यक्षवाद आ नव तार्किक प्रत्यक्षवाद क दृष्टिकोण दिस पोस्ट-थ्योरी आयल अछि।
pairs.e: The text of the work is the only important thing; no theory is needed for it. Thus, post-theory has moved towards the perspectives of logical positivism and new logical positivism.

pairs.m: सुशीलक रचनाक समीक्षा अही पोस्ट-थ्योरीसँ सम्भव भेल अछि, ओना नारीवाद आ मनोवैज्ञानिक विश्लेषणक समीचीनता पोस्ट-थ्योरीक अनुरूप सेहो आबि गेल अछि।
pairs.e: A critique of Sushil's work has been possible through this post-theory, although the relevance of feminism and psychoanalytic analysis has also aligned with post-theory.

pairs.m: नित नवल सुभाष चन्द्र यादव (राइटर्स ब्लॉक- मैथिलीक समानान्तर धाराक लेखन- सुभाष चन्द्र यादवक समस्त साहित्य) सुभाषचन्द्र यादव (१९४८- ), जन्म ०५ मार्च १९४८, मातृक दीवानगंज, सुपौलमे।
pairs.e: Nit Naval Subhash Chandra Yadav (Writer's Block - The parallel stream of Maithili literature - The complete literature of Subhash Chandra Yadav) Subhashchandra Yadav (1948- ), born 05 March 1948, maternal place Diwangunj, Supaul.

pairs.m: पैतृक स्थान: बलबा-मेनाही, सुपौल।
pairs.e: Ancestral place: Balba-Menahi, Supaul.

pairs.m: मूल मैथिली लेखन पहिल चरण: घरदेखिया (१९८३) मूल मैथिली लेखन दोसर चरण: बनैत बिगड़ैत (विदेह ई-पत्रिकामे २००८ सँ २००९ धरि, फेर पुस्तकाकार २००९ मे), गुलो (मिथिला दर्शनमे प्रकाशन, २०१४, फेर पुस्तकाकार २०१५ मे), रमता जोगी (२०१९), मडर (२०२१), राजकमल चौधरी मोनोग्राफ (जनवरी १९९७ मे साहित्य अकादेमी द्वारा रिजेक्ट, रचना पत्रिकामे दिसम्बर २००५- मार्च २००६ अंकमे आ पुस्तकाकार २०२२ मे "नित नवल राजकमल" नामसँ), भोट (२०२२)।
pairs.e: Original Maithili writing first phase: Ghardekhiya (1983) Original Maithili writing second phase: Banait Bigadait (in Videha e-magazine from 2008 to 2009, then in book form in 2009), Gulo (published in Mithila Darshan, 2014, then in book form in 2015), Ramta Jogi (2019), Madar (2021), Rajkamal Chaudhary Monograph (rejected by Sahitya Akademi in January 1997, in Rachna magazine December 2005-March 2006 issue and in book form in 2022 under the name "Nit Naval Rajkamal"), Bhot (2022).

pairs.m: "जे सुनबैए खिस्सा से राज करैए ऐ संसारपर"- होपी अमेरिकी कबीलाक लोकोक्ति।
pairs.e: "Those who listen to stories rule this world" - Hopi American tribe proverb.

pairs.m: आ सुभाष चन्द्र यादव खिस्सा सुनेनाइ छोड़ि देलन्हि।
pairs.e: And Subhash Chandra Yadav did not stop listening to stories.

pairs.m: "स्वप्नसुन्दरी एण्ड द मैजिकल बर्ड्स ऑफ मिथिला" (गीता धर्मराजन, कथा, १९९६) मिथिलाक टुनमुनिया राजकुमारी स्वप्नसुन्दरीकेँ गाबैबला चिड़ै सभ बड्ड पसिन्न छलै।
pairs.e: "Swapnasundari and the Magical Birds of Mithila" (Geeta Dharmarajan, Katha, 1996) The singing birds of Swapnasundari, the little princess of Mithila, were very pleased. But she would turn even fish, goats, and cows into birds.

pairs.m: मुदा ओ माछ, बकड़ी आ गाय सभकेँ सेहो चिड़ै बना दैत छली।
pairs.e: The subjects complained about the princess, the princess got angry and stopped making birds.

pairs.m: प्रजा सभ राजकुमारीकेँ उपराग देलक, राजकुमारी रूसि गेली आ चिड़ै बनेनाइ बन्न कऽ देलनि।
pairs.e: She would not come out of the palace either.

pairs.m: ओ महलसँ बाहरो नै अबै छली।
pairs.e: The remaining birds from the magical land of Mithila also disappeared. The farmers were still ploughing the fields, but without the colourful birds, this Mithila did not seem like that Mithila anymore.

pairs.m: जादूबला देश मिथिलासँ जे बाँचल चिड़ै छल सेहो निपत्ता भऽ गेल।
pairs.e: Then came the timber merchants.

pairs.m: जनीजाति खेतमे अखनो गबैत छली, मुदा बिनु रङ-बिरङक चिड़ैक ई मिथिला आब ओ मिथिला नै लगैत छल।
pairs.e: What is the use of a tree without birds?

pairs.m: आ फेर आयल गाछक पैकार सभ, बिनु चिड़ै गाछक कोन काज, ई गप लोक सभकेँ बुझा, ठकि कऽ ओ सभ मिथिलाक सभटा गाछकेँ काटि कऽ लऽ गेल।
pairs.e: They convinced the people, cut down all the trees of Mithila and took them away.

pairs.m: जनीजाति सभ, सभटा जुगति भिड़ेलनि जे किछु गाछ बचि जाय, मुदा से भऽ नै सकल।
pairs.e: The farmers tried all kinds of tricks to save some trees, but they could not succeed. Without trees, all the streams dried up. Without trees, the people became poor, and the timber merchants became rich.

pairs.m: बिनु गाछक सभटा धार सुखा गेल।
pairs.e: The farmers went to the princess, pleading and saying, Princess, please help us, we were wrong.

pairs.m: बिनु गाछक लोक सभ होइत गेल गरीब आ पैकार सभ होइत गेल धनीक।
pairs.e: Swapnasundari sat in her room, where on all four walls were paintings of colourful birds.

pairs.m: जनीजाति सभ गेलीह राजकुमारी लग, कनैत, कहैत जे राजकुमारी, मदति करू, हम सभ गलत छलौं।
pairs.e: Looking at those bird paintings, the princess of Mithila, sad, said – No, you all were right.

pairs.m: स्वप्नसुन्दरी बैसल छली अपन कोठलीमे, जतऽ चारूकात देबालपर छल रङ बिरङक चिड़ै सभक चित्र।
pairs.e: Birds alone cannot keep us happy.

pairs.m: ओइ चिड़ैक चित्र सभकेँ देखैत मिथिलाक राजकुमारी स्वप्नसुन्दरी उदास भऽ बजली- नै, अहीं सभ ठीक छलौं।
pairs.e: But the farmers said – Without birds, we too cannot remain happy, bring those birds back, princess.

pairs.m: खाली चिड़ै हमरा सभकेँ प्रसन्न नै राखि सकैए।
pairs.e: But the princess was so sad that she could not perform magic.

pairs.m: मुदा जनीजाति सभ बजली- बिनु चिड़ै सेहो हम सभ प्रसन्न नै रहि सकब, ओ चिड़ै सभ घुरा कऽ आनि दिअ राजकुमारी।
pairs.e: Then the farmers said – Don't worry, we will plant trees, and the singing birds will return.

pairs.m: मुदा राजकुमारी ततेक उदास छली जे ओ जादू नै कऽ सकली।
pairs.e: And this time, we will not let anyone cheat us.

pairs.m: तखन जनी-जाति सभ कहलन्हि- चिन्ता नै, हम सभ गाछ रोपब आ गाबैबला चिड़ै सभ घुरि आओत।
pairs.e: And the entire Mithila began to stir as if a drought had broken.

pairs.m: आ ऐबेर ककरो बेइमानी सेहो हमरा सभ नै करऽ देबै।
pairs.e: No one had any respite, and they started planting trees, singing and dancing, and continued planting.

pairs.m: आ बताह सन खटऽ लागल सम्पूर्ण मिथिला।
pairs.e: And as the trees grew and went around, everyone started laughing and began to pat each other on the back.

pairs.m: ककरो कोनो पलखति नै, आ ओ सभ गाबि कऽ नाचि कऽ रोपऽ लागल आ पटबऽ लागल गाछ।
pairs.e: Working together made them happy.

pairs.m: आ गाछ जेना-जेना बढ़ऽ लागल आ चकरगर होइत गेल सभ हँसऽ लगला, आ थोपड़ी पारऽ लगला।
pairs.e: And because of this, the trees also grew well and quickly.

pairs.m: संग मिलि कऽ काज केलासँ हुनका सभकेँ खुशी होइन।
pairs.e: And in the land of Princess Swapnasundari, Mithila, small and big trees began to smile everywhere.

pairs.m: आ ऐ सँ गाछो सभ नीकसँ आ जल्दीसँ बढ़ऽ लागल।
pairs.e: Magical music spread in all four directions.

pairs.m: आ स्वप्नसुन्दरीक देश मिथिलामे छोट-पैघ गाछ सभ मुस्की देमऽ लागल चारू कात।
pairs.e: Birds of seven colors – red, vermilion, sky-blue, yellow, pink, and water-soaked like – were making their nests.

pairs.m: जादूबला संगीत चारू दिस पसरि गेल।
pairs.e: The princess said to the farmers – Our children go to school, this has been our dream for a long time.

pairs.m: लाल रङक, जोमक रङक, अकासी, पीअर, गुलाबी आ पनिसोखा सन सात रङक चिड़ै सभक डेरा बनत ई।
pairs.e: The farmers said – Yes princess, if we had been educated, no timber merchant could have cheated us.

pairs.m: राजकुमारी कहलनि जनीजाति सभसँ- हमर देशक बचियासभ पाठशाला जाथि, ई हमर सभ दिन सँ सपना अछि।
pairs.e: But then came a dilemma.

pairs.m: जनीजाति सभ बजली- हँ राजकुमारी, जँ हम सभ पढ़ल लिखल रहितौं तँ कोनो पैकार हमरा सभकेँ ठकि नै सकितय।
pairs.e: Some among the farmers said – But then who will do the household work?

pairs.m: मुदा आयल एकटा झमेल।
pairs.e: Then the princess of Mithila ordered – Children should learn the work of girls, and girls should learn the work of boys.

pairs.m: जनीजातिमे सँ किछु कहलनि- मुदा तखन घरक काज के करत?
pairs.e: Everyone likes moving forward together.

pairs.m: तखन मिथिलाक राजकुमारी आदेश देलनि- बालक सभ बालिकाक काज सीखत आ बालिका सभ बालकक काज।
pairs.e: And Swapnasundari gave a bicycle to every girl who went to school.

pairs.m: मिलिये-जुलि कऽ आगाँ बढ़ैमे सभकेँ नीक लागत।
pairs.e: Swapnasundari understood – In the world, those who can move quickly from one place to another are happy, because that way they finish all their work quickly.

pairs.m: आ स्वप्न सुन्दरी सभ पढ़ैबाली बालिकाकेँ देलन्हि एकटा साइकिल।
pairs.e: The farmers asked – Can't we ride a bicycle?

pairs.m: स्वप्न सुन्दरी बुझेलखिन्ह- संसार भरिमे ओ सभ प्रसन्न छथि जे एक-ठामसँ दोसर ठाम जल्दी पहुँचि जाइ छथि, कारण तइसँ ओ सभ सभटा काज जल्दी-जल्दी पूरा कऽ लै छथि।
pairs.e: The princess said – Why not?

pairs.m: जनीजाति सभ पुछलन्हि- की हम सभ साइकिल नै चला सकै छी?
pairs.e: Women should do the work they like.

pairs.m: राजकुमारी कहलन्हि- किए नै?
pairs.e: A few asked the princess – But where does money come from?

pairs.m: महिलाकेँ ओ सभ काज करबाक चाही जे ओकरा नीक लगै छै।
pairs.e: But everyone hushed them.

pairs.m: किछु गोटे राजकुमारीसँ पुछलन्हि- मुदा टाका कतऽ सँ आओत?
pairs.e: Does the princess do all the work?

pairs.m: मुदा सभ ओकरा दबाड़ि देलक।
pairs.e: And everyone set up a big fair.

pairs.m: सभटा काज राजकुमारिये करती?
pairs.e: Money was collected, and Princess Swapnasundari's school was built.

pairs.m: आ लगेलक सभ एकटा बड़का मेला।
pairs.e: The school performs magic, there children go and become like colourful birds.

pairs.m: पाइ जमा भेल, आ बनल राजकुमारी स्वप्नसुन्दरीक पाठशाला।
pairs.e: Why don't you come to the princess's school?

pairs.m: पाठशाला करैए जादू, ओतऽ बच्चा सभ जा कऽ बनि जाइत अछि जेना होथि रङ-बिरङक चिड़ै ।
pairs.e: The princess came to school one day, came the next day, and started coming every day.

pairs.m: अहूँ किए नै अबै छी पाठशाला राजकुमारी?
pairs.e: And then she said that the parents of the children should also come to school.

pairs.m: राजकुमारी एक दिन पाठशाला एली, दोसर दिन एली आ सभ दिन आबय लगली।
pairs.e: And then the princess started doing magic again.

pairs.m: आ फेर ओ कहलनि जे बच्चा सभक माय बाबू सेहो आबथु पाठशाला।
pairs.e: But this time, she started turning only useless things into birds.

pairs.m: आ फेर राजकुमारी फेरसँ करऽ लगली जादू।
pairs.e: And many colorful birds have also returned to Mithila, without any magic.

pairs.m: मुद ऐबेर ओ खाली बेकार चीज सभकेँ बनबऽ लगली चिड़ै।
pairs.e: Everyone has taken one thousand books each in their lap, books that bring joy, red, pink, and sky-blue.

pairs.m: आ बहुत रास कएक रङक पाँखिबला चिड़ै सभ सेहो घुरि आयल अछि मिथिलामे, बिनु जादू कयने।
pairs.e: Red, vermilion, yellow, pink, and water-soaked like – seven colors, because the sandy place has been taken over by green lush trees.

pairs.m: सभ अपना लोलमे एक-एक हजार पोथी लेने, उल्लास आनैबला पोथी सभ, लाल, गुलाबी आ अकासी रङक।
pairs.e: And the birds are singing – Without knowledge, beauty is like a dried stream.

pairs.m: लाल, जोमक रङक, पीअर, गुलाबी आ पनिसोखा सन सात रङक, कारण रेतक स्थान लऽ लेलक अछि हरियर कचोर गाछ सभ।
pairs.e: And the Mithila of that little princess became the greenest and most cheerful country in the world.

pairs.m: आ चिड़ै सभ गाबि रहल अछि- बिनु ज्ञान सुन्दरता अछि सुखायल धार सन।
pairs.e: People even say that because of these birds, there is magic in Mithila's environment.

pairs.m: आ ओइ टुनमुनिया राजकुमारीक मिथिला बनि गेल विश्वक सभसँ बेशी हरियर आ प्रफुल्लित देश।
pairs.e: And if we too happen to find such a place, where men and women can think big thoughts!

pairs.m: लोक सभ तँ ईहो कहैत छथि जे अही चिड़ै सभक कारण मिथिलाक हबामे रहैत अछि जादू सदिखन।
pairs.e: Is this magical place near us?

pairs.m: आ जँ कहियो हमरो सभकेँ भेटि जाय एकटा एहेन स्थान, जतय महिला आ पुरुख सोचि सकथि पैघ-पैघ गप!
pairs.e: Isn't this place, where magical things exist, inside us ordinary people?

pairs.m: ई जादूबला स्थान हमरा सभक लगे-पासमे तँ नै?
pairs.e: If you find this place, wherever it is, then inform us at our e-mail address editorial.

pairs.m: ई स्थान जतऽ जादूबला बौस्तु सभ अछि, हमरे सन साधारण लोकक भितरे तँ नै अछि?
pairs.e: staff.

pairs.m: जँ अहाँकेँ भेटय ई स्थान, जतऽ कतौऽ, तँ सूचित करू हमरा हमर ई-पत्र सङ्केत editorial.
pairs.e: videha@gmail.

pairs.m: com पर।
pairs.e: But he remained stubborn since 1983, some so-called lower-caste writers also went along with the imaginary spelling of Maithili and left him, they became victims of gaslighting.

pairs.m: सुभाष चन्द्र यादव खिस्सा सुनेनाइ छोड़ि देलन्हि।
pairs.e: The timber merchants started pressuring from all sides.

pairs.m: मुदा ओ १९८३ सँ अड़ल रहलाह असगरे, आइक तथाकथित निम्न वर्गक किछु लेखक सभ सेहो मैथिलीक आभासी वर्तनीक सङ्ग चलि गेला आ छोड़ि देलखिन्ह असगर हुनका, ओ सभ गैसलाइटिंगक शिकार भऽ गेला।
pairs.e: Like that little princess, he kept watching.

pairs.m: नचिकेता २५ बर्खक मौनभंगक बाद विदेह पाक्षिक ई-पत्रिका मे नो एण्ट्री: मा प्रविश प्रकाशित करय लगलाह आ ओही बर्ख २५ बर्खक बाद सुभाष चन्द्र यादव विदेहक पाठककेँ खिस्सा सुनबय लगलाह पक्ष-दर-पक्ष।
pairs.e: But from 01.01.2008, after 8 years of preparation, Videha – Maithili e-magazine started planting trees, in Mithila, side by side, and colourful birds started returning.

pairs.m: सुभाष चन्द्र यादवक घरदेखिया आयल १९८३ मे आ २५ बर्खक बाद "बनैत बिगड़ैत" २००८-०९ मे विदेहमे ई-प्रकाशित भेलाक बाद २००९ मे प्रिण्टमे सेहो आबि गेल।
pairs.e: After 25 years of breaking his silence, Nachiketa started publishing "No Entry: Ma Pravesh" in Videha fortnightly e-magazine, and in that same year, after 25 years, Subhash Chandra Yadav started telling stories to Videha readers, side by side.

pairs.m: "जे सुनायत खिस्सा सएह राज करत ऐ संसारपर" आ समानान्तर धारा सुना रहल अछि खिस्सा।
pairs.e: Subhash Chandra Yadav's Ghardekhiya came in 1983, and after 25 years, "Banait Bigadait" was e-published in Videha in 2008-09 and then came in print in 2009.

pairs.m: मिथिलाक कएक रङगक पाँखिबला चिड़ै सभ घुरि आयल जेना सन्दीप कुमार साफी, उमेश पासवान, बेचन ठाकुर, कपिलेश्वर राउत, उमेश मण्डल, राम विलास साहु, राजदेव मण्डल, नन्द विलास राय, जगदीश प्रसाद मण्डल, दुर्गानन्द मण्डल, आचार्य रामानन्द मण्डल, ललन कुमार कामत, नारायण यादव, मुन्नी कामत, शिव कुमार प्रसाद, धीरेन्द्र कुमार, रामदेव प्रसाद मण्डल "झारूदार" एला रङ बिरङक कथा आ गीतक सङ।
pairs.e: "Those who tell stories rule this world" and the parallel stream is telling stories.

pairs.m: घुरि आयल अछि सभ अपना लोलमे एक-एक हजार पोथी लेने, उल्लास आनैबला पोथी सभ, लाल, गुलाबी, जोमक, पनिसोखाक सात रङक आ अकासी रङक।
pairs.e: Colourful birds of Mithila have returned like Sandeep Kumar Saffi, Umesh Paswan, Bechen Thakur, Kapileshwar Raut, Umesh Mandal, Ram Vilas Sahu, Rajdev Mandal, Nand Vilas Ray, Jagdish Prasad Mandal, Durganand Mandal, Acharya Ramanand Mandal, Lalan Kumar Kamat, Narayan Yadav, Munni Kamat, Shiv Kumar Prasad, Dhirendra Kumar, Ramdev Prasad Mandal "Jharudar" have come with colourful stories and songs.

pairs.m: कारण रेतक स्थान लऽ लेलक अछि हरियर कचोर गाछ सभ।
pairs.e: Everyone has taken one thousand books each in their lap, books that bring joy, red, pink, vermilion, water-soaked seven colors, and sky-blue.

pairs.m: आ सुभाष चन्द्र यादव खिस्सा सुनेनाइ फेरसँ शुरू कऽ देलन्हि।
pairs.e: Because the sandy place has been taken over by green lush trees.

pairs.m: बनैत बिगड़ैतक बाद आयल रमता जोगी, गुलो, मडर, भोट।
pairs.e: And Subhash Chandra Yadav started telling stories again.

pairs.m: साहित्य अकादेमी द्वार रिजेक्ट कएल राजकमल चौधरी मोनोग्राफ आयल “नित नवल राजकमल” नामसँ।
pairs.e: After Banait Bigadait came Ramta Jogi, Gulo, Madar, Bhot.

pairs.m: आ तेँ सुभाष चन्द्र यादव छथि “नित नवल सुभाष चन्द्र यादव”।
pairs.e: The Rajkamal Chaudhary Monograph rejected by Sahitya Akademi came under the name "Nit Naval Rajkamal".

pairs.m: मैथिली स्टोरी साइंस (मैथिला कथाशास्त्र) आ सुभाष चन्द्र यादव मिशेल फोको (Foucault)क "अनुशासन संस्था" बा मनोवैज्ञानिक बार्टन आ ह्वाइटहेडक "गैसलाइटिंग" दुनूक लक्ष्य एक्के छै।
pairs.e: And thus Subhash Chandra Yadav is "Nit Naval Subhash Chandra Yadav".

pairs.m: मिशेल फोकोक "अनुशासन संस्था" अछि, सोझाँबलाकेँ अनुशासनमे आनू आ तइ लेल सभकेँ आपसेमे लड़ाउ, किछुकेँ पुरस्कृत करू आ जे अनुशासनमे नै अबैए तकरा आस्ते-आस्ते माहुर दियौ।
pairs.e: Maithili Story Science (Maithila Kathashastra) and Subhash Chandra Yadav The target of Michel Foucault's "Disciplinary Institution" and the psychological "Gaslighting" of Barton and Whitehead is the same.

pairs.m: गैसलाइटिंगमे सोझाँबला केँ विश्वास दिआओल जाइत अछि जे अहाँ जे यथास्थितिक विरोध कऽ रहल छी से कोनो विरोध नै, ई तँ सभ कऽ रहल अछि, अहाँ तँ विरोधक नामपर विरोध कऽ रहल छी आ से अपन कमी नुकेबा लेल।
pairs.e: Michel Foucault's "Disciplinary Institution" is to bring the straightforward person into discipline and for that, make everyone fight among themselves, reward some, and gradually marginalize those who do not come into discipline.

pairs.m: ऐमे समाजमे वर्तमान आधारभूत कमीक सहायता सेहो लेल जाइत अछि, आ आस्ते-आस्ते टारगेट बताह भऽ जाइत अछि बा पलायन कऽ जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: In gaslighting, the straightforward person is made to believe that what you are opposing is not opposition, everyone is doing it, you are opposing just for the sake of opposition to hide your own shortcomings.

pairs.m: से सुभाष चन्द्र यादवक राइटर्स-ब्लॉक सामान्य राइटर्स ब्लॉक नै छल, ई छल "गैसलाइटिंग"।
pairs.e: For this, the existing basic flaws in society are also utilized, and gradually the target becomes confused or flees.
