pairs.m: कएक टा कथा मे सुभाष अबै छथि आ एकटा कथामे सुभाष, महाप्रकाश आ सुकान्त तीनू [सहरसा दुपहर राति (१९७२)]।
pairs.e: He had read in the letter.

pairs.m: नित नवल राजकमल (राजकमल मोनोग्राफ) [राजकमल चौधरी मोनोग्राफ (जनवरी १९९७ मे साहित्य अकादेमी द्वारा रिजेक्ट, रचना पत्रिकामे दिसम्बर २००५- मार्च २००६ अंकमे आ २०२२ मे "नित नवल राजकमल" नामसँ प्रकाशित)] राजकमल जी (विनिबंध) क प्रकरण कान मे पडल तँ छल, से कतोक वर्ख भऽ गेलै आब।
pairs.e: But there, the engineer is playing with the baby.

pairs.m: मुदा से एहन कुरूप छैक से अहींक समादमे स्पष्ट भेलय।
pairs.e: The engineer works at the Lamp Works.

pairs.m: तें एकर धन्यवाद अहीं केँ दैत छी गजेन्द्र जी।
pairs.e: Kishori Bhai bids farewell handing a sixty-watt bulb to the protagonist, but after going ahead, the protagonist learns that it is fused; he told him to bring a replacement.

pairs.m: ओना वास्तविकता तँ ई जे सम्पूर्ण पढबासँ पहिने मोन ’विरक्त’ भऽ गेल | नै पढ़ि भेल आगाँ!
pairs.e: Look at the themes of the stories in Ghardekhiya, look at the sketch of Badariya (became like a log, no matter how much you beat him, not a single response comes out, eyes sunken, all the bones of the chest visible, back hurts, doesn't play, looks blank as if thinking about something), stories about extramarital relations, the protagonist's poverty, his poverty-stricken life.

pairs.m: नीक केलौहेँ नेट पर दऽ कऽ।
pairs.e: In several stories, Subhash appears, and in one story, all three – Subhash, Mahaprakash, and Sukant [Saharsa Dupahar Raati (1972)].

pairs.m: समकालीन आ आगत पीढ़ी सेहो बुझओ ई कारी-कथा!
pairs.e: □ Nit Naval Rajkamal (Rajkamal Monograph) [Rajkamal Chaudhary Monograph (rejected by Sahitya Akademi in January 1997, in Rachna magazine December 2005-March 2006 issue, and published in 2022 under the name "Nit Naval Rajkamal")] I had heard about the matter concerning Rajkamal Ji (monograph), it has been many years now.

pairs.m: हमरा सन लोकक विडम्बना देखू जे पूरा प्रकरण अपन अनुज-मित्र-अग्रज सँ जुडल अछि।
pairs.e: But it is so ugly that it became clear in your message.

pairs.m: से एहन ऐतिहासिक दुर्घटना भऽ गेल अछि!
pairs.e: So for this, I thank you, Gajendra Ji.

pairs.m: उत्तरदायी व्यक्तिगत हम कतहु सँ नै।
pairs.e: Actually, the truth is that even before reading the whole thing, my mind became 'disgusted'.

pairs.m: मुदा साहित्यिक पीढ़ीक नैतिकता सँ "अपराध बोध" सहबा लेल अभिशप्त छी।
pairs.e: Went ahead without reading!

pairs.m: उपाय?
pairs.e: You did well by putting it on the net.

pairs.m: - सस्नेह, गंगेश गुंजन ११ सितम्बर २०१२ (विदेह ११७) राजकमल मोनोग्राफ साहित्य अकादेमीक मैथिली विभाग द्वारा मोहन भारद्वाजक किरदानी आ राजमोहन झा केर सहयोगसँ अस्वीकृत भेल छल सन् जनवरी १९९७ ई मे आ तइ कृत्य लेल राजमोहन झाकेँ साहित्य अकादेमी द्वारा १९९६ ई.
pairs.e: The contemporary and future generations will also understand this dark story!

pairs.m: क मैथिलीक मूल पुरस्कारसँ पुरस्कृत कएल गेल सन् १९९७ मे।
pairs.e: Look at the irony of people like us that the entire matter is connected to my younger brother, friend, and elder.

pairs.m: संगहि ओही कृत्यक सम्पादन लेल मोहन भारद्वाज साहित्य अकादेमीक मैथिली परामर्शदातृ समितिक सदस्य बनाओल गेले छलाह।
pairs.e: It has become such a historical accident!

pairs.m: नित नवल राजकमल (राजकमलक जीवन) समर्पित अछि- धर्मप्राण संतोष कुमार कुन्दन सुव्रता गिरिवर कृष्ण राधे राधे ।
pairs.e: We are not personally responsible from any side.

pairs.m: नित नवल राजकमल - दन्तकथाक नायक राजकमल चौधरी (मणीन्द्र नारायण चौधरी प्रसिद्ध फूल बाबू) १९२९-१९६७, महिषी, सहरसा।
pairs.e: But the literary generation is cursed to bear "guilt" with morality.

pairs.m: रचना- आदि कथा, आन्दोलन, पाथर फूल (उपन्यास), स्वरगंधा (कविता संग्रह), ललका पाग (कथा संग्रह), कथा पराग (कथा संग्रह सम्पादन)।
pairs.e: Solution?

pairs.m: भूकम्प, बंगाली संन्यासिन आ रासलीलाक चित्र राजकमलक आत्मवक्तव्यपर आधारित अछि।
pairs.e: – With affection, Gangesh Gunjan 11 September 2012 (Videha 117) The Rajkamal monograph was rejected by the Maithili department of Sahitya Akademi with the help of Mohan Bhardwaj and Rajmohan Jha in January 1997, and for this act, Rajmohan Jha was awarded the 1996 Maithili Sahitya Akademi Award in 1997.

pairs.m: १९३४क भूकम्पक स्मृति, धरती कड़कड़ायल आ हुनकर माय बाबू अपन जानक चिन्तामे लागि गेला हिनका छोड़ि देलखिन्ह।
pairs.e: At the same time, to edit that act, Mohan Bhardwaj was made a member of the Sahitya Akademi's Maithili Advisory Committee.

pairs.m: फेर ओइ भूकम्पक थोड़बे दिन बाद आयलि एकटा बंगाली संन्यासिन, कोनो तीर्थमे हुनकर माँ सँ ओइ संन्यासिनक भेँट छलनि।
pairs.e: Nit Naval Rajkamal (Life of Rajkamal) is dedicated to – Dharmapran Santosh Kumar Kundan Suvrata Girivar Krishn Radhe Radhe.

pairs.m: हुनका ओ अपना संगे चलै ले कहलखिन्ह आ ओहो जाय चाहैत छला।
pairs.e: Nit Naval Rajkamal - The Hero of Legend Rajkamal Chaudhary (Manindra Narayan Chaudhary, famous as Phool Babu) 1929-1967, Mahishi, Saharsa.

pairs.m: एकटा रासलीलाक चित्र देखलन्हि कृष्ण आ कएकटा गोपी।
pairs.e: Works – Aadi Katha, Andolan, Pathar Phool (novel), Swar Gandha (poetry collection), Lalka Pag (story collection), Katha Parag (edited story collection).

pairs.m: राजकमल ऐ सभ गपकेँ हवा देलन्हि।
pairs.e: The descriptions of earthquake, Bengali sannyasin, and Raslila are based on Rajkamal's self-statement.

pairs.m: सभ अपन व्यक्तित्व लेल/ विचारधारा लेल कोनो बहन्ना तकैत अछि।
pairs.e: Memories of the 1934 earthquake, the earth rumbled, and his parents, engrossed in their own concerns, abandoned him.

pairs.m: राजकमल ई मार्केटिंग स्ट्राटेजी सेहो छल।
pairs.e: Then a few days after that earthquake, a Bengali sannyasin came; his mother had met that sannyasin at some pilgrimage.

pairs.m: नित नवल राजकमल - जीवन कोनो खास नै, वएह पहिल पत्नीमे बच्चा नै, से दोसर विवाह (रामपुर हवेली राजकमलक माय) मायक मृत्यु आ पिताक दोसर फेर तेसर बियाह हिनकर उमेरक युवतीसँ ।
pairs.e: She asked him to come with her, and he too wanted to go.

pairs.m: राजकमल अपन पिता मधुसूदन चौधरीकेँ लाल कक्का कहै छला।
pairs.e: He saw a painting of a Raslila – Krishna and some Gopis.

pairs.m: तेसर विवाहक एकटा पुत्री मदालसा जीवित रहलखिन्ह, हुनकासँ राजकमलकेँ स्नेह छलन्हि।
pairs.e: Rajkamal exaggerated all these stories.

pairs.m: पिता आज्ञा, उपदेश आ मारिपीटक माध्यमसँ ब्राह्मण संस्कारमे दीक्षित केलखिन्ह।
pairs.e: Everyone is looking for some excuse for their own personality/ideology.

pairs.m: राजकमल केँ मैट्रिक पास करबासँ पूर्वे गीता आ दुर्गासप्तशतीक सभटा श्लोक कंठस्थ भऽ गेलन्हि।
pairs.e: This was also a marketing strategy for Rajkamal.

pairs.m: पिताक ट्रांसफरक संग एत्तऽ सँ ओत्तऽ घुमैत रहलथि, नवादासँ पटना एला बी.
pairs.e: Nit Naval Rajkamal - Life Nothing special.

pairs.m: एन.
pairs.e: First wife had no children, so second marriage (Rampur Haveli is Rajkamal's maternal home).

pairs.m: कॉलेजमे नामांकन, पिता ४४२ टा निषेध मंत्र देलखिन्ह जे की-की नै करिहऽ।
pairs.e: Mother's death and father's second and then third marriage with a young girl of his age.

pairs.m: प्रेम सम्बन्ध- पहिल प्रेम सम्बन्ध पटनामे शोभना झा संगे।
pairs.e: Rajkamal called his father Madhusudan Chaudhary "Lal Kakka".

pairs.m: एक बेर आइ.
pairs.e: From the third marriage, a daughter, Madalsa, survived; he had affection for her.

pairs.m: कॉममे फेल, फेर बी.
pairs.e: Father initiated him into Brahminical rites through orders, advice, and beatings.

pairs.m: कॉममे एक बेर फेल।
pairs.e: Even before passing matriculation, Rajkamal had memorized all the verses of the Gita and Durga Saptashati.

pairs.m: १९५१ मे चानपुरा (दरभंगा)क शशिकान्ता सँ विवाह।
pairs.e: He kept moving here and there with his father's transfer; enrolled at B.
N. College in Patna from Nawada; his father gave him 442 prohibition mantras about what not to do.

pairs.m: कॉम केर बाद पढ़ाइ समाप्त।
pairs.e: Love affairs – first love affair in Patna with Shobhana Jha.

pairs.m: विवाहक बाद किछु दिन पटनामे कोनो अखबारमे प्रूफरीडर, फेर सचिवालयमे लोअर डिवीजन क्लार्क।
pairs.e: Failed in I.

pairs.m: दरभंगा, पटना, दिल्ली, मसूरी, फेर कलकत्ता।
pairs.e: Com once, then failed in B.

pairs.m: ओतहियेसँ मसूरीक सावित्री शर्मासँ पत्राचार, १९५६मे हुनका संग विवाह, फेर संतोष नामक स्त्री (प्रायः सावित्रीक भतीजी) सँ प्रेम, गर्भावस्थामे सावित्रीकेँ छोड़ि ३ जुलाइ १९५७केँ मसूरीसँ पलायन।
pairs.e: Com once.

pairs.m: फेर पटना, दरभंगा आ नवादा तीन- चारि मास धरि आ तखन कलकत्ता, हिन्दी लेखक छेदीलाल गुप्तक नामे यात्रीक चिट्ठी लऽ कऽ।
pairs.e: Married Shashikanta of Chanpura (Darbhanga) in 1951.

pairs.m: बाबू साहेब चौधरीक मिथिला दर्शनसँ जुड़ि गेला।
pairs.e: After B.

pairs.m: फेर भारतीय ज्ञानपीठ (कलकत्ता), पत्नी संगे छलथिन्ह।
pairs.e: Com, education ended. After marriage, worked as a proofreader in some newspaper in Patna for a few days, then as a Lower Division Clerk in the secretariat.

pairs.m: फेर नोकरी छोड़ि रागरंग बहार केलन्हि मुदा ओ बन्द भऽ गेल।
pairs.e: Darbhanga, Patna, Delhi, Mussoorie, then Calcutta.

pairs.m: छओ बर्खक कलकत्ता प्रवासमे बहुत साहित्य लिखलनि।
pairs.e: From there, correspondence with Savitri Sharma of Mussoorie; married her in 1956; then fell in love with a woman named Santosh (probably Savitri's niece); abandoned Savitri, who was pregnant, on 3 July 1957 and fled from Mussoorie.

pairs.m: शरीर दुर्बल, पेट पर जे लम्प छलन्हि से कलकत्ते मे शुरू भेलन्हि।
pairs.e: Then Patna, Darbhanga, and Nawada for three or four months, then Calcutta, with a letter for Yatri in the name of Hindi writer Chhedilal Gupta.

pairs.m: फेर दिल्ली।
pairs.e: Got associated with Babu Saheb Chaudhary's Mithila Darshan.

pairs.m: कीर्ति नारायण मिश्र, जीवकान्त आ हंसराज संग पत्र व्यवहार होइन्हि।
pairs.e: Then worked at Bharatiya Jnanpith (Calcutta), he was with his wife.

pairs.m: फेर अक्टूबर ६३ मे ओ पटनामे स्थिर भऽ गेलाह।
pairs.e: Then left the job and started Rāgarang Bahār, but it closed down.

pairs.m: १९६६, शशि, दिव्या, मुक्ता,नीलू नवादासँ भेँट करैले एलखिन्ह।
pairs.e: During his six-year stay in Calcutta, he wrote a lot of literature.

pairs.m: बनारसक अलका संगे पत्राचार, अन्तिम प्रेम प्रसंग।
pairs.e: His body weakened; the lump on his stomach started in Calcutta.

pairs.m: नवम्बर १९६६ क पहिल सप्ताहमे ओकरासँ भेँट करय बनारस गेलाह।
pairs.e: Then Delhi.

pairs.m: साम्यवादी आलोचक नामवर सिंह एक टा पोस्टकार्डो देब उचित नै बुझलनि, नईं कहानियाँमे मधुकर गंगाधरक वक्तव्य छपल जे राजकमलक बीमारीक इलाज चलि रहल अछि, ओ अभाव आ उपेक्षाक बहाना कए लोकसँ पाइ ऐंठय चाहैत छथि।
pairs.e: Correspondence with Kirti Narayan Mishra, Jivakanth, and Hansraj.

pairs.m: ओ चिट्ठी लिखि कऽ लहरक सम्पादक प्रकाश जैन आ मनमोहिनीकेँ सेहो भड़कौलनि।
pairs.e: Then in October 1963, he settled in Patna.

pairs.m: राजकमल डायरीमे लिखलनि- मधुकर एण्ड लहर पीपुल्स हैभ जोइण्ड हैण्ड्स इन डर्टी प्रोपेगैण्डा अगेंस्ट मी।
pairs.e: 1966, Shashi, Divya, Mukta, Nilu came from Nawada to meet him.

pairs.m: एक दिन डॉ चतुर्वेदी हुनका कहलकनि जे अहाँक शिश्न मे यूरेथ्रल कैंसर भऽ सकैत अछि आ जँ से भेल, तखन शिश्न काटय पड़ि सकैत अछि।
pairs.e: Correspondence with Alka of Varanasi, his last love affair.

pairs.m: राजकमल डायरीमे लिखलनि- इफ आइ हैभ टू कट माइ पेनिस, आइ विल कमिट सूसाइड।
pairs.e: In the first week of November 1966, he went to Varanasi to meet her.

pairs.m: सभसँ बेशी सेवा चन्द्रमौलि उपाध्याय दुनू व्यक्ति केलखिन्ह, आर्थिक रूपेँ सेहो, राजकमलक मृत्युक बहुत दिन बाद चन्द्रमौलि उपाध्याय दुनू व्यक्ति आत्महत्या कऽ लेलनि।
pairs.e: The communist critic Namwar Singh did not find it appropriate to send a postcard; in Nai Kahaniyan, a statement by Madhukar Gangadhar was published that Rajkamal was undergoing treatment for his illness; he was asking people for money by making excuses of poverty and neglect.

pairs.m: राजकमलक देहगाथा हिनके दुनू गोटेकेँ समर्पित अछि।
pairs.e: He wrote a letter and also provoked Prakash Jain and Manmohini, editors of Lahar.

pairs.m: हंसराज आ आलोक धन्वा देखैले अबथिन्ह अस्पताल।
pairs.e: Rajkamal wrote in his diary – Madhukar & Lahar people have joined hands in dirty propaganda against me.

pairs.m: राजकमलक चारि भाँय पटनामे रहथिन्ह, तीन भाँयकेँ नोकरी सेहो रहन्हि मुदा एक पाइक दबाइयो अनबाक कष्ट ओ सभ नै केलन्हि।
pairs.e: One day, Dr. Chaturvedi told him that he might have urethral cancer, and if that happens, the penis may have to be cut.
Rajkamal wrote in his diary – If I have to cut my penis, I will commit suicide.

pairs.m: नित नवल राजकमल - मैथिली साहित्य राजकमल एक सय कविता, तीनटा उपन्यास, ३७टा कथा, तीन टा एकांकी आ चारि टा आलोचनात्मक निबन्ध लिखलनि।
pairs.e: Chandramouli Upadhyay provided the most service, both individuals, even financially; long after Rajkamal's death, both Chandramouli Upadhyay and the other individual committed suicide.

pairs.m: स्वरगंधामे राजकमलक नवम्बर १९५७ सँ अप्रैल १९५८ धरिक कलकत्ता प्रवासमे लिखल गेल कविता संकलित अछि।
pairs.e: Rajkamal's deha-gāthā is dedicated to these two individuals.

pairs.m: ओ यात्रीकेँ अर्वाचीन होइतो आधुनिक नै मानैत छथि।
pairs.e: Hansraj and Alok Dhanwa used to visit him in the hospital.

pairs.m: राजकमल उपन्यास लिखलनि आन्दोलन, पाथर फूल आ आदिकथा।
pairs.e: Rajkamal's four brothers lived in Patna; three brothers also had jobs, but none of them bothered to send even money for medicine.

pairs.m: लिली रेक रंगीन परदा हुनका एमाइल जोलाक उपन्यास लज्जाक स्मरण करा देलकन्हि।
pairs.e: Died in June 1967.

pairs.m: रमानाथ झा परम्परावादी चिन्तनक कारणेँ राजकमलक साहित्यक सम्पूर्ण शिल्पकेँ कृत्रिम घोषित कऽ देलन्हि।
pairs.e: Nit Naval Rajkamal - Maithili Literature Rajkamal wrote one hundred poems, three novels, thirty-seven stories, three one-act plays, and four critical essays.

pairs.m: बनैत बिगड़ैत सुभाषचन्द्र यादवजीक “बनैत बिगड़ैत” कथा-संग्रहक सभ कथामे सँ अधिकांशमे ई भेटत जे कथा खिस्सासँ बेशी एकटा थीम लऽ आगाँ बढ़ल अछि आ अपन काज खतम करिते अन्त प्राप्त कएने अछि।
pairs.e: Swar Gandha collects the poems written during his Calcutta stay from November 1957 to April 1958.

pairs.m: दोसर विशेषता अछि एकर भाषा।
pairs.e: He considers Yatri as modern but not contemporary.

pairs.m: बलचनमाक भाषा ओइ उपन्यासक मुख्य पात्रक आत्मकथात्मक भाषा अछि मुदा एतए ई भाषा कथाकारक अपन छन्हि आ तइ अर्थेँ ई एकटा विशिष्ट स्वरूप लैत अछि।
pairs.e: Rajkamal wrote novels Andolan, Pathar Phool, and Aadikatha.

pairs.m: एक दिस कथाक उपदेशात्मक खिस्सा-पिहानी स्वरूप ग्रहण करबाक परिपाटीक विरुद्ध सुभाषजीक कथाकेँ एकटा सीमित परिमितिमे थीम लऽ कऽ चलबाक, भाषाक शिल्प जे खाँटी देशी अछि पर ध्यान देबाक सम्मिलित कारणसँ पाठकक एक वर्गकेँ ऐ संग्रहक कथा सभमे असीम आनन्द भेटतन्हि तँ संगे-संग खिस्सा-पिहानीसँ बाहर नै आबि सकल पाठक वर्गकेँ ई कथा संग्रह निराश नै करत वरन हुनकर सभक रुचिक परिष्करण करत।
pairs.e: Lily Ray's colorful screen reminded him of Emile Zola's novel Nana.

pairs.m: किछु भाषायी मानकीकरण प्रसंग- जेना ऐछ, अछि, अ इ छ ।
pairs.e: Ramanath Jha, due to his traditionalist thinking, declared the entire craft of Rajkamal's literature as artificial.

pairs.m: जइ कालमे मानकीकरण भऽ रहल छल ओइ समय ऐपर ध्यान देबाक आवश्यकता रहय।
pairs.e: □ Banait Bigadait (Making and Breaking) In most of the stories of Subhashchandra Yadavji's "Banait Bigadait" story collection, it is found that the story, more than a tale, moves forward with a theme and ends upon completing its function.

pairs.m: जेना “जाइत रही” केँ “जाति रही” लिखी आ फेर जाति (जा इ त) लेल प्रोनन्सिएशनक निअम बनाबी तेहने सन ऐछ संगे अछि।
pairs.e: Another specialty is its language.

pairs.m: मुदा आब देरी भऽ गेल अछि से लेखको कनियाँ-पुतरा मे एकर प्रयोग कऽ दिशा देखबैत छथि मुदा दोसर कथा सभमे घुरि जाइत छथि।
pairs.e: The language of Balchanma is the autobiographical language of the protagonist of that novel, but here this language is the storyteller's own, and in that sense, it takes on a unique form.

pairs.m: मुदा ऐसँ ई आवश्यकता तँ सिद्ध होइते अछि जे एकटा मानक रूप स्थिर कएल जाय आ “छै” लिखबाक अछि तँ सेहो ठीक आ “छैक” लिखबाक अछि तँ “अन्तक ’क’ साइलेन्ट अछि” से प्रोनन्सिएशनक निअम बनय।
pairs.e: On one hand, against the tradition of the story adopting a didactic tale-like form, Subhashji's method of moving with a theme within a limited scope, paying attention to the craft of language which is pure native, gives a section of readers immense pleasure in the stories of this collection; at the same time, readers who couldn't come out of the tale-narrative tradition are not disappointed by this story collection; rather, it refines their taste.

pairs.m: मुदा से जल्दी बनय आ सर्वग्राह्य हुअय तकर बेगरता हमरा बुझाइत अछि, आजुक लोककेँ “य” लिखल जाए वा “ए” ऐपर भरि जिनगी लड़बाक समय नै छै, जे ध्वनि सिद्धांत कहैत अछि से मानू, य व्यंजन थिक आ ए स्वर, से एकक बदला दोसरक सर्वभौम प्रयोग असम्भव।
pairs.e: Some language standardization contexts – like aich, achi, a i ch.

pairs.m: आ सेनै हुअय तँ प्रोनन्सिएशनक निअम बनाउ।
pairs.e: During the time when standardization was happening, it was necessary to pay attention to this.

pairs.m: “नहि” लेल “नञि” लिखब तँ बुझबामे अबैत अछि मुदा नइँ (अन्तिका), नइं (एन.
pairs.e: Like "jāit rahī" was written as "jāti rahī" and then rules of pronunciation were made for jāti (jā i t), similarly aich is included. But now it is too late; the writer uses it in Kaniya-Putra to show direction, but reverts in other stories.
But this at least proves the need to fix a standard form, and if "chhai" is to be written, it's fine, and if "chhaik" is to be written, then a pronunciation rule that the 'k' at the end is silent.

But the urgency to make it quickly and acceptable to all is understood; today's people do not have the time to argue over writing 'ya' or 'e'; whatever the phonology says, accept it; 'y' is a consonant, 'e' is a vowel; using one universally in place of the other is impossible.

pairs.m: आ नँइ (साकेतानन्द - कालरात्रिश्च दारुणा) मे सँ साकेतानन्दजी बला प्रयोग ध्वनि-विज्ञान सिद्धांतसँ बेशी समीचीन सिद्ध होइत अछि आ से विश्वास नै हुअए तँ ध्वनि प्रयोगशाला सभक मदति लिअ।
pairs.e: If it is not acceptable, then make pronunciation rules.

pairs.m: “बनैत बिगड़ैत” पोथीक ई एकटा विशेषता अछि जे सुभाषचन्द्र यादवजी अपन विशिष्ट लेखन-शैलीक प्रयोग कएने छथि जे ध्वन्यात्मक अछि आ मानकीकरण सम्वादकेँ आगाँ लऽ जेबामे सक्षम अछि।
pairs.e: Writing "nañi" for "nahi" is understandable, but among "naim" (Antika), "nain" (N.

pairs.m: स्वतन्त्रताक बादक पीढ़ीक कथाकार छथि सुभाषजी।
pairs.e: B.

pairs.m: कथाक माध्यमसँ जीवनकेँ रूप दैत छथि।
शिल्प आ कथ्य दुनूसँ कथाकेँ अलंकृत कऽ कथाकेँ सार्थक बनबैत छथि।
pairs.e: , and "nãi" (Sāketānand - Kālarātirśca Dāruṇā), the usage of Sāketānandji proves more appropriate according to phonological principles; if you don't trust that, take help from phonetic laboratories.

pairs.m: अस्तित्वक लेल सामान्य लोकक संघर्ष तँ ऐ स्थितिमे हिनकर कथा सभमे भेटब स्वाभाविके।
pairs.e: A special feature of the book "Banait Bigadait" is that Subhashchandra Yadavji has used his distinctive writing style, which is phonetic and capable of advancing the standardization dialogue.

pairs.m: कएक दशक पूर्व लिखल हिनक कथा “काठक बनल लोक” क बदरिया साइते संयोग हंसैत रहय।
pairs.e: Subhashji is a storyteller of the post-independence generation.

pairs.m: एहु कथा संग्रहक सभ पात्र एहने सन विशेषता लेने अछि।
pairs.e: He gives form to life through stories.

pairs.m: हॉस्पीटलमे कनैत-कनैत सुतलाक बाद उठि कऽ कोनो पात्र फेरसँ कानय लगैत छथि तँ कोनो पात्र प्रेममे पड़ल छथि।
pairs.e: He adorns the story with both craft and content to make it meaningful.

pairs.m: किनकोमे बिजनेस सेन्स छन्हि तँ हरिवंश सन पात्र सेहो छथि जे उपकारक बदला सिस्टम फॉल्टक कारण अपकार कऽ जाइत छथि।
pairs.e: The struggle of ordinary people for existence is naturally found in his stories.

pairs.m: आब “बनैत बिगड़ैत” कथा संग्रहक कथा सभपर गहिंकी नजरि दौगाबी।
pairs.e: The Badariya of his story "Kathak Banal Lok", written decades ago, would laugh at times.

pairs.m: कनियाँ-पुतरा- ऐ कथामे रस्तामे एकटा बचिया लेखकक पएर छानि फेर ठेहुनपर माथ राखि निश्चिन्त अछि, जेना माएक ठेहुनपर माथ रखने हुअय।
pairs.e: All the characters in this story collection have such unique qualities.

pairs.m: नेबो सन कोनो कड़गर चीज लेखकसँ टकरेलन्हि।
pairs.e: A character, after waking up from sleeping while crying in the hospital, starts crying again; another falls in love.

pairs.m: ई लड़कीक छाती छिऐ।
pairs.e: Some have business sense, while there are characters like Harivansh who, in return for a favour, end up doing a disservice due to a system fault.

pairs.m: लड़की निर्विकार रहय जेना बाप-दादा वा भाय बहिन सऽ सटल हो।
pairs.e: Now, let's take a deep look at the stories in the "Banait Bigadait" collection.

pairs.m: लेखक सोचैत छथि, ई सीता बनत की द्रौपदी।
pairs.e: Kaniya-Putra (Girl's Son) – In this story, a child on the road rubs the writer's feet, then places its head on his lap and is carefree, as if placing its head on its mother's lap. No hard object seemed to have hit the writer.

pairs.m: राबन आ दुर्जोधनक आशंका लेखककेँ घेर लैत छन्हि।
pairs.e: This girl's chest is wet.

pairs.m: कनियाँ-पुतरा पढ़बाक बाद वएह सड़कक चौबटिया अछि आ वएह रेड-लाइटपर गाड़ी चलबैत-रोकैत काल बालक-बालिका सभ देखबामे अबैत छथि।
pairs.e: The girl remains unperturbed, as if touching her father, grandfather, or brother.

pairs.m: मुदा आब दृष्टिमे परिवर्तन भऽ जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: The writer thinks, is this Sita becoming, or Draupadi?

pairs.m: कारक शीसा पोछि पाइ मंगनिहार बालक-बालिकाकेँ पाइ-देने वा बिन देने, मुदा बिनु सोचने आगाँ बढ़ि जाएबला दृष्टिक परिवर्तन।
pairs.e: Suspicions of Ravan and Duryodhan surround the writer.

pairs.m: कनियाँ-पुतरा पढ़बाक बाद की हुनकर दृष्टिमे कोनो परिवर्तन नै होयतन्हि?
pairs.e: After reading Kaniya-Putra, one sees the same crossroads on the road and children while driving at traffic lights.

pairs.m: बालक तँ पैघ भऽ चोरि करत वा कोनो ड्रग कार्टेलक सभसँ निचुलका सीढ़ी बनत मुदा बालिका ?
pairs.e: But now the vision changes.

pairs.m: ओ सीता बनत आकि द्रौपदी आकि आम्रपाली।
pairs.e: Whether wiping the car's glass to give money to begging children or not giving, but moving ahead without thinking – does reading Kaniya-Putra not change that perspective?

pairs.m: जे सामाजिक संस्था, ह्यूमन राइट्स ऑरगेनाइजेशन कोनो प्रेमीक बिजलीक खाम्हपर चढ़ि प्राण देबाक धमकीपर नीचाँ जाल पसारि कऽ टी.
pairs.e: The boy will grow up and steal or become the lowest rung of some drug cartel, but the girl?
Will she become Sita, or Draupadi, or Amrapali?

pairs.m: कैमरापर अपन आ अपन संस्थाक नाम प्रचारित करैत छथि ओ ऐ कथाकेँ पढ़लाक बाद ओइ पुरातन दृष्टिसँ काज कऽ सकताह?
pairs.e: The social institutions, human rights organizations, which, on the threat of someone dying by climbing an electric pole for love, spread their net below to publicize themselves and their organization on TV cameras – can they work with that outdated view after reading this story?

pairs.m: ओ सरकार जे कोनो हॉस्पीटलक नाम बदलि कऽ जयप्रकाश नारायणक नामपर करैत अछि वा हार्डिंग पार्कक नाम वीर कुँअर सिंहक नामपर कऽ अपन कर्त्तव्यक इतिश्री मानि लैत अछि ओ समस्याक जड़ि धरि पहुँचि नव पार्क आ नव हॉस्पीटल बना कऽ जयप्रकाश नारायण आ वीर कुँअर सिंहक नामपर करत आकि दोसरक कएल काजमे “मेड बाइ मी” केर स्टाम्प लगाओत?
pairs.e: The government, which changes the name of a hospital to Jai Prakash Narayan's name or Harding Park to Veer Kunwar Singh's name and considers its duty done, will it reach the root of the problem and build new parks and hospitals and name them after Jai Prakash Narayan and Veer Kunwar Singh, or put a stamp of "Made by Me" on work done by others?

pairs.m: ई संस्था सभ आइ धरि मेहनतिसँ बचैत अयबाक आ सरल उपाय तकबाक प्रवृत्तिपर रोक नै लगाओत?
pairs.e: Do these institutions still not stop the tendency to survive with minimal effort and seek easy solutions?

pairs.m: असुरक्षित- ट्रेनसँ उतरलाक बाद घरक २० मिनटक रस्ताक राति जतेक असुरक्षित भऽ गेल अछि तकर सचित्र वर्णन ई कथा करैत अछि।
pairs.e: Asurakshit (Insecure) – After getting off the train, the 20-minute walk home at night has become so insecure that this story paints a vivid picture.

pairs.m: पहिने तँ एहन नै रहैक- ई अछि लोकक मानसिक अवस्था।
pairs.e: It wasn't like this before – this is the people's mental state.

pairs.m: मुदा ऐ तरहक समस्या दिस ककरो ध्यान कहाँ छै।
pairs.e: But where is anyone's attention to such problems?

pairs.m: पैघ-पैघ समस्या, उदारीकरण आन कतेक विषयपर मीडिआक ध्यान छै।
pairs.e: The media's attention is on big problems, liberalization, and many other issues.

pairs.m: चौक-चौराहाक ऐ तरहक समस्यापर नव दृष्टि अबैत अछि, ऐमे स्टेशनसँ घरक बीचक दूरी रातिक अन्हारमे पहाड़ सन भऽ जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: A new perspective is emerging on such problems at squares and crossings; in this, the distance from station to home becomes like a mountain in the darkness of night.

pairs.m: प्रदेशक तत्कालीन कानून-व्यवस्थापर ई एक तरहक टिप्पणी अछि।
pairs.e: This is a kind of comment on the then law and order situation in the state.

pairs.m: एकाकी- ऐ कथामे कुसेसर हॉस्पीटलमे छथि।
pairs.e: Ekaki (Alone) – In this story, Kusesar is in the hospital.

pairs.m: हॉस्पीटलक सचित्र विवरण भेल अछि।
pairs.e: A detailed description of the hospital is given.

pairs.m: ओतय एकटा स्त्री पतिक मृत्युक बाद कनैत-कनैत प्रायः सुति गेलि आ फेर निन्न टुटलापर कानय लागलि।
pairs.e: There, a woman, after her husband's death, practically fell asleep crying and then started crying again when she woke up. This is how it happens.

pairs.m: एना होइत अछि।
pairs.e: The storyteller points to a reality of human life and comments on the hospital's talk and management, not by directly stating it but by showing its vitality.

pairs.m: कथाकार मानव जीवनक एकटा सत्यता दिस इशारा दैत आ हॉस्पीटलक बात-व्यवस्थापर टिप्पणी तेना भऽ कऽ नै वरण जीवन्तता देखा कऽ करैत छथि।
pairs.e: Wo Ladki (That Girl) – In this story, among the lives of hostel boys and girls, a young man named Naveen sees an empty cup in a girl's hand, which belongs to that girl and her boyfriend.

pairs.m: ओ लड़की- ऐ कथामे हॉस्टलक लड़का-लड़कीक जीवनक बीच नवीन नामक युवक एकटा लड़कीक हाथमे ऐंठ खाली कप, जे ओइ लड़कीक आ ओकर प्रेमीक अछि, देखैत अछि।
pairs.e: The girl asks Naveen where he is going.

pairs.m: लड़की नवीनकेँ पुछैत छै जे ओ केम्हर जा रहल अछि।
pairs.e: Naveen feels that she asked him to throw the cup, assuming he is going the same way.

pairs.m: नवीनकेँ होइत छै जे ओ ओकरा अपनासँ दब बुझि कप फेंकबाक लेल पुछलक।
pairs.e: Naveen refuses.

pairs.m: नवीन ओकरा मना कऽ दैत अछि।
pairs.e: Thoughts keep swirling in his mind.

pairs.m: विचार सभ ओकर मोनमे घुरमैत रहैत छै।
pairs.e: This story is based on a small incident.

pairs.m: जे ओ हमरा दब बूझि चाहक कप फेकबाक लेल कहलक?
pairs.e: And he moves ahead firmly without saying anything.

pairs.m: आ ओ दृढ़तासँ नै कहि आगाँ बढ़ि जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: Like 'Ekaki', this story is also based on psychological analysis.

pairs.m: एकाकी जेकाँ ई कथा सेहो मनोवैज्ञानिक विश्लेषणपर आधारित अछि।
pairs.e: Ekta Prem Katha (A Love Story) – Previously, those who had phones at home, others' calls would come to their home; they would just forward the call.

pairs.m: एकटा प्रेम कथा- पहिने जकरा घरमे फोन रहैत छल तकरा घरमे दोसराक फोन अबैत रहैत छल, जे एकरा तँ ओकरा बजा दिअ।
pairs.e: The writer had a phone at home, and when a call came for a lover from his beloved, he used to forward it to the lover.

pairs.m: लेखकक घरमे फोन छलन्हि आ ओ एकटा प्रेमीक प्रेमिकाक फोन अयलापर, ओकर प्रेमीकेँ बजबैत रहैत छथि।
pairs.e: The lover bought a mobile phone, so the calls stopped. But when the lover changed his number, the beloved's calls started coming to the writer's home again.

pairs.m: प्रेमी मोबाइल कीनि लैत अछि से फोन आयब बन्द भऽ जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: The beloved is Ritu, a friend of the lover's cousin; the writer becomes worried about helping her.

pairs.m: मुदा प्रेमी द्वारा नम्बर बदलि लेलापर प्रेमिकाक फोन फेरसँ लेखकक घरपर अबैत अछि।
pairs.e: In this story, lover-beloved, mobile phone, and telephone all appear naturally in a new story with the new age.

pairs.m: प्रेमिका, प्रेमीक ममियौत बहिनक सखी रितु छथि आ लेखक ओकर सहायताक लेल चिन्तित भऽ जाइत छथि।
pairs.e: Banait-Bigadait (Making-Breaking) is the title story.

pairs.m: ऐ कथामे प्रेमी-प्रेमिका, मोबाइल आ फोन ई सभ नव युगक संग नव कथामे सेहो स्वाभाविक रूपेँ अबैत अछि।
pairs.e: Three named characters: Mala, her husband Satta, and granddaughter Munniya.

pairs.m: टाइटल कथा अछि बनैत-बिगड़ैत।
pairs.e: The usual mother-in-law/daughter-in-law issues of the village remain, like asking if they have eaten after bathing, etc. But when the son and daughter-in-law go outside, the mother-in-law becomes worried about the crow's call.

pairs.m: तीन टा नामित पात्र ।
pairs.e: This story depicts the reality of the village after migration.

pairs.m: माला, ओकर पति सत्तो आ पोती मुनियाँ ।
pairs.e: One day, along with Satta, the crow also disappeared, and Munniya kept looking at the crow and her grandfather.

pairs.m: गाम-घरक जे सास-पुतोहुक गप छै, सेहन्ता रहि गेल जे कहियो नहेलाक बाद खाइ लेल पुछितए, एहन सन।
pairs.e: The story of migrants, of son, daughter-in-law, and granddaughter, of mother-in-law/daughter-in-law quarrels and love!

pairs.m: मुदा सैह बेटा-पुतोहु जखन बाहर चलि जाइत छथि तँ वएह सासु कार कौआक टाहिपर चिन्तित होमए लगैत छथि।
pairs.e: Apan-Apan Dukh (Each Their Own Sorrow) – In the story, the wife, in her situation of neglect, curses her daughter and son.

pairs.m: माइग्रेशनक बादक गामक यथार्थकेँ चित्रित करैत अछि ई कथा।
pairs.e: Having to feed daughter and son at night, the wife having to stay hungry when the barn is locked after eating, and consequently getting angry at the husband's blustering voice – it's natural.

pairs.m: सत्तोक संग कौआ सेहो एक दिन बिला जायत आ मुनियाँ कौआ आ दादा दुनूकेँ तकैत रहत।
pairs.e: Everyone has their own world.

pairs.m: प्रवासीक कथा, बेटा-पुतोहुक आ पोतीक कथा, सासु-पुतोहुक झगड़ा आ प्रेम!
pairs.e: People think that their world's joys and sorrows alone are complete, but that is not true.

pairs.m: अपन-अपन दुःख कथामे पत्नी, अपन अवहेलनाक स्थितिमे, धीया-पुताकेँ सरापैत छथि।
pairs.e: Everyone has their own joys and sorrows, their own hopes and aspirations.

pairs.m: रातिमे धीया-पुताक खेनाइ, खा लेबा उत्तर भनसाघरक ताला बन्द रहबाक स्थितिमे पत्नीक भूखल रहब आ परिणामस्वरूप पतिक फोंफक स्वरसँ कुपित हएब स्वाभाविक।
pairs.e: The storyteller reveals such truths that come within his experience.

pairs.m: सभक अपन संसार छै।
pairs.e: How can the autobiographical experience be independent of the environment, and this is evident in all of Subhash Chandra Yadavji's stories.

pairs.m: लोक बुझैए जे ओकरे संसारक सुख आ दुःख मात्र सम्पूर्ण छै मुदा से नै अछि।
pairs.e: Atank (Terror) – In the story, the storyteller meets an old friend, Harivansh, at the office.

pairs.m: सभक अपन सुख-दुःख छै, अपन आशा आ आकांक्षा छै।
pairs.e: The writer's work of filing didn't get done because after Harivansh's transfer, no one took a bribe from him and therefore did not do his work.

pairs.m: कथाकार ओहन सत्यकेँ उद्घाटित करैत छथि, जे हुनकर अनुभवक अंतर्गत अबैत छन्हि।
pairs.e: The reason for Harivansh's forced bribe was money; why would others give up their own money?

pairs.m: आत्मानुभूति परिवेश स्वतंत्र कोना भऽ सकत आ से सुभाष चन्द्र यादवजीक सभ कथामे सोझाँ अबैत अछि।
pairs.e: The writer is terrified. The office environment, corruption, and changing social relations due to one person's transfer are all expressed here.

pairs.m: आतंक कथामे कथाकारकेँ पुरान संगी हरिवंशसँ कार्यालयमे भेँट होइत छन्हि।
pairs.e: When we or you go to the Block or Secretariat for any work today, we hear the same thing: just give whatever money is demanded, and if there's any problem then say!

pairs.m: लेखकक दाखिल-खारिज बला काज ऐ लऽ कऽ नै भेलन्हि जे हरिवंशक स्थानान्तरणक पश्चात् ने क्यो हुनकासँ घूस लेलक आ तइ द्वारे काजो नै केलक।
pairs.e: And if we take someone's name or recommendation in exchange for money, the employee will neither take the money nor will your work get done.

pairs.m: हरिवंशक बगेबानी घूसक अनेर पाइक कारण छल से दोसर किएक अपन पाइ छोड़त ?
pairs.e: Ekta Ant (An End) – In the story, upon the father-in-law's death, the writer's brother-in-law has cut his hair, but the writer has not; there are various discussions about this.

pairs.m: लेखक आतंकित छथि।
pairs.e: The brother-in-law, having cut his hair, is carefree.

pairs.m: कार्यालयक परिवेश, भ्रष्टाचार आ एक गोटेक स्थानांतरणसँ बदलैत सामाजिक सम्बन्ध ई सभ एतऽ व्यक्त भेल अछि।
pairs.e: This cultural symbolism has come – whoever is caught is the thief and the wrongdoer, whoever is not caught is the idealist.

pairs.m: आइ काल्हि हम आकि अहाँ ब्लॉकमे वा सचिवालयमे कोनो काज लेल जाइत छी, तँ यैह ने सुनऽ पड़ैत अछि, जे पाइ जे माँगत से दऽ देबैक आ तखन कोनो दिक्कत हुअय तँ कहब !
pairs.e: Not completely, but in this story too, such beliefs are born and broken.

pairs.m: आ पाइक बदला ककरो नाम वा पैरवी लऽ गेलौं तँ कर्मचारी ने पाइये लेत आ नहिये अहाँक काज हएत।
pairs.e: In Haryana, when a father dies, people do not cut their hair; does that mean there is any lack of sorrow?

pairs.m: एकटा अन्त कथामे ससुरक मृत्युपर लेखकक साढ़ू केश कटेने छथि आ लेखक नै, ऐपर कएक तरहक गप होइत अछि।
pairs.e: In Punjab, women do not wear vermilion or bangles for a year after the husband's death, but bangles are filled until the first death anniversary; does that mean after the first year of marriage, their love for their husband decreases?

pairs.m: साढ़ू केश कटा कऽ निश्चिन्त छथि।
pairs.e: Kabachhu (Massage) – In the story, the masseuse comes to the writer, puts her hand on his thigh.

pairs.m: ई जे सांस्कृतिक सिम्बोलिज्म आयल अछि, जे पकड़ा गेल से चोर आ खराप काज केनिहार, जे नै पकड़ाएल से आदर्शवादी।
pairs.e: Due to the writer being surrounded by people of upper-caste culture, he removes her hand.

pairs.m: पूरा-पूरी तँ नै, मुदा अहू कथामे एहने आस्था जन्म लैत अछि आ टूटि जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: The masseuse says, what is the hesitation in touching the body that is touched. As if the masseuse was explaining to the writer that she had mistaken him for a young woman.

pairs.m: हरियाणामे बापो मरलापर लोक केश नै कटबैत अछि, तँ की ओकर दुःखमे कोनो कमी रहैत छै तेँ ?
pairs.e: The writer feels she is a woman and the masseuse is her old friend.

pairs.m: पंजाबक महिला एक बरखक बाद ने सिनूर लगबैत छथि आ ने चूड़ी पहिरैत छथि मुदा पहिल बरख कान्ह धरि चूड़ी भरल रहैत छन्हि, तँ की बियाहक पहिल बरखक बाद हुनकर पति-प्रेममे कोनो घटंती आबि जाइत छन्हि ?
pairs.e: The masseuse does not understand the subtlety of place and time; otherwise, the writer would have had a massage even in this heat.

pairs.m: कबाछु कथा मे चम्पीबलाक लेखक लग आयब, जाँघपर हाथ राखब।
pairs.e: There is regret over the masseuse's helplessness, but also disgust at her 'she-e-e' thought.

pairs.m: अभिजात्य संस्कारक लोक लग बैसल रहबाक कारणसँ लेखक द्वारा ओकर हाथ हटायब ।
pairs.e: Freud's psychoanalysis has been heavily criticized for centering on sex.

pairs.m: चम्पीबला द्वारा ई गप बाजब जे छुअल देहकेँ छूलामे कोन संकोच।
pairs.e: But from experience, it is clear that the more distance you create from sex, the more you keep it away from conversation, story, and literature, the more intense its attack becomes.

pairs.m: जेना चम्पीवला लेखककेँ बुझाइय रहन्हि जे हुनका युवती बुझि रहल छलन्हि।
pairs.e: Karbar (Business) – In the story, the writer meets Mr. Verma, Sinha, and two others.

pairs.m: लेखककेँ लगैत छन्हि जे ओ स्त्री छथि आ चम्पीबला ओकर पुरान यार।
pairs.e: In the bar, Sinha, distinguishing friendship from business, tells two tales.

pairs.m: ठाम-कुठाम आ समय-कुसमयक महीन समझ चम्पीवलाकेँ नै छइ, नै तँ लेखक ओतेक गरमीयोमे चम्पी करा लितय।
pairs.e: Everything has a price; after buying a TV for a friend's wife, the discussion of the demand for a fridge ends in between.

pairs.m: चम्पीवलाक दीनतापर अफसोच भेलन्हि मुदा ओकर शी-इ-इ केँ मोन पाड़ैत वितृष्णा सेहो।
pairs.e: In the second tale, a woman goes to her ex-lover to pay the doctor's fee to save her husband's life.

pairs.m: फ्रायडक मनोविश्लेषणक बड्ड आलोचना भेल जे ओ सेक्सकेँ केन्द्रमे राखि गप करैत छथि।
pairs.e: The ex-lover, in return for giving money, asks to spend the night with her.

pairs.m: मुदा अनुभवसँ ई गप सोझाँ अबैत अछि जे सेक्ससँ जतेक दूरी बनायब, जतेक एकरा वार्तालाप-कथा-साहित्यसँ दूर राखब, ओकर आक्रमण ततेक तीव्र हएत।
pairs.e: Sinha does not find anyone at fault in this story; why would the doctor treat without taking money?

pairs.m: कारबार मे लेखकक भेँट मिस्टर वर्मा, सिन्हा आ दू टा आर गोटेसँ होइत अछि।
pairs.e: Why would the ex-lover give money for the engagement?

pairs.m: बार मे सिन्हा दोस्ती आ बिजनेसकेँ फराक कहैत दू टा खिस्सा सुनबैत अछि।
pairs.e: And if the woman does not spend the night with the ex-lover, her husband will die.

pairs.m: सभ चीजक मोल अछि, ऐपर एकटा दोस्तक वाइफ लेल टी.
pairs.e: Now when the writer comes out of the bar, the guard's salute also starts sounding like the clink of money.
The breakdown of ancient values, friendship, and ideals gives a feeling of loneliness.

pairs.m: किनबाक बाद फ्रिजक डिमान्ड अएबाक गप बीचेमे खतम भऽ जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: Kushti (Wrestling) – In this story too, Freud appears.

pairs.m: दोसर खिस्सामे एकटा स्त्री पतिक जान बचबय लेल डॉक्टरक फीस देबाक लेल पूर्व प्रेमी लग जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: The story begins with a dried, hard stain on the lungi.

pairs.m: पूर्व प्रेमी पाइ देबाक बदलामे ओकरा संगे राति बितबय लेल कहैत छै।
pairs.e: But it immediately becomes clear that it is not a semen stain, but a scar.

pairs.m: सिन्हा ऐ कथामे ककरो गलती नै मानैत छथि, डॉक्टर बिना पाइ लेने किए इलाज करत, पूर्व प्रेमी मँगनीमे पाइ किए देत आ ओ स्त्री जे पूर्व प्रेमी संग राति नै बिताओत, तँ ओकर पति मरि जेतै।
pairs.e: Then, at the market wrestling match, discussions about village problems arise; the health centre being closed, the theft of bricks there.

pairs.m: आब बारसँ लेखक निकलैत छथि तँ दरबानक सलाम मारलापर अहूमे पैसाक टनक सुनाइ पड़ऽ लगैत छन्हि।
pairs.e: The younger brother, in the course of treatment, moves from allopathy to homeopathy; this is discussed. People come to ask about each other's wounds, and the writer tells everyone in detail, but ignores some who are younger in age; all this creates an atmosphere.

pairs.m: प्राचीन मूल्य, दोस्ती-यारी आ आदर्शक टूटबाक स्थिति एकटा एकाकीपनक अनुभव करबैत अछि।
pairs.e: Kainari Ailanḍak Larel (Laurel of Canary Island) – In the story, Subhash and Upiya are characters.

pairs.m: कुश्ती मे सेहो फ्रायड सोझाँ अबैत छथि, कथाक प्रारम्भ लुंगीपरक सुखायल कड़गर भेल दागसँ शुरू होइत अछि।
pairs.e: Here there is an image – like the decision is like the erosion of the Kosi.

pairs.m: मुदा तुरत्ते स्पष्ट होइत अछि, जे ओ से दाग नै अछि, वरन घावक दाग अछि।
pairs.e: The cousin brother's letter, going for a bath not having taken a blade, coming to the stream of ochre water, getting off at the bath ghat, and water up to the thighs even after the bath.

pairs.m: फेर हाटक कुश्तीमे गामक समस्याक निपटारा, हेल्थ सेन्टरक बन्द रहब, ओतय ईंटाक चोरिक चरचा अबैत अछि।
pairs.e: Seeing someone pushing a bicycle on the sandy bank, someone says – "Did you get the bicycle from your in-laws?

pairs.m: छोट भाइ कोनो इलाजक क्रममे एलोपैथीसँ हटि कऽ होम्योपैथीपर विश्वास करय लगैत छथि, ऐ गपक चरचा आयल अछि।
pairs.e: Make a sound like a big bullock."

pairs.m: लोक सभक घावक समाचार पुछबा लऽ एनाइ आ लेखक द्वारा सभकेँ विस्तृत विवरण कहि सुनओनाइ मुदा उमरिमे कम वयसक कएक गोटेकेँ टारि देनाइ, ई सभ क्रम एकटा वातावरणक निर्माण करैत अछि।
pairs.e: Didi and Pisa heard the discussion there, that the marriage of their daughter was delayed by two days because of the coat, and also that highly educated people become confused. Subhash cannot even ask for two hundred rupees, Didi's behaviour is ambiguous, Subhash is not assured and returns.

pairs.m: कैनरी आइलैण्डक लारेल कथामे सुभाष आ उपिया कथाक चरित्र छथि।
pairs.e: Trishna (Thirst) – In the story, the writer meets Akhilan.

pairs.m: एतऽ एकटा बिम्ब अछि- जेना निर्णय कोसीक धसना जकाँ।
pairs.e: He recounts the details of his meeting with Shrilata.

pairs.m: ममियौत भाइक चिट्ठी, कटारि देने नाहपर जयबाक, गेरुआ पानिक धारमे आयब, नाहक छीटपर उतारब, छीटक बादो बहुत दूर धरि जांघ भरि पानिक रहब।
pairs.e: After returning on the fifth day, he did not meet her on the train.

pairs.m: धीपल बालुपर साइकिलकेँ ठेलैत देखि कियो कहैत छन्हि- “साइकिल ससुरारिमे देलक-ए?
pairs.e: Now what does Akhilan do?

pairs.m: कने बड़द जकाँ टिटकार दियौक”।
pairs.e: Keeps roaming on the route between Visakhapatnam and Vijayawada, or spends his days with memories.

pairs.m: दीदी-पीसा ऐठाम ऐ गपक चरचा सुनलन्हि, जे कोटक खातिर हुनकर बेटीक विवाह दू दिन रुकि गेल छलन्हि आ ईहो जे बेसी पढ़ने लोक बताह भऽ जाइत अछि।
pairs.e: Analysis of small emotional incidents is present in the stories "Kainari Ailanḍak Larel" and "Trishna".

pairs.m: जेना कोनो हिट फिल्म जेना कन्नड़क “कनतारा” जँ अहाँ पी.
pairs.e: Just as Rajmohan Jha was saddened and held his head after seeing the Hindi translation (by Vibha Rani) of Harimohan Jha's "Kanyadaan", and author Vikram Chandra was sad after seeing the poor web series adaptation of Sacred Games by Anurag Kashyap, we had a similar feeling after seeing the Hindi translation of Gulo.

Mainstream people translate without reading translation theory; when the Brahminical and neo-Brahminical writers of the mainstream are dried up of vocabulary in original writing, what can they do in translation?

pairs.m: आर.
pairs.e: Translator Raman Kumar Singh has neither Maithili vocabulary nor Hindi.

pairs.m: मे देख लेने छी तँ की पाँचो मिनट तकरा अहाँ ओ.
pairs.e: In his newspaper-like language, in the very first line of Gulo's translation, he translates "Tila Sakaranti" as "Makar Sankranti"!

Do the Brahminical and neo-Brahminical writers of the Maithili mainstream not use Makar Sankranti, and could Subhash Chandra Yadav not write "Makar Sankranti" instead of "Tila-Sakaranti" in the first line of Gulo?
So if you wish to read Subhash Chandra Yadav's Gulo in both Hindi and Maithili and want to see the application of gaslighting theory, read the Hindi translation first, then the original Maithili.

pairs.m: हिन्दी जइ हिसाबे अपन भूगोल बढेलक अछि ओइ हिसाबे ओकर शब्दावली नै बढ़ल छै ।
pairs.e: is distressing.

pairs.m: अमेरिकामे ३५० शब्दक अंग्रेजीक "हाइ प्रेक्वेन्सी" आ ३५०० "बेसिक वर्ड लिस्ट" हाइ स्कूलक छात्र लेल छै जे क्रमशः कॉलेज आ ग्रेजुएट स्कूल (ओतए पोस्ट ग्रेजुएटकेँ ग्रेजुएट स्कूल कहल जाइ छै) धरि पहुँचलापर दुगुना (गएर भाषा फेकल्टीक छात्र लेल) भऽ जाइ छै।
pairs.e: □ Preface of Gulo "The Story of the Last Destitute Person" - Author Kedar Kanan Before the beginning of the original Maithili Gulo, there is a preface titled "The Story of the Last Destitute Person".

pairs.m: साहित्यक विद्यार्थी/ साहित्यकार लेल ऐ सँ दस गुणा अपेक्षित होइत अछि।
pairs.e: This is the cannon fodder of the mainstream, and this time the burden of that cannon fodder is on Kedar Kanan's shoulders, and it was written on the day of Tila-Sankranti!

pairs.m: हिन्दीमे अपवाद, जकरा हिन्दीक पुरोधा लोकनि उपहासमे आंचलिक उपन्यास/ लेखन कहैत छथि आ एक तरहेँ नकारैत छथि, केँ छोड़ि हिन्दीक कवि आ उपन्यासकार अठमा वर्गक २००० शब्दक शब्दावलीसँ साहित्य (पद्य, उपन्यास) रचै छथि आ मैथिलीक किछु साहित्यकार ऐ बेसिक २००० शब्दक वर्ड लिस्ट धरि सीमित रहए चाहै छथि, जखन कि जापानी अल्फाबेटक चेन्ह ५०० धरि पहुँचि जाइ छै।
pairs.e: But Subhash Chandra Yadav never wrote the story of the last destitute person.

pairs.m: जइ भाषामे रामलोचन शरण १४-१५ हजार पाँतीक श्रीरामचरित मानसक अनुवाद सन् १९६८ (वि. २०२५) मे पूर्णभाव रक्षित मैथिली रूपमे केलन्हि ओत सन् २०२१ (वि. २०७८) मे गुलोक ऐ तरहक हिन्दी अनुवाद मोनकेँ विखिन्न करैत अछि।
pairs.e: This preface is a malicious attempt to push Subhash Chandra Yadav's writing to the other side.

pairs.m: गुलो केर आमुख “आखिरी विपन्न मनुक्खक गाथा”- लेखक केदार कानन मूल मैथिली गुलोक प्रारम्भसँ पूर्व एकटा आमुख अछि “आखिरी विपन्न मनुक्खक गाथा”।
pairs.e: He wrote the biography of Gulo Mandal, that Gulo Mandal who says – "Do you recognize me?

pairs.m: ई अछि मूल धाराक कन्नारोहट आ ऐ बेर ओइ कन्नारोहटक भार छन्हि केदार काननक कान्हपर, आ ई लिखल गेल अछि तिला-संक्रान्ति दिन!
pairs.e: If you don't recognize me, then recognize me.

pairs.m: मुदा सुभाष चन्द्र यादव तँ आखिरी विपन्न मनुक्खक गाथा लिखबे नै केने छथि।
pairs.e: I am Gulo Mandal.

pairs.m: ई आमुख सुभाष चन्द्र यादवक लेखनीकेँ ओइपार ठाढ़ करबाक कुत्सित प्रयास अछि।
pairs.e: My house is on this square.

pairs.m: ओ तँ लिखने छथि गुलो मण्डलक जीवनी, ओ गुलो मण्डल जे कहैत छथि- “हमरा चिन्है छिही?
pairs.e: If you have messed with me, then understand.

pairs.m: ने चिन्है छिही तऽ चीन्ह ले।
pairs.e: I am Gulo Mandal."

pairs.m: हम छिऐ गुलो मण्डल।
pairs.e: And Kedar Kanan is messing with Gulo Mandal.

pairs.m: अही चौक पर घर छै।
pairs.e: "Those who speak in big, high-sounding words are considered to be corrupting the language."

pairs.m: हमरा संगे ठेठपनी केलही तऽ बूझि ले।
pairs.e: – Kedar Kanan writes.

pairs.m: हम छिऐ गुलो मण्डल।
pairs.e: But Kedar Kanan does not have the courage to name those who use big, high-sounding words.

pairs.m: “ आ केदार कानन कऽ रहल छथि ठेठपनी गुलो मण्डलक सङ।
pairs.e: Kedar Kanan takes all benefits from the Sahitya Akademi and Maithili Akademi from those who use big, high-sounding words.

pairs.m: “बड़का-बड़का ठोप-चानन कयनिहार भाषा कें दूषित करब मानैत छथि।
pairs.e: And now when those big, high-sounding word users are on their deathbed, some have actually died, he wants to show himself separate from them.

pairs.m: “- केदार कानन लिखै छथि।
pairs.e: And if the children of the big, high-sounding word users call Kedar Kanan, Veena Thakur, Ashok Avichal, and others who receive lakhs of rupees worth translation/editing assignments and Sahitya Akademi's children/translation awards ungrateful, that is their internal matter.

pairs.m: मुदा ओइ ठोप-चानन कयनिहारक नाम लेबाक साहस केदार काननमे नै छन्हि।
pairs.e: But it is true that all these things came to them through the grace of the ancestors of the children of the big, high-sounding word users.

pairs.m: केदार कानन सभटा लाभ साहित्य अकादेमी आ मैथिली अकादेमीक ठोप-चानन कयनिहारसँ लैत छथि।
pairs.e: Also, if you do not use big, high-sounding words, or wear pants-shirts instead of dhoti-kurta, or are neo-Brahminical, then you automatically get the rights of all fraud, which is evident from Kedar Kanan's preface.

pairs.m: आ आब जखन ओ ठोप-चानन कयनिहार मरनासन्न छथि, किछु वास्तविक रूपमे मरियो गेल छथि, तँ ओ अपनाकेँ ओकरासँ अलग देखाबय चाहैत छथि।
pairs.e: Gulo was published in 2015 with a preface by Kedar Kanan Ji.

pairs.m: आ ठोप-चानन कयनिहारक बाल बच्चा सभ जँ केदार काननकेँ, वीणा ठाकुरकेँ, अशोक अविचलकेँ आ आन लाखक-लाख टाकाक अनुवाद/ सम्पादनक असाइनमेण्ट आ साहित्य अकादेमीक बाल/ अनुवाद पुरस्कार लेनिहार केँ कृतघ्न कहि रहल छन्हि तँ ओ हुनका लोकनिक आपसी मामिला छन्हि।
pairs.e: The 2019 Sahitya Akademi translation award was given to Kedar Kanan Ji for his Maithili translation of the Hindi poetry collection "Akal Mein Saras" (author – Kedarnath Singh).

pairs.m: मुदा ठोप-चानन कयनिहारक बाल बच्चाक पुरखाक कृपासँ हुनका सभकेँ ई सभ भेटलन्हि से तँ सत्य छैहे।
pairs.e: Now you first tell me, are there people who cannot read a Hindi poetry collection and need its Maithili translation?

pairs.m: संगहि जँ अहाँ ठोप-चानन नै कऽ रहल छी, बा धोती-कुर्ता नै वरन् पेण्ट-शर्ट पहिरै छी बा नव-ब्राह्मणवादी छी तँ अहाँकेँ सभ छल-प्रपञ्चक अधिकार स्वतः भेटि जाइत अछि, से केदार काननक आमुख सँ जनतब भेटैत अछि।
pairs.e: And in this, Kedar Kanan got a double benefit of translation assignment and award, didn't he?

pairs.m: गुलो २०१५ मे प्रकाशित भेल केदार कानन जी केर आमुखक संग।
pairs.e: Because in Ramta Jogi (2019), Kedar Kanan is absent; in Madar (2021), there is no preface.

pairs.m: २०१९क साहित्य अकादेमी अनुवाद पुरस्कार केदार कानन जी केँ हिन्दी कविता संग्रह "अकाल में सारस" (लेखक- केदार नाथ सिंह)क मैथिली अनुवादपर देल गेल।
pairs.e: But in Bhot (2022), Kedar Kanan has written 8 lines on the back cover – but there is not a single revolutionary line in it, why?

pairs.m: आब अहाँ पहिने हमरा बताउ जे के एहन लोक अछि जे हिन्दीक कविता संग्रह नै पढ़ि सकैए आ से ओकरा मैथिली अनुवादक खगता छै?
pairs.e: Now, what did Sahitya Akademi do with the novel "Gulo"?

pairs.m: आ ऐ मे केदार काननकेँ अनुवाद असाइनमेण्ट आ पुरस्कार, डबल फएदा भेलन्हि सएह ने।
pairs.e: Works published between 2016-2020 were considered for Sahitya Akademi awards in 2022.

pairs.m: कारण रमता जोगी (२०१९) मे केदार कानन निपत्ता छथि, मडर (२०२१) मे कोनो आमुख नै अछि।
pairs.e: Meaning Gulo was out of the race.

pairs.m: मुदा भोट (२०२२) मे केदार कानन बैक-कवरमे ८ पाँति लिखने छथि- मुदा एक्कोटा क्रान्तिकारी पाँति ओइमे नै अछि, किए?
pairs.e: Meaning, the one who wrote the revolutionary preface of Gulo got the Sahitya Akademi translation award, and Gulo was out of the race.

pairs.m: आब "गुलो" उपन्यासक सङ साहित्य अकादेमी की केलक।
pairs.e: So was Gulo's preface blackmailing?

pairs.m: २०१६-२०२० मे प्रकाशित रचनाकेँ साहित्य अकादेमी पुरस्कार २०२२ मे देल जयतै।
pairs.e: Understand it as such.

pairs.m: माने गुलो रेससँ बाहर।
pairs.e: There is much more cannon fodder in Gulo's preface.

pairs.m: माने गुलोक क्रान्तिकारी आमुख लिखनिहारकेँ साहित्य अकादेमी अनुवाद पुरस्कार आ गुलो रेससँ बाहर।
pairs.e: 'Desil bayna sab jan miththa' (Native speech is sweet to all), this is recited by the mainstream, and Kedar Kanan also recites it.

pairs.m: तँ गुलोक आमुख ब्लैकमेलिंग छल?
pairs.e: But Avahattha was a literary language.

pairs.m: बुझि तँ सएह पड़ैए।
pairs.e: But Jyotirishwar-pre Vidyapati is different from the Vidyapati of Sanskrit and Avahattha.

pairs.m: गुलोक आमुखमे आर बहुत रास कन्नारोहट अछि।
pairs.e: Possibly the son of Shri Mahesh Thakur of the barber caste from Bisfi village.

pairs.m: ’देसिल बयना सब जन मिट्ठा’, मूल धाराक सभकेँ ई रटल छै, से केदार काननकेँ सेहो रटल छन्हि।
pairs.e: In the parallel tradition of Bidapat Nach, dance-drama of Vidyapati Padavali (pre-Jyotirishwar) is performed. The description of pre-Jyotirishwar Vidyapati is found in the book "Ishvara Pratyabhijna-Vibharshini" written by Abhinavagupta of Kashmir (late 10th and early 11th century), where he mentions Vidyapati.

pairs.m: मुदा अवहट्ठ तँ साहित्यिक भाषा छल।
pairs.e: In Sridhar Das's Sadukti Karnamrit (composed 11 February 1206, see Madhyakaleen Mithila, Vijay Kumar Thakur) – Sridhar Das has quoted five verses of Vidyapati here, which are in the language of Vidyapati Padavali.

pairs.m: मुदा ज्योतिरीश्वर-पूर्व विद्यापति संस्कृत आ अवहट्ठक विद्यापति ठक्कुरः सँ भिन्न छथि।
pairs.e: "Jāva na mālato kara paragāsa tāve na tāhi madhukara vilāsa."

pairs.m: सम्भवतः बिस्फी गामक बार्बर कास्टक श्री महेश ठाकुरक पुत्र।
pairs.e: and "Mundala mukula kataya makaranda".

pairs.m: समानान्तर परम्पराक बिदापत नाचमे विद्यापति पदावलीक (ज्योतिरीश्वर पूर्वसँ) नृत्य-अभिनय होइत अछि।
pairs.e: Jyotirishwar (1275-1350) mentions Bidapat in Shashthah Kallol – "Atha Vidyavant Varnana" and Ashtamah Kallol – "Atha Rajya Varnana".

pairs.m: ज्योतिरीश्वर पूर्व विद्यापतिक विवरण- कश्मीरक अभिनव गुप्त द्वारा (दशम शताब्दीक अन्त आ एगारहम शताब्दीक प्रारम्भ) लिखल ग्रन्थ अछि "ईश्वर प्रत्याभिज्ञा- विभर्षिणी" जइमे ओ विद्यापतिक उल्लेख करै छथि।
pairs.e: So Vidyapati must have become so famous that Jyotirishwar mentioned him in the context of dance.

pairs.m: श्रीधर दासक सदुक्तिकर्णामृत- (रचना ११ फरबरी १२०६, देखू मध्यकालीन मिथिला, विजय कुमार ठाकुर)- श्रीधर दास विद्यापतिक पाँच टा पद एतऽ उद्धृत केने छथि जे विद्यापतिक पदावलीक भाषा छी।
pairs.e: (Picture of Vidyapati by Panakalal Mandal, awarded the Videha Chitrakala Samman).

pairs.m: "जाव न मालतो कर परगास तावे न ताहि मधुकर विलास।"
pairs.e: After that, Kedar Kanan writes the summary of the novel Gulo.

pairs.m: आ "मुन्दला मुकुल कतय मकरन्द"।
pairs.e: It seems he either did not read the novel properly or could not understand its meaning.
He writes – "Anwar understood that the old woman (Sukhba's mother) had money.

pairs.m: अथ विद्यावन्त वर्णना॥
pairs.e: His efforts were not futile.

pairs.m: मे बिदापत केर उल्लेख करैत छथि।
pairs.e: he would take it by force."

pairs.m: से विद्यापति ततेक प्रसिद्ध भऽ गेल रहथि जे ज्योतिरीश्वर तकर उल्लेख नाचक रूपमे केलन्हि।
pairs.e: and whatnot.

pairs.m: (विद्यापतिक चित्र: विदेह चित्रकला सम्मानसँ पुरस्कृत पनकलाल मण्डल द्वारा)।
pairs.e: But this is not in the novel; neither Anwar nor any other character does what Kedar Kanan suggests.

pairs.m: केदार कानन तकर बाद गुलो उपन्यासक सारांश लिखै छथि।
pairs.e: Those who are about to flee give money when everyone thinks they will not give.

pairs.m: लगैए ओ ठीकसँ उपन्यास पढ़लन्हि नै बा अर्थ बुझि नै सकला।
pairs.e: Those with wings replace the fan and give it when even Gulo suspects they will not replace.

pairs.m: ओ लिखै छथि- "अनवरकेँ बूझल छैक जे बुढ़िया (सुखबाक माय) लग पाइ छैक।
pairs.e: Anwar helped many times, later also warned that vultures are hovering for money.

pairs.m: ओकर कोनो प्रयास निरर्थक नै हेतैक।
pairs.e: Yes, sometimes when money was not available/delayed, he was definitely annoyed. Kedar Kanan is surprised by the language of Gulo; the mainstream should be too.

pairs.m: पलाइबला पाइ दैए जखन सभकेँ होइ छै जे ओ नै देत।
pairs.e: etc. Kedar Kanan's pretence of being surprised creates humour; we see this as the arrogance of the mainstream.

pairs.m: पंखाबला पंखा बदलि कऽ दऽ दैए जखन गुलोकेँ सेहो आशंका रहय जे ओ नै बदलत।
pairs.e: It seems he has come from another planet and lives in a big palace in Supaul, far from the common world.

pairs.m: अनवर तँ कतेक बेर मदति केलकै, बादमे सतर्को केलकै जे गिद्ध सभ पाइ लेल मड़रा रहल छै।
pairs.e: This 9-page preface disrupts the reading of Gulo.

pairs.m: हँ कताक बेर पाइ नै भेटलापर/ भेटैमे देरी भेलापर एक बेर ओ अनठेने जरूर रहै।
pairs.e: So I urge you to start reading Gulo directly from page 17.

pairs.m: गुलो केर भाषासँ केदार कानन अचम्भित छथि, मूल धारा केर लोककेँ हेबाको चाही।
pairs.e: Read the novel in one sitting.

pairs.m: मुदा से हुनकर मैथिली साहित्यसँ १५ सालसँ दूर हएब मात्र दर्शित करैत अछि।
pairs.e: Then come back and read this preface or cannon fodder as a humorous essay.

pairs.m: केदार काननकेँ आनो बहुत चीजपर आश्चर्य होइ छन्हि, उपन्यासक एकटा झगड़ाक बादक स्थितिपर ओ लिखै छथि जे "तथाकथित भद्र समाजमे एतबे टा घटना की सँ की कऽ दैत अछि मुदा सामान्य लोकक घरमे एहन घटना रहरहाँ…"।
pairs.e: In the Hindi translation of Gulo, Kedar Kanan's preface "The Story of the Last Destitute Person" is placed at the end of the novel, and for that, Gaurinath is a person to be thanked. □ Gulo (Original Maithili) In Gulo, only the grihastha (householder) and malik (master) are mentioned, who have a pen-orchard, but it is just mentioned, and once there is an address saying Bhabhana, meaning he is a Brahmin.

pairs.m: केदार काननक ऐ तरहक अचम्भित होयबाक स्वाङ करब हास्य उत्पन्न करैत अछि, मूलधाराक ऐंठीक रूपमे हम एकरा देखैत छी।
pairs.e: Only after Gulo's death does Doctor Malik come, who while crying says that now what will they call him, Malik?

pairs.m: लगैत अछि जे ओ कोनो दोसर ग्रहसँ आयल छथि आ सुपौलक एकटा बड़का महलमे रहै छथि, दीन-दुनियाँ सँ दूर।
pairs.e: Understand that he is a Kayastha, but in this novel, his caste is not described. So a grihastha/malik appears two or three times, and the doctor saheb once.

pairs.m: ९ पन्नाक ई आमुख गुलोक पाठमे व्यवधान उत्पन्न करैत अछि।
pairs.e: And the one who gets the most money is the owner of Palai Mill – Dashrath Mandal.

pairs.m: से अहाँसँ आग्रह जे पृ.
pairs.e: This novel is roughly divided into 14 sections, and with flashbacks, it presents the life of Gulo Mandal along with the description of his surroundings.

pairs.m: सं १७ सँ सोझे गुलो पढ़नाइ शुरू करू।
pairs.e: The purpose of the novel is not to give a detailed description of time and place, and therefore it does not analyze the inherent problems of society or the economy.

pairs.m: एक निसाँसमे उपन्यास पढ़ि जाउ।
pairs.e: It revolves around Gulo Mandal.

pairs.m: फेर घुरि कऽ ई आमुख बा कन्नारोहट हास्य आलेखक रूपमे पढ़ू।
pairs.e: Wherever Gulo Mandal's eyes, his virtual or real presence go, the novel wanders there.

pairs.m: गुलोक हिन्दी अनुवादमे केदार काननक आमुख “आखिरी विपन्न मनुक्खक गाथा” केर अनुवाद उपन्यासक अन्तमे देल गेल अछि आ तइ लेल गौरीनाथ धन्यवादक पात्र छथि।
pairs.e: If it goes here and there for a while, it returns.

pairs.m: गुलो (मूल मैथिली) गुलो मे गिरहत आ मालिकक चर्च मात्र भेल अछि, जिनकर कलम-गाछी छन्हि, मुदा बस चर्चे अछि, आ एक बेर बभना कहि कऽ सम्बोधन अछि, माने ओ ब्राह्मण थिका।
pairs.e: Even after his death, when people are angling for their share in the relief fund, the writer returns to Riniya, and she hears Gulo's call, listening for another call from her father.

pairs.m: मात्र गुलोक मरलाक बाद डाक्टर मलिक अबै छथि, जे कानैत कहैत छथि जे आब हुनका मालिक के कहतन्हि, मानि लिअ ओ कायस्थ छथि, मुदा ऐ उपन्यासमे हुनकर जातिक विवरण नै अछि।
pairs.e: "He is crying."

pairs.m: ई उपन्यास मोटा-मोटी १४ खण्डमे विभक्त अछि, आ फ्लैशबैकक संग गुलो मण्डलक जीवनक विवरण ओकर लगक वातावरणक विवरणक संग दैत अछि।
pairs.e: This book demands analysis in terms of textuality of words and sentences, distinct from ordinary novels.

pairs.m: उपन्यासक उद्देश्य काल-स्थानक विस्तारपूर्ण विवरण देबाक नै अछि, आ से समाज बा अर्थव्यवस्थाक अन्तर्निहित समस्याक विश्लेषण ई नै करैत अछि।
pairs.e: Julia Kristeva's longitudinal relation pole connecting writer and reader, and the vertical pole connecting this text with another text.

pairs.m: गुलो मण्डलक चारू कात ई घुमैत अछि।
pairs.e: Gulo is not divided into chapters; it flows like a stream, but through the use of typography in the print version, chapters or sections have been made possible for the convenience of the reader.

pairs.m: गुलो मण्डलक आँखि, गुलो मण्डलक आभासी बा वास्तविक उपस्थिति जतऽ जतऽ ओ जाइत अछि ततऽ ततऽ ई उपन्यास विचरैत अछि।
pairs.e: However, often within a section, by giving paragraph-spaces, sub-sections are also created. Now let's read Gulo once.

pairs.m: कने काल लेल एम्हर-ओम्हर गेबो कएल तँ फेर आपस।

pairs.m: ई पोथी सामान्य उपन्याससँ भिन्न शब्द आ वाक्यक पाठ्यता (टेक्स्टुएलिटी) क रूपमे विश्लेषण मंगैत अछि।

pairs.e: Flashback in one paragraph, Gulo's father Munilal, Gulo's two younger sisters, the death of his mother, leaving the railway job, Benarji Saheb, the head officer at Saharsa railway, did not like that Munilal left his job; he would have given him big, live fish?

pairs.e: Since Bela, his sister-in-law had come to see her sister, meaning Gulo's wife, and says – "This gajpiā (cannabis smoker) has ruined my sister."

pairs.m: रिनियाँक पेटमे चाली छै कारण ओ पचपच थूकै छै।

pairs.e: The process of sending invitations for the sister's marriage, a sari, saya, blouse, and 251 rupees had to be given.

pairs.m: ऊ सुइया दै छै।

pairs.e: The girl is suppressed, but if she refuses, will any prestige remain (will any deed remain, prestige is a small word – note for translator), "Even if I have to get beaten up.."

pairs.m: गुलोक ससुरारि छै बेला आ ओकर पत्नीक नाम छै बेलावाली।

The third son does mobile recharge, downloads films and songs.

pairs.e: Radha had given birth to a daughter just after the Intermediate exams ended. Radha started looking for a job.

pairs.m: कामेसर मुखिया लग गेल, मुदा मुखिया साफ नकारि गेलै कारण बहुत दिन भाए गेलै।
pairs.e: Now Madan had forgotten about beating and abusing.

pairs.m: मदन महावीर सऽ राधाक भेँटपर शंकालु होइ छै।
pairs.e: Mahavir got a job at that school.

pairs.m: कामेसर चरचित लैत रहै छै।
pairs.e: Rekha also came there; she was Radha's younger sister; her in-laws were also in Pathra.

pairs.m: की घटलइ, कोन दुख तकलीफ छै।
pairs.e: But the hunt for the honorarium's bribe made Madan guard Radha again.

pairs.m: बहनोइ राजकुमार इंटर कौलेज मे पढ़बइ छै।
pairs.e: There was a Raghunath Sahni.

pairs.m: दरमाहा नै भेटै छै।
pairs.e: He would say while roaming – 'No trust in women!

pairs.m: घर पहुँचल.
pairs.e: He made her quit the job.

pairs.m: राधा.
pairs.e: Madan went to his maternal uncle's for help, but got only 100 rupees.

pairs.m: मटिया तेल ढारि कए देह मे आगि लगाय लेने रहइ।
pairs.e: Radha started regretting more.

pairs.m: कामेसर जे राधा के सेवा केलकइ.
pairs.e: If she could get a job again, Mahavir said he would need to meet the headmaster and the Mukhiya; the headmaster said he would need to meet the Mukhiya.

pairs.m: दुनू मे जरूर कोनो लटपट छै।
pairs.e: Madan suspects Radha's meetings with Mahavir.

pairs.m: मदन बहीन लग जाइत रहय तए राधा भनभनाइत रहय- ससुरो-भैंसुर कए नै छोड़लक।
pairs.e: Kamesar keeps inquiring.

pairs.m: आगि लगा लेब… .
pairs.e: Madan asks.

pairs.m: कामेसर छौंड़ा कए लाए जाइ छै।
pairs.e: Madan went to his sister's.

pairs.m: राधा लेल टॉनिक कीनै छै।
pairs.e: But tuition classes are very popular.

pairs.m: ओकर बाप ओइ दुनू लए एतेक चिन्ता.
pairs.e: The brother-in-law asked – 'Why did you make the girl quit her job?

pairs.m: राधा कहइ छै- पिपराही वाली दीदी सए चाउर लाए ने आबौ।
pairs.e: You people are suspicious.

pairs.m: दीदी पाँच किलो चाउर देलकै.
pairs.e: ' Madan left.

pairs.m: अपन चाउर राख.
pairs.e: Radha had poured kerosene and set herself on fire.

pairs.m: ऊ ममाक जेठका सार लग गेल जेठका के कनियाँ डरि गेलै।
pairs.e: Kamesar takes the girl.

pairs.m: पड़ल-पड़ल कखनू आँखि लागि गेलइ।
pairs.e: buys tonic for Radha.

pairs.m: राधा फेर आयल छै।
pairs.e: Her father worries so much about these two things.

pairs.m: निन्न टूटि गेलइ।
pairs.e: Madan kept thinking.

pairs.m: राधा कतौ नइँ छै।
pairs.e: His suspicion was getting confirmed. Radha says – 'The Didi from Piprahi hasn't come to give rice yet.

pairs.m: लेकिन ओकरा बुझाइ छइ राधा सामने ठाढ़ छै।
pairs.e: ' The Didi gave five kilos of rice.

pairs.m: करिहो पहुँचै छथि।
pairs.e: Madan got angry.

pairs.m: राजद नेता राजिनदर कहै छन्हि जे सबहक ऐठाम एक-एक कप चाह पीबि लेता तहीमे बेड़ा पार।
pairs.e: Keep your rice.

pairs.m: करिहो पहिने सुपौलमे पड़ैत रहइ।
pairs.e: This woman is the only one.

pairs.m: अइ बेर काइट कए पिपरा विधान सभामे दाए देलकइए, विजेनदर यादव एतऽ सऽ जितैत रहइ, तैं एतौका बेसी भोटर जदयू के छै।
pairs.e: She called an old husband to sleep with.

pairs.m: जहिया कहियो अनुदान आबै छै तए थोड़े आमदनी भाए जाइ छै।
pairs.e: seeing the medicine in her father's hand, Shanichari got scared – 'Don't kill Mother, Papa.

pairs.m: हरदी के परोग्राम बनत तऽ प्रोटैगोनिस्ट कामता (जदयू) संगे निकलि जेता, मुदा ने दीना पाठक (जदयू)केँ किछु पता छै ने एकटा आन कांगरेसीये केँ।
pairs.e: Radha's throat was slit.

pairs.m: प्रोटैगोनिस्ट कामताकेँ कहै छथि जे ऐ तरहेँ सोचलासँ राजद, जदयू आ कांगरेसक गठबन्धन कमजोर भऽ जेतै।
pairs.e: Madan left.

pairs.m: कामता हरदी जाइले तैयार भऽ जाइए।
pairs.e: Madan's maternal uncle's in-laws are in Rampur.

pairs.m: फेर हरदासँ करिहो।
pairs.e: The middle sister's husband there is the chief of criminals.

pairs.m: एलेक्शनक परचारक प्रोटैगोनिस्टक ई पहिल अनुभव छन्हि।
pairs.e: He went to the elder sister's husband; the elder sister's daughter got scared. The middle one was there.

pairs.m: यदुवंश कहै छन्हि जे पैसा नइ अय।
pairs.e: He said – 'We will keep him.

pairs.m: प्रोटैगोनिस्टकेँ ई चिन्तित करै छन्हि।
pairs.e: He fell asleep sometime.

pairs.m: सोचै छथि जे करिहोक खर्चाक भार उठा लेथि, मुदा फेर होइ छन्हि जे नै जानि कत्ते खर्च हेतइ।
pairs.e: ' Radha came again.

pairs.m: प्रोटैगोनिस्ट आ कामता सुपौलमे पढ़ैत रहथि आ यदुवंश रफीगंजमे, तकर एकटा खिस्सा प्रोटैगोनिस्ट सुनबै छथि, कामताक साइकिलसँ प्रोटैगोनिस्ट आ यदुवंश एकडारा गेलथि ओत्तऽ साइकिल चोरि भऽ गेलै, कामता यदुवंशकेँ मोन पाड़ैत अछि, यदुवंश आब ओकरे क्षेत्रक एमेले बनतै, एक दोसर के बेगरता पड़तै।
pairs.e: Wearing a white sheet.

pairs.m: मुदा पाटी लाए कए दूरी छै, शीर्ष नेता सभ समझौता कऽ लेलकै मुदा एतेक दिनक कटुता तुरन्ते भाफ जकाँ केना उड़ि जेतै?
pairs.e: A stake wrapped around her neck.

pairs.m: बिन्दुलाल दास मखौलिया अय।
pairs.e: He woke up. Radha is nowhere.

pairs.m: खिस्सा सुनबै छै, चाह दोकानबलाक नाम बीडियो छै, घूस जे लेलकै, से ससपेन भाए गेलै।
pairs.e: But he feels Radha is standing in front of him.

pairs.m: तहिया सए चाह बेचइ छै।
pairs.e: □ Bhot (Vote) In Bhot, a call comes to the writer/protagonist from his nephew (it is revealed later that he is the protagonist's nephew) Yaduvansh, who came to a village assembly once a long time ago (this is also mentioned later), whom people call Vidhayakji.

pairs.m: सभ भभारो हँसलै, बीडियो मिडिलो पास नइ-ए।
pairs.e: The protagonist Subhash Ji is counted among the good people, so he seeks his help. He reaches Kariho.

pairs.m: बीडियोसँ प्रोटैगोनिस्ट पूछै छथि, ओ ककरा भोट देत, मुदा ओ टेढ़ जबाब दइ छन्हि- ककरा देबै आ ककरा नै देबै, से तोरा कहि कए!
pairs.e: Rajindar, a leader of RJD, says that if everyone has a cup of tea here, the boat will cross.

pairs.m: बीजेपी के कंडीडेट विश्वमोहन छिऐ, एक बेर एमपी भएल रहइ, दोसर बेरा हारि गेलै, एतुक्क एमेले ओकर कनियाँ सुजाता छिऐ।
pairs.e: In the assembly election, there is an alliance of RJD, JDU, and Congress.

pairs.m: विश्वमोहन कीयट छिऐ।
pairs.e: Yaduvansh Kumar Yadav is a candidate from the Pipra Legislative Assembly, which fell to RJD. Kariho used to be in Supaul earlier.

pairs.m: एक बेर दिलेसर कामत जीतल रहै, ओहो कीयटे रहइ।
pairs.e: This time, through a trick, they fielded him in the Pipra Assembly; Vijender Yadav used to win from here, so here there are more voters from JDU. Rajindar Yadav ran for Mukhiya from here.

pairs.m: ओना सोशलिस्ट पाटी के नेता विनायक यादवक जमाइ दीनबन्धु यादव सेहो एक बेर जीतल छै।
pairs.e: He couldn't win, but he's a capable man; he has a name in a non-government college; he goes to college for two or four days a month, marks his attendance, and leaves.

pairs.m: पिपरामे कीयट के जनसंख्या बेशी छै।
pairs.e: Whenever there is a grant, he gets some income.

pairs.m: दोसर नम्बरपर धानुक आ तेसर नम्बरपर जादव छै।
pairs.e: He goes around acting like a big man.

pairs.m: धानुक जातिके अपन कोनो कंडीडेट नै छै, से ओकर भोट बँटतै, चौधरी भेलै बनियाँ आ बीजेपी भेलै बनियां पाटी, ऊ बीजेपी छोड़ि कतय जेतै?
pairs.e: The protagonist also goes around with him.

pairs.m: हँ पाइ पर कियो ढूइल जाइ तँ.
pairs.e: When a programme is made in Hardi, the protagonist would go with Kamta (JDU), but neither Dina Pathak (JDU) nor any Congressman knows anything.

pairs.m: समाजवादी पाटी के कंडीडेट सत्यनारायण गुप्ता, ऊ तेली छिऐ, तेली के बेसी भोट ओकरे जेतै।
pairs.e: Kamta agrees to go to Hardi.

pairs.m: वामपंथी के उमेदवार नागेसर यादवक कोनो चरचा नै छै।
pairs.e: Then from Harda to Kariho.

pairs.m: मुसलमान बीजेपी सए भड़कै छै, ऊ सब छेहा गठबंधन के भोटर छिऐ।
pairs.e: This is the protagonist's first experience of election campaigning.

pairs.m: तहिना बाभन गठबंधन सए भड़कै छै।
pairs.e: Yaduvansh says there is no money.

pairs.m: सुखदेव बीजेपी मे छै, ओना ओकर उमेदवार सए जनता नराज छै।
pairs.e: This worries the protagonist.

pairs.m: शिवजीक चाहक दोकान छै, नै नीक चाह बनबै छै, निशांमे रहै छै, परधानमंतरी तक के गारि दै छै, प्रोटैगोनिस्टकेँ कहै छन्हि खरचा करै ले- तोरे कोन कमी छह!
pairs.e: How to fight an election without money?

pairs.m: ओकर बेटी लछमी जीन्स आ टॉप पिन्हइ छै।
pairs.e: How much could the protagonist donate, 5-10 thousand?

pairs.m: लिट्टी चाह बनबइ आ गैंहकी सम्हारइमे ओस्ताज भाए गेल छै।
pairs.e: Better to spend that much on campaigning; what expense is there in tea and water?

pairs.m: प्रोटैगोनिस्टकेँ शिवजी लछमीसँ पढ़ाइक टेस्ट करबैत अछि, प्रोटैगोनिस्ट पूछै छथिन्ह पुष्प माने की?
pairs.e: He thinks he will bear the campaign expenses, but then he thinks, who knows how much it will cost.

pairs.m: उत्तर- फूल।
pairs.e: The protagonist and Kamta studied in Supaul, and Yaduvansh in Rafiganj; the protagonist tells a story about this: the protagonist and Yaduvansh had gone together on Kamta's bicycle; the bicycle got stolen; Kamta blames Yaduvansh; now Yaduvansh is becoming an MLA from that area; they need each other.

pairs.m: शिवजी, बेटी पढ़ैमे तेज छौ!
pairs.e: But party differences create distance; the top leaders have compromised, but how can years of bitterness evaporate like steam?

pairs.m: प्रोटैगोनिस्ट कंडीडेट संगे अपन सम्बन्ध सभकेँ बता देनहि छथि, ता बात पसरइ छै, दस दिनुका बाद फेर एता।
pairs.e: Bindu Lal Das is a joker.

pairs.m: घरनी कहै छथिन्ह- घुचघुच जाय कए की हेतै।
pairs.e: He tells a story: the tea shop owner's name is Bidiya; the bribe he took turned into a cooking pot.

pairs.m: अहाँ के कहने लोग भोट दाए देतै?
pairs.e: Then he started selling tea.

pairs.m: दाइ सकइए।
pairs.e: Everyone laughed heartily; Bidiya hasn't passed middle school.

pairs.m: सौ-पचास भोट के फरक पड़तै।
pairs.e: The protagonist asks Bidiya who he will vote for, but he gives a crooked answer – 'Who I will give and who I won't, why should I tell you?

pairs.m: तए फैदा हेतै यदुवंश कए।
pairs.e: ' BJP's candidate is Vishwamohan; he was an MP once, lost the second time, this time his daughter Sujata is the candidate.

pairs.m: अहाँ कए यदुवंश की देत?
pairs.e: Vishwamohan is of the Kiyat caste.

pairs.m: अपन काम करतै तए दू गो पैसो हेतै।
pairs.e: In Pipra, the population of Kiyat is high.

pairs.m: परचार केने सए पेट भरतै?
pairs.e: Next are Dhanuk, then Jadav.

pairs.m: सब कुइछ पैसे नै ने छिऐ।
pairs.e: The Dhanuk community has no candidate of their own, so their vote is divided; if Choudhary is a Baniya, and BJP is a Baniya party, where would they go?

pairs.m: नै छिऐ तए जाउ।
pairs.e: Yes, if someone is tempted with money.

pairs.m: आइ हम नै काम देबै तए काइल्ह ऊ हमरा काम देतै?
pairs.e: There's no talk of the Left's candidate, Nagesar Yadav.

pairs.m: जे मन होइ-ए सएह करू- घरनी आजिज भेल कहइए।
pairs.e: Muslims are angry with BJP; they will vote for the Grand Alliance.

pairs.m: यदुवंश नामांकन दाखिल केलक।
pairs.e: Similarly, Brahmins are angry with the Grand Alliance.

pairs.m: प्रभास प्रभारी छिऐ।
pairs.e: Sukhdev is in BJP; actually, people are unhappy with his candidate.

pairs.m: चुनाव लगिचा गेलै, प्रोटैगोनिस्ट करिहो पहुँचै छथि।
pairs.e: Shivji has a tea shop; he doesn't make good tea; he stays intoxicated; he abuses even the Prime Minister; he tells the protagonist to spend – 'What do you lack?

pairs.m: कामता मंत्रीजी के परचार करय गेल अय, ओ ओकर पाटी जदयू के उमेदवार छिऐ, क्षेत्र बदैल गेलै, लेकिन पाटी तए नै बदललै।
pairs.e: ' His daughter Laxmi wears jeans and a top. She has become an expert at making litti tea and harvesting wheat.

pairs.m: बिन्दू मास्टरी करइए।
pairs.e: Shivji tests the protagonist with Laxmi's education; the protagonist asks what 'pushpa' means.

pairs.m: पराइविट।
pairs.e: Answer – Flower.

pairs.m: पचास टके मेहीना।
pairs.e: His wife says – 'Wandering around here and there, what's the point?

pairs.m: कोय दइ छइ, कोय नइ दइ छइ।
pairs.e: The difference of a hundred or fifty votes matters.

pairs.m: जैह भेटल, सैह लाए लेलक।
pairs.e: If you do your work, you might get some money.

pairs.m: चाउरे देबें, दूधे देबें, जरने देबें.
pairs.e: Does campaigning fill the stomach?
If there's no money, then don't go.

pairs.m: एक बेर ऊ पंचायत समिति के सदस्य भेल।
pairs.e: Just sit here.

pairs.m: इनरा आवास तए ककरो बिरधा पिलसिन।
pairs.e: Today if I don't give you work, will he give me work tomorrow?

pairs.m: काम भए गेला पर.
pairs.e: Do whatever you want – the wife, exasperated, says.

pairs.m: कोय दस, कोय बीस।
pairs.e: Prabhas is the in-charge.

pairs.m: लेकिन अगिला चुनाव जातीय गोलबन्दी के ओजह सए हाइर गेल।
pairs.e: Bindu is doing mastership. Private.

pairs.m: खाली खरचा-पाइन के इंतजाम केने जाहक.
pairs.e: fifty rupees a month.

pairs.m: करिहोमे मुसहर के दूटा घनगर टोल छै।
pairs.e: Whatever he finds, he takes.

pairs.m: करिहो के दुनू टोल आ बगहीओ के मुसहरी धांगलिऐ।
pairs.e: After the work is done.

pairs.m: साँझ पैड़ गेलै।
pairs.e: some give ten, some twenty.

pairs.m: चंदेसरी चाह-पान के पैसा तए हमरा सए लेबे केलक; मुसहरी के एगो नेता खातिर सौ टका और लेलक।
pairs.e: But in the next election, due to caste polarisation, he lost.

pairs.m: नीतीश कुमार भोटर के नाम जे अपील केने छै से, सात निश्चय बला घोषणा-पत्र, बैलेट पेपर के नमूना आ कलेंडर लाए कए निकलै छी।
pairs.e: does pāikāri (brokering) of goods and supplies.

pairs.m: महिला कए ठीक सए बुझाबहक।
pairs.e: Now it's a free-for-all for self-interest.

pairs.m: कहिहक नीतीश आ लालू मिल गेलै।
pairs.e: Today, everyone's target is the Mushari and Domrahi settlements.

pairs.m: दुनू एक भाए गेलै।
pairs.e: In Kariho, there are two dense colonies of Musahars.

pairs.m: पुछलक- नीतीश मुखमंतरी बनतै ने?
pairs.e: Go to every voter's door.

pairs.m: सफीना आयल हइ!
pairs.e: He also came to me.

pairs.m: - जमील सुनबइए।
pairs.e: He also asked me.

pairs.m: बबा कए गोली लाबि दइ छिऐ।
pairs.e: Smoke and fumes, deceit, and Gulo had to settle on government land.

pairs.m: ' भगिनाक बियाहमे गुलो सोनक जाइए।
pairs.e: The greatest sorrow of Gulo's death was felt by Riniya; the writer keeps Riniya's presence at both the beginning and the end.

pairs.m: गुलोक ओतऽ चलती छै ओतऽ ओकर बॉडी लैंगुएज बदलल छै।
pairs.e: Gulo's eldest son's migration to Punjab for employment.

pairs.m: कनियाँ दब छै मुदा ओ डाँटि-डपटि दैए, बियाह हेतै, नै तऽ गज्जन होइतै।
pairs.e: The entire market is Rajindar Dealer's co-brother Inra Ganhi and his four sons.

pairs.m: कोन कोन रोजगार छै से देखाबैले उपन्यासकार पुतोहु कंदाहावालीकेँ मलहदक चाँपमे मखानक कमौनीक नव-रोजगार भेटलइ-ए, ओतऽ लऽ जाइ छथि।
pairs.e: Then step outside the market: Bela, Bina, and Sonak.

pairs.m: छोटुआ काज करैए दशरथ मण्डलक पिलाइ मिलमे।
pairs.e: And then Gulo receives two betel nuts and two invitations – one for the wedding of Lalo Pandit's granddaughter, the other for the wedding of Sari's daughter-in-law.

pairs.m: मूंगक बीया बाउग हेतै, हरवला चास, समार आ चौकी दइ के अढ़ाइ सय मांगै छै।
pairs.e: Gulo's in-laws are in Bela, and his wife's name is Belawali.

pairs.m: फेर गुलोक मृत्युक प्रारम्भ एना करै छथि गुलोकेँ खोंखी होइ छै, दू सय टाकामे दू कट्ठा थोकड़ा ओलइ के बात भेलै।
pairs.e: Earlier, Gulo had collapsed while smoking gājā.

pairs.m: पारिवारिक सम्बन्ध, वृद्धावस्थाक समस्याक विवरण देखैले सुखबा लग जाउ, ओ अपन माय कए घर सए निकालि देलक, फेर बदनामी भेलापर आपस लऽ गेल।
pairs.e: Meaning, the trade of the intoxicant gājā used to happen all around.

pairs.m: ओ छै इनरा गानहीक पितिया सासु।
pairs.e: The process of sending invitations for the sister's marriage, a sari, saya, blouse, and 251 rupees had to be given.

pairs.m: स्थानीय संस्कृति देखबैले कालीबन्दीक सेवा, भगैत, फुलहासि।
pairs.e: He goes to Sonak, with Chhotua and Riniya.

pairs.m: आब तकरा बाद कुशक कलेपो नै लगतै।
pairs.e: Gulo's eldest daughter Runiya is married without ceremony.

pairs.m: गुलो मालिकसँ टौर्च मांगत, ओ नै देतै तऽ एक्को गो आम कि लुच्ची भोग नै हुअय देत।
pairs.e: Runiya also comes directly from her husband's place, wants to take her sister Riniya to her in-laws.

pairs.m: आ एतबी नै आर देखू- बाँस डेढ़ सय सए कम मे नै भेटै छै, गुलो चोरा कए बेचत तैं एक्के सय मे दिअय पड़तै।
pairs.e: But she refuses; people say that since the father is sick, days are difficult, so she has come to earn a living.

pairs.m: आ चोर सेहो छै।
pairs.e: She is very sharp; Gulo is surprised, how did this girl understand all this.

pairs.m: चोरबा सौंसे गाछक लुच्ची सिसोहि लेलकै।
pairs.e: And in the end, Riniya learns that Gulo is very upset.

pairs.m: एकटा ठकुरबा सेनियल चोर छै।
pairs.e: She returns in the morning.

pairs.m: से गुलोक बाँसक चोरि आ ठकुरबाक चोरिमे अन्तर छै।
pairs.e: Mother asked if she had performed the Ratuka worship. Riniya's mind was not in hearing about the events.

pairs.m: गुलोक मुख्य धंधा छै चाहक दोकान, बाँसक चोरि तँ सालमे एकाध बेर मुदा ठकुरबा सेनियल चोर छै।
pairs.e: The mother said – 'Mother, I don't have money.

pairs.e: To show what kinds of employment exist, the novelist takes his daughter-in-law Kandahawali to Malhad, where she finds a new employment making makhana.

pairs.m: गाछीक मालिक कियो आन छै।
pairs.e: Chhotua works as a labourer for Dashrath Mandal in the mill.

pairs.m: आदिवासी इलाकामे ओ सरकारक रहै छै आ फॉरेस्ट एक्टमे आदिवासीकेँ ओतऽ जाड़नि, महुआ, वन्य-उत्पाद बिछबाक अधिकार देल गेल छै।
pairs.e: Sowing mung beans, hiring a plough, he asks for two and a half hundred for providing plough, support, and chauki.

pairs.m: मुदा सिपौलमे गाछी सरकारक नै छिऐ।
pairs.e: Then the beginning of Gulo's death is described like this: Gulo has a cough; the deal to buy two katha of thokda (land) for two hundred rupees was made.

pairs.m: से एतुक्का सर्वहाराक स्थिति आरो खराप, फॉरेस्ट एक्ट एतऽ लागू नै अछि।
pairs.e: To see family relationships, problems of old age, go to Sukhba; he had thrown his mother out, then brought her back due to bad reputation.

pairs.m: से एक बेर रिनियाँ गछपक्कू आम लाए कए गेल रहै तऽ मालिक पचास गो टाका देने रहै।
pairs.e: She is Inra Ganhi's father's mother-in-law.

pairs.m: मलिकानि खाइ लए देने रहै।
pairs.e: To see local culture, worship of Kalibandi, bhagait, phulhasi.

pairs.m: रुनियाँ के दीयर नेरहू एलै, ओ चुपचाप रिनियां के देखैत रहइ-ए आ मुस्की छोड़इ-ए।
pairs.e: Now after that, they don't even apply kush ka lep. Gulo asks the landlord for a torch; if he doesn't give, then neither a single mango nor a luchchi will be offered for enjoyment.

pairs.m: दशरथ मण्डलक साढ़ूक लड़का नेपालमे छै, रिनियां लेल कथा अबै छै।
pairs.e: And look no further – Bamboo is not available for less than one and a half hundred; if Gulo sells stolen ones, he would have to give them for one hundred. And there are thieves too.

pairs.m: मुदा गुलो चारि मासक टेम मंगैए, अरजुनमा नालायक छै मुदा तैयो ओकरासँ पुछतै।
pairs.e: He stole luchchi and sisohi from the thief's mango tree.

pairs.m: दशरथ मण्डल पाइबला छै, मुदा एक्के बेरमे गुलो हँ नै कहलकै, अरजुनमासँ पुछतै।
pairs.e: There is a Thakurba who is a serial thief.

pairs.m: माने संस्था वर्तमान छै।
pairs.e: So there is a difference between Gulo's theft of bamboo and the Thakurba's theft.

pairs.m: से रिनियां तऽ लग छैहे गुलोक, पुतोहु जे झगड़ा कऽ कय गेल रहै सेहो एकदम्मे दूर नै छै, देखू एतऽ- .
pairs.e: Gulo's main business is a tea shop; stealing bamboo is once or twice a year, but the Thakurba is a serial thief.

pairs.m: कंदाहावाली पहुँच गेलइ।
pairs.e: 'Would that Bhabhana catch me?

pairs.m: 'पपा, कल वला मिसतरी कए बजा आनथिन।
pairs.e: ' (Barseserwali).

pairs.m: ठीक काए देतै।

pairs.m: जे लागतै से देबै।’
pairs.e: The orchard owner is someone else.

pairs.m: लेखक अहाँकेँदूर आ लग सभ ठाम लऽ जाइ छथि।
pairs.e: In tribal areas, it belongs to the government, and the Forest Act gives tribals the right to collect mahua, forest produce, etc. But in Sipaul, the orchard is not government-owned. So the condition of the proletariat here is worse; the Forest Act does not apply here.

pairs.m: केन्द्र कखनो गुलो रहैए, कखनो रिनियां आ कखनो कन्दाहावाली।
pairs.e: So once Riniya had brought Gachhpakku mangoes, and the landlord gave fifty rupees.

pairs.m: रमता जोगीमे सुभाष चन्द्र यादव किछु एहेन चीजक अनुभव करै छथि जे बहुत लोक आनो ठाम करैत अछि (भारतकेँ छोड़ि कऽ), जेना जेब्रा क्रॉसिगपर मनुक्खकेँ जाइत देखि गाड़ी सभक रुकि जायब, जेना सिंगापुरमे।
pairs.e: The landlord's wife had them and ate.

pairs.m: यूरोपमे जँ अहाँ जाइ तँ सड़कक तिराहाबला कॉर्नरपर अहाँकेँ देखि दोसर गाड़ी सभ ठाढ़ भऽ जायत, अपन ऐठाम लोक अपन गाड़ी आगाँ बढ़ा लैए, ऐ आशामे जे दोसर पाछाँ आबैबला सदाशयता देखाओत।
pairs.e: Runiya's husband's brother Nerhu came; he kept silently looking at Riniya and smiling.

pairs.m: लेखक सिगरेट बहुत पीबै छथि, से हुनका ईहो बुझना गेलन्हि जे ओतय निअम छै जे पूरा बा अल्प घेराबाबला जगहपर अहाँ सिगरेट नाइ पीबि सकै छी, मुदा सार्वजनिक स्थलपर सिगरेट नै पीब आब भारतोमे स्वीकृत भऽ गेल छै, पहिने लोक ऑफिसमे सिगरेटक धुँआक छल्ला बनबैत छल आ आब जँ बाथरूमोमे नुकाकेँ पीबऽ चाहैए तँ लोक आँखि गुराड़ि कऽ देखै छै।
pairs.e: Dashrath Mandal's brother-in-law's son is in Nepal; there is talk for Riniya.

pairs.m: घरदेखिया कथा संग्रहक प्रोटैगोनिस्ट सेहो खूब सिगरेट पीबैए, से सिगरेट पीबाक हिस्सक लेखकमे पुरान बुझाइत छन्हि, आ तेँ पुतोहु मधुलिका सेहो ऐ लऽ कऽ चिन्तित होइत छथिन्ह।
pairs.e: But Gulo asks for a four-month time; Arjunma is useless, but still he asks him.

pairs.m: फेर सुपौलसँ फोन अबिते रहैत छन्हि, डी.
pairs.e: Dashrath Mandal has money, but Gulo did not agree immediately; he asks Arjunma.

pairs.m: एम.
pairs.e: Meaning the institution still exists.

pairs.m: गप करै छन्हि, खाली केना छथि से पुछै छन्हि तखने जा कऽ निश्चिन्त होइ छथि।
pairs.e: So Riniya is definitely close to Gulo; the daughter-in-law who had left after quarreling is also not completely distant; look here – .

pairs.m: कोनो डाक्टर ओकरा बताहि नै कहतैक… मुदा ओ बताहि अछि।
pairs.e: Kandahawali arrived. – 'Papa, the mason from yesterday will come tomorrow.

pairs.m: मडर (२०२१) मे ई पद्धति विस्तार पेलक।
pairs.e: He will fix it.

pairs.m: रघुनाथ निछच्छ बताह रहइ।
pairs.e: In Ramta Jogi, Subhash Chandra Yadav experiences some things that many people do elsewhere (leaving India), like vehicles stopping to let people cross a zebra crossing, like in Singapore.

pairs.m: मदन बताह नै छै।
pairs.e: If you go to Europe, at a corner with a side road, other vehicles stop when they see you; people here push their vehicle forward, hoping the other person will show kindness coming from behind.

pairs.m: … आ स्त्री-विमर्श, मदनक कनियाँ राधाक फर्स्ट डिविजनमे पास हएब मुदा चाहियो कऽ अपन शरीरपर अधिकार जतबैत मदनकेँ रोकि नै सकब, घुरब, नोकरी छोड़ब, मुदा ओकर हत्या।
pairs.e: The writer smokes a lot; he also realized that there is a rule there that you cannot smoke in a full or partial enclosed space, but not smoking in public places has also become accepted in India now; earlier people used to make smoke rings in offices, and now if you want to smoke hidden in the bathroom, people frown.

pairs.m: आ ओइ हत्याक प्रयासकेँ रोकबा लेल लेखक शनीचरीकेँ आगाँ करैत छथि- “माय कए नै मारहक हौ पप्पा।
pairs.e: The protagonist of the Ghardekhiya story collection also smokes a lot; it seems the writer has a long history of smoking, and so his daughter-in-law Madhulika is also worried about this.

pairs.m: “ लेखक मदनकेँ मुदा बेनीफट ऑफ डाउट नै दै छथि, ओ घोषित कऽ दै छथि जे मदन बताह नै छै।
pairs.e: Then calls kept coming from Supaul; D.
